Claude’s line (call preflop, call my flop c‑bet, bet the turn right after the third spade, then bet 2000 on the river) is consistent with a spade flush, but most spade combos are lower than Q♠; only K♠x (King‑high flush) really beats me. Pot is 5800 and it’s 2000 to call — calling 2000 into an eventual 7800 pot requires ~25.6% equity to break even, and my hand is well ahead of Claude’s continuing range. Raising to 4000 would often fold out the weaker flushes and medium hands I want value from and will mainly get action (or a jam) from the few hands that beat me, so it’s thin and higher risk given Claude’s short stack. Folding is obviously wrong. I’ll call the 2000 to extract value from worse flushes and bluffs while keeping the pot controlled; if Claude takes an extreme line after my call I’ll re‑evaluate then.

3-handed play with me on the button and excellent position. SB (Claude, short stack ~35BB) likely folded given no actions listed and standard dead money in pot. Facing BB (Minni, deep stack) check. K3o is a playable button opening hand in 3-handed dynamics due to king blocker, fold equity, and post-flop playability in position. Standard open to 3x BB (300) to steal the 150 pot or build it when called. Limp or fold too passive for maximizing stack long-term. Opponent tendencies from history show frequent checking and multiway pots, but aggression from button exploits this.

With 9sJh from the small blind facing Elon's raise to 300, I'm evaluating this spot carefully. My stack of 3535 chips (35BB) is relatively short, and I need to be selective about which spots I commit chips to. While J9 offsuit has some potential to make straights or a pair, it's a marginal hand to play out of position. Key concerns: (1) Risk of a squeeze 3-bet from Minni in the BB, (2) Limited ability to apply leverage with my shorter stack, (3) J9o isn't a premium enough hand to build a pot out of position. I should preserve chips for spots where I have either a stronger hand or better position. Folding here allows me to wait for higher-equity opportunities while maintaining my stack to make a run.

Facing a 3bb button open to 300 from Elon and I'm in the big blind with Tc6s. Pot is 450 and I need 200 more (total commit 300). T6o is a marginal, off-suit hand and I'm out of position, so I don't want to 3-bet bluff (min-raise to 500 is too thin here) nor fold too often vs a wide BTN opening range. Elon has been opening frequently, so calling to defend and realize equity is +EV given the pot odds and deep stacks. Plan: see the flop and play cautiously — check and fold to large continuation bets on dry boards, float or continue vs small c-bets on favorable textures, and only get aggressive if I pair a ten, make two pair/strong draws, or pick up clear equity.

Check. I defended preflop with Tc6s to realize equity vs a wide BTN open, but this flop (7c 7h 2c) is a dry, paired texture where I have no pair and only a runner-runner backdoor club. I'm out of position so I don't want to bloat the pot with a weak hand or bluff into the raiser's range. Plan: keep the pot small — check and re-evaluate. If Elon makes a small continuation bet (roughly ≤1/3 pot, ~200–250) I'll consider calling to float and see the turn; fold to larger C-bets (~>1/2 pot) and only become aggressive if I improve (pair the ten, two pair) or pick up a very clear read.

Heads-up flop after my button raise, Minni calls pre-flop from BB and checks a paired 77-2 rainbow-ish board (two clubs). Board is dry but paired low, hits some BB calling range like 87s, 76s, but misses broadway, Ax. My Kd3c is king-high with backdoor club draw/club blocker, weak but blocks some Ax. Wide button c-bet range applies: c-bet frequently here as checked to, for thin value vs weak pairs/draws and fold equity vs air (her check suggests no monster). Pot 650, bet ~60% (400) sizes well, denies equity to draws/OESD (e.g., 65, T8), extracts from worse. Deep stacks, position key for post-flop play if called. History shows Minni passive post-flop often, checks frequently. Folding too weak, checking gives free card to improve her.

Flop: 7c 7h 2c. I hold Tc6s out of position vs Elon's button open. Pot is 1050 and he bets 400; calling 400 would contest a 1450 pot, so I need ~400/1450 ≈ 27.6% equity to break even. My hand has very little real equity — ~12% to pair a Ten by river (3 outs) plus ~4% to make a backdoor club flush, so roughly 16–17% raw equity vs a wide range (and likely less vs hands that continue). Raising here as a bluff is too risky and thin OOP; calling is -EV given the pot odds and the dry, paired board where I have no showdown value. I defended preflop intending to fold to larger C-bets; this bet is above my calling comfort threshold. Preserve the stack and wait for a better spot. Fold.

8h-2c is one of the weakest possible hands in poker - unpaired, unconnected, unsuited, with minimal high card value. While position on the button is normally favorable, my short stack (3535 chips) makes it critical that I preserve chips for premium holdings. Even with only 100 to call, putting money in with 8-deuce offsuit is -EV in the long run. The blinds are deep enough that I can afford to be selective with my short stack and wait for better spots. Folding is the mathematically correct decision here.

Heads-up from the small blind with Q3s and very deep stacks (~181bb). This is a hand with reasonable heads-up equity and good postflop playability, so I want to seize the initiative rather than limp. A standard 3x-ish open to 300 applies pressure to the big blind, builds a pot when I’m often ahead of his calling range, and gives me fold equity. If Elon 3-bets large I’ll re-evaluate (likely fold), but if he calls I’ll c-bet or realize equity on Q/flush turns. Calling is too passive and folding is overly tight here, so raise to 300.

J5o is a weak offsuit hand with poor connectivity and playability out of position against a SB raise. Minni's raise from SB after button fold is wide-ranging, but defending this trash hand preflop OOP risks difficult postflop spots with low equity realization. Pot odds are 200 into 400 (33% needed), but equity vs her wide range (~35-40%) is marginal, and fold equity isn't worth a raise. Standard fold to preserve stack for better opportunities.

I have pocket Aces on the button with two players to act (SB Elon 18,100; BB Claude 3,435). Pot is 150 and the cost to see the flop is 100. This is the best hand preflop — I want to build the pot and avoid letting multiple speculative hands see a cheap flop. I’ll open-raise to a size large enough to charge draws and to induce the short-stack (Claude) to commit chips or isolate heads-up. A raise to 900 balances value extraction and fold equity: it prices out some callers, gives Claude an opportunity to shove (where I’m happy to call), and still keeps the pot manageable against Elon. Therefore: raise to 900 total preflop.

Facing a large 9x BB raise to 900 from Minni on the button preflop in the small blind. My hand is 7h8d offsuit, a speculative connector but too weak to call out of position with poor immediate pot odds (calling 850 into 1050 pot) and uncertain implied odds given Minni's aggressive history. No value to raise, and folding preserves stack for better spots in a long tournament with deep stacks.

Calling with J9 from the Big Blind. Good pot odds (43% equity needed, J9 has better) against Minni's likely wide button-raising range. Stack depth allows for post-flop play while maintaining options. This is a standard profitable call in position with decent playability.

Checking with J9 on 3h9s6s. Middle pair is decent but vulnerable against Minni's wide button range. My stack is getting short (2635), so I want to keep the pot manageable while gathering information. Checking allows me to see if Minni continues with her wide range and make better decisions on turn/river. This also allows me to hit J or 9 on the turn while controlling variance. If Minni checks back, I gain a free card. If she bets, I can evaluate based on my equity and remaining stack.

I have pocket Aces on a 3h-9s-6s flop, heads-up vs Claude who is a short stack (≈2.6k) and just called my 900 preflop. This is an overpair to the board and likely ahead of his calling range (pairs, A-high, suited connectors, spade combos). The board has two spades, so there are potential one-card flush draws if he holds two spades — and a small bet will charge those draws and get value from worse pairs and hands that will likely shove given his stack. I want a bet size that (1) extracts value from pocket pairs and weaker made hands, (2) charges spade draws, and (3) invites or induces a shove so I can call comfortably with Aces. Betting ~1,200 (≈65% pot) achieves that balance: it’s big enough to deny free cards to many draws but small enough that Claude will often commit with worse hands or shove, which I’m happy to call. Therefore: c‑bet 1,200 for value/protection.

Calling with J9 (middle pair) on 3h9s6s. Pot odds are favorable (need 28% equity, J9 has ~35-40% vs Minni's wide button range). Short stack but calling preserves turn card option. Standard profitable call in this spot.

With J♦9♥ on 3♥9♠6♠4♦, I have middle pair but critically short stack (1435 vs 4250 pot). Minni has been aggressive throughout (raised preflop and flop). The turn blank 4♦ doesn't change the dynamics. My J9 has roughly 35-40% equity but is vulnerable. Best play is to check: (1) If Minni checks, I get a free river card with equity; (2) If she bets, I'll have excellent pot odds to call anyway given stack size. This is standard poker with a vulnerable middle pair and short stack—information gathering via check is superior to either folding a decent hand or committing chips unnecessarily.

I hold Ad As on 3h-9s-6s-4d, heads-up vs Claude who is very short (1,435 effective). The pot is 4,250. Claude called my preflop raise and my 1,200 c-bet on the flop, so his range is lots of pairs (9x,6x,77-88), some A‑x, and spade draws. I’m way ahead of most of that range (only sets beat me) and the remaining stack is small relative to the pot. Shoving here maximizes value and fold equity: it forces Claude to risk his tournament life with poor pot odds (~25%) and gets called by worse pairs and draws, while the downside to me is minimal. I’ll put him all‑in and extract his stack now.

Calling all-in with J♦9♥. Middle pair with solid kicker against Minni's wide aggressive range. Pot odds require ~20% equity (5685:1435), and J9 has ~35-40% equity minimum. Stack-committed situation makes this mathematically sound and strategically correct. The 4♦ turn card is a blank that doesn't improve her range meaningfully.

Heads-up on the button/SB with Jd8d, a strong suited connector with good playability in position. Deep stacks allow for post-flop maneuvering. Minni in BB defends wide based on history. Raising builds the pot when ahead equity-wise or takes down the blinds frequently. Sizing to 3x the BB (300 total) is standard and balanced.

Heads-up situation: Elon (button/small blind) opened to 300; I’m in the big blind out of position with K♠5♠. Pot is 400 and it costs me 200 more to call (I’d put 300 total) — that requires ~33% equity. K5s is a playable heads‑up hand: it has a King blocker and spade equity, and plays well postflop. With deep stacks I prefer pot control OOP with a marginal but playable hand rather than bloating the pot with a light 3‑bet. Calling keeps his weaker hands in, lets me realize equity, and preserves flexibility on multi‑street play. Plan: call to 300; on the flop I’ll continue with top pair/draws, float small c‑bets when appropriate, and fold to heavy aggression with no connectivity.

I have K♠5♠ on Td 9d 5h — a marginal made hand (bottom pair) out of position. Elon opened wide preflop and has position; he checked this flop, which is consistent with many missed broadways, medium pairs or pot‑control hands. With deep stacks I don't want to inflate the pot OOP with a one‑pair hand that is easily outdrawn and will be called by many better hands or diamond draws. Checking keeps the pot under control, preserves showdown value, and lets me call small-to-medium c-bets to realize equity. Plan: check here; if Elon bets small (roughly ≤1/3–1/2 pot) I will call and re-evaluate on the turn, but fold to large sizing or a check‑raise that shows strong hands. If he checks back I'll take it to showdown or extract value only on safe runouts or improvements.

Flop of Td9d5h is excellent for Jd8d: open-ended straight draw (8-9-T-J, any 7 or Q completes) plus nut flush draw, giving massive equity (~50%+ against most ranges). Minni checks from BB after calling preflop raise, indicating weakness or medium strength; history shows she defends wide but checks flops often. In position heads-up with deep stacks (17800 effective), continuation bet semi-bluffs for value from worse draws/pairs, protection, and pot building. Size to ~2/3 pot (400) balances value and fold equity without overbetting.

I call 400. Heads‑up on Td 9d 5h I have K♠5♠ — bottom pair plus backdoor spade and a King blocker. Elon’s c‑bet of 400 into a 1000 pot (~40% sizing) gives me ~28.6% pot odds (400/(1000+400)), and my hand has roughly 30–35% equity vs a typical continuation range of missed broadways, some pairs and bluffs. Calling keeps worse hands and bluffs in, preserves pot control OOP, and lets me realize equity and re‑evaluate on the turn. Plan: call now, fold to large turn aggression if I don’t improve, and extract value or apply pressure only if I pair my K/5 or complete a spade draw.

Board Td 9d 5h Qc. I hold K♠5♠ — a vulnerable bottom pair with a good kicker blocker (King) but no real backdoor diamond completed. Pot is 1,400 and I'm out of position. Betting here is thin value: better hands (T‑x, 9‑x, Qx) will call and stronger hands can raise me off my pair. Checking keeps the pot under control, lets me realize equity vs Elon's wide HU c‑betting range, and induces bluffs. Plan: check and call small‑to‑medium bets (roughly up to ~1/3–1/2 pot) where I still get reasonable pot odds and can beat bluffs; fold to large bets or any raise because my pair is often behind. If Elon checks back I get to the river cheaply and can re‑evaluate on showdown or a safe runout.

Turn Qc completes my straight (8-9-T-J-Q) for a very strong hand, likely the best possible straight as higher broadway requires a K which isn't on board. The board is draw-heavy with diamond flush draw and possible straight draws completed, but Qc reduces some flush outs and adds showdown value hands like Qx to villain's range. Minni has checked twice, showing weakness or trap potential, but her flop call suggests medium strength (pair, draw). Deep effective stacks (17k), position, and history of her calling bets (e.g., Hand 33, 39) indicate value betting to extract from worse made hands (top pair, two-pair, weaker straights) while protecting against draws. Pot-sized bet or close maximizes value without overexposing to hero calls. Betting 1000 (~70% pot) balances thin value, fold equity from draws/bluffs, and pot control if checked back.

Turn decision: I hold K♠5♠ on Td 9d 5h Qc. Pot is 2,400 and Elon has raised to 1,000, so calling would cost 1,000 for ~41.7% equity. My hand is a vulnerable bottom pair with no realistic backdoor (only 5 outs: 3 Kings + 2 Fives ≈ 11% to improve on the river) and limited showdown value versus a turn‑raising range that strongly contains Qx, T/9, sets and other strong value hands (plus a few bluffs). Raising OOP with this hand is unattractive — I have poor fold equity and risk getting 3‑bet or called by better hands. Given the sizing, required equity, and Elon's aggressive line, folding and preserving my 21,200 stack for better spots is the correct play.

Heads-up on the button with Q9 suited (Qh9h) is a clear open-raise candidate: good playability, suit and a queen blocker, plus postflop position advantage. I want to take the initiative, apply pressure to the big blind’s wide calling range, and keep the pot manageable if called. Standard open to 2x the BB (raise to 200 total) accomplishes that — large enough to charge draws and marginal hands but small enough to keep worse hands in. Plan: c-bet favorable flops (Q-high, hearts, and many dry boards), pot-control or check behind on coordinated boards, and re-evaluate if Elon 3-bets (fold or call depending on sizing and ranges).

Heads-up in BB vs Button/SB min-raise to 200 (small raise, wide button range expected). A9o is a premium hand heads-up with a strong ace blocker, good equity vs wide range, and postflop playability. Opponent Minni has been aggressive with frequent raises but folds to 3bets in history (e.g., Hand 36). 3betting takes initiative, builds pot with strong hand, and applies pressure. Sizing to 600 (3x the raise) is standard for value/isolation deepstacked.

I opened to 200 on the button with Q♥9♥ and Elon 3-bet to 600 (I must call 400 more). Q9s is a strong heads-up calling hand: it has a queen blocker, suit for flush/straight potential, and excellent post‑flop playability in position. Elon’s 3-bet sizing is fairly small and likely represents a wide range, so calling keeps worse and middling hands in and lets me exploit position postflop. 4-betting as a bluff is possible but I don’t want to inflate the pot out of position with a marginal hand; 4-betting for value is inappropriate. Plan: call to 600 total, see the flop, c-bet favorable Q‑high/heart/dry boards, and opt for pot control or check‑back on coordinated boards — then re-evaluate versus continuation bets or further aggression.

Heads-up on a dry rainbow flop 2s6c8h after I 3bet preflop and got called. My Ad9c has overcards with the nut ace blocker, decent equity against Minni's calling range (which includes floats, weak pairs, and draws), and good fold equity due to my strong 3bet range representation. Board favors my range with no low cards pairing well often. OOP but initiative is mine; standard continuation bet ~2/3 pot to deny equity and build pot if called. Minni aggressive pre but history shows folds to postflop aggression. Sizing 800 maintains pressure without overcommitting.

I raise to the minimum (to 1600). Recap: I opened to 200 with Q♥9♥, Elon 3‑bet to 600 and I called. On 2♠‑6♣‑8♥ he bets 800 into a 2000 pot. My raw equity to simply call (pairing a Q/9 by river + the very small backdoor heart path) is borderline relative to the pot odds (~24–27% equity vs ~28.6% needed), so a pure call is marginal. Raising to 1600 is a positional, minimum-sized semi‑bluff that buys fold equity against a wide 3‑bet/c‑bet range, leverages my Q blocker, and allows me to take the initiative in a small pot with deeper stacks. If he folds I win the pot now; if he calls I keep the initiative and can continue on favorable turns; if he 4‑bets large I’ll respect that and fold this marginal holding. This line maximizes pressure and exploitability in heads‑up play while keeping future options open.

Facing a flop raise from Minni after my continuation bet as the preflop 3-bettor on a dry, rainbow 2s6c8h board. Ad9c is Ace-high with overcards and nut ace blocker, offering some equity against bluffs and weak pairs, but no pair or draw. Minni's button calling range preflop includes speculative hands, pairs, and broadways; her raise here polarizes to value (88/66, sets unlikely, overpairs like TT+) or bluffs (gutshots like 79o/47o, pure floats). Pot odds to call 800 into 3600 (~4.5:1) are decent, but OOP calling air leads to difficult turn/river decisions with 17k stack deep. Raising adds fold equity but risks stack-off with marginal equity vs value. History shows Minni aggressive pre but folds to postflop pressure often (e.g., Hand 40, 43); however, her flop raise suggests strength. Best to fold, preserve stack for better spots in tournament with 150 hands total.

Heads-up on the button/SB vs Minni in BB with Qs5s. Suited queen-high is a standard raising hand from this position preflop due to excellent implied odds, straight/flush potential, and postflop playability with position. Standard raise size in this game is 300 based on history. Limping is suboptimal as it denies equity realization; folding too tight. Pot 150, already posted 50 SB, raising to 300 total commits 250 more. Deep stacks allow for flexibility.

Heads-up vs the button open to 300. I’m in the big blind with 5♥9♣ — a weak, unsuited hand with limited postflop playability and I’ll be out of position. That said, calling 200 more to see the flop gives decent pot odds (call 200 to win a 400 pot -> ~25%) and the SB’s opening range is wide in heads-up, so this hand has enough equity to defend. I won’t 3-bet — no fold equity and this isn’t a good bluff or value 3-bet. Plan: call to see the flop, proceed cautiously OOP and fold to heavy pressure unless I pair or pick up meaningful equity.

I have 9♣5♥ on A♦8♣6♦ — no pair, no meaningful draw and I’m out of position vs the button’s wide opening range. I defended preflop for price, but this flop heavily favors the opener (many A-x and broadway/overcards). I have very little equity or fold equity, so the correct line is to check and concede the initiative. If Elon checks back we see a cheap showdown; if he bets I’ll fold to normal c-bets and only continue to very small probes or if I pair/improve on the turn.

Heads-up on the button post-flop with Qs5s on Ad 6d 8c board after Minni checks. Hand has queen-high, backdoor spade flush draw, and backdoor straight draws (e.g., running spades or 4-7), giving ~25-30% equity against typical BB calling ranges. Board is Ace-high with diamond draw, favoring my range as preflop raiser. Minni's check suggests weakness or trapping, but her history shows she calls frequently and can be bluffed. Position allows control; a small continuation bet (1/3 pot) exploits fold equity, denies equity to her draws/improvers, and builds pot if called with showdown value or further draws. Checking back risks giving free cards to her Ax/draws. Betting 200 is balanced size for this dynamic, deep stacks (17100 eff).

Call 200. Elon’s flop bet is very small (200 into an 800 pot), so I’m getting ~20% pot odds. I have ~10 realistic outs (four 7s to complete a 5-6-7-8-9 straight plus three remaining 9s and three remaining 5s to pair) for roughly ~38% to improve by the river, plus a tiny backdoor club chance — more than enough to continue for this price heads‑up against a wide button range. Plan: call to see a cheap turn and realize equity; if I miss I'll fold to substantial turn aggression but will continue (check/call or raise) if I pair, hit the straight, or pick up strong club/runner equity.

Check. On this turn my hand (9♣5♥) is essentially air: no pair, no flush possibility, only a one-card straight (7) as a clean out (4 outs ≈ 4/46 = 8.7%) and the 3 remaining 9s/3 remaining 5s (6 more cards) which may pair but often won’t be good vs Elon's c-bet range (A‑x, broadways, and now some 6x after the board paired). The turn paired the 6s and removed backdoor equity I had hoped for, so my realistic equity to win a river showdown is low (~<15% vs a continuing range). There is no bet to face now and I’m out of position, so the correct, low-variance line is to check and avoid bloating the pot with air. Plan: check and fold to any meaningful bet on the turn (only consider calling very tiny probes that give correct pot odds); if Elon checks back I see a cheap river and can realize my weak equity or fold if I miss.

Turn board pairs the 6s, bringing a possible straight draw completer (7-9) and club flush draw potential, while making trips/sets more in villain's range. Minni has checked twice, indicating possible weakness or mid-strength Ax/pairs without improvement, but her history shows frequent calling stations (e.g., calling large bets with marginal holdings in hands 33,39). My Qs5s is queen-high with minimal showdown value (~20-25% equity vs. her flop-calling range of Ax, 87, 67, diamond draws) and backdoor spade draw. Preflop raiser image + small flop c-bet called positions me strongly for Ax6x, 66, A8. With 16.9bb effective? Wait, deep stacks (17k/22k, 170bb+), position, and pot 1000, a half-pot bet (500) exploits fold equity against her capped range, denies equity to draws/improvers, and builds pot for river leverage. Checking risks free river cards to OESD/flush draws or check-raises, suboptimal for thin value/bluff balance. Sizing mirrors flop small bet for consistency.

Fold. Heads‑up out of position with 9♣5♥ on A♦8♣6♦6♣: Elon bets 500 into a 1500 pot. Calling 500 to win ~2000 requires ~25% equity. My realistic river equity is well below that: naive outs are 4 sevens (straight) + 3 nines + 3 fives = 10 outs (10/46 ≈ 22%), but the pair outs are often ‘dirty’ (still behind A‑x, 6x, 8x, or any stronger pair), and the backdoor club chance is gone after the turn. Effective equity vs Elon’s continuing range is likely in the mid‑teens, so this call is -EV. A raise/bluff OOP with no blockers or showdown value is thin and unnecessary given stacks. Preserve chips and fold to the turn bet.

Heads-up on the button (SB) with K♦7♥ — a playable, often-best hand in heads-up play. Pot is 150 and it’s only 50 to call, but I want to seize initiative, build the pot against worse hands and avoid giving the big blind a cheap price to see a flop. A standard 3x BB open to 300 (total) is appropriate: it’s consistent with table sizing, keeps the pot manageable with a marginal hand, preserves post‑flop flexibility, and gives fold equity. If Elon 3‑bets big I can fold and protect my stack; if he calls I have position and can play the hand aggressively on many flops. Therefore I raise to 300.

Preflop in BB facing a button/SB raise to 300 (3x BB) with K8o. Pot is 400, calling 200 more gives excellent pot odds (3:1). Button ranges are wide, especially from SB/button in heads-up play, including many worse kings, Ax suited, suited connectors, etc. K8o has decent blocker value with the Kh, playable postflop equity, and reverse implied odds aren't terrible deep-stacked. Folding is too tight given odds; 3-betting OOP as a bluff with mediocre blocker isn't ideal vs aggressive Minni who calls raises often per history. Calling to realize equity and control pot size OOP is balanced EV play to maximize stack long-term.

Heads-up on a dry Th-4c-2d flop (rainbow, disconnected). Holding Kh8s for King-high with a backdoor spade draw. Preflop call was correct for odds and playability against Minni's wide button range, which includes many Ax, Kx, suited broadways/gappers, and small pairs. Minni checked to me OOP, representing weakness or traps, but this board misses much of her range (few sets, top pair unlikely with T-high board). Betting risks bloating the pot OOP with no made hand and thin value/protection, while vulnerable to check-raises from 2p/sets/draws she might float pre. Checking realizes equity, controls pot size, and keeps her guessing about my range (which hits pairs/draws here). Turn will clarify draws/straights, and King blocker helps vs her overcards. Balanced, low-variance play deep-stacked to grind profitably over 150 hands.

I opened preflop to 300 and he flat-called, so in position I have range advantage. The flop T♥-4♣-2♦ is very dry and misses a large portion of his calling range (many broadways and small pairs). K♦7♥ has one clean overcard and a backdoor diamond — some showdown value but mostly a bluff/semibluff candidate. As the preflop aggressor I should take the initiative and c-bet to fold out his weaker holdings and collect the pot. I’ll bet about 2/3 pot (400 into 600) to apply pressure: if he folds I take it down, if he calls I’ll reevaluate on the turn and only continue with improved equity (a king/diamond) or use pot control; if he raises big I’ll give credit and fold this marginal hand.

Facing a 400 bet into 1000 pot (~40% pot) on a dry Th4c2d rainbow board with Kh8s (King-high + backdoor spade). Excellent pot odds (2.5:1) to call OOP. Minni's button range is wide (Ax, Kx, suited broadways, pairs, connectors), and this board misses much of it—no flush draws, few straight draws (only 53,35 etc. unlikely). My K-high has good showdown value vs her bluffs/floats/missed draws, solid equity (~40-45% vs polarized betting range), and reverse implied odds manageable deep-stacked. Folding too tight given odds/playability; raising OOP as semi-bluff risks vs her calling history (folds sometimes but stations light) and commits too much without strong equity/draw. Calling realizes equity, controls pot, disguises hand strength for turn value-bet potential or fold to bigger bets. Balanced, low-variance play to grind edges over 150 hands.

Heads-up pot of 1400 on turn board Th4c2d2c (paired deuces, rainbow except two clubs). Holding Kh8s for king-high no pair, no draw, pure showdown value vs bluffs/floats/missed draws from Minni's wide button range (Ax, Kx, suited broadways, pairs, connectors). Preflop/flop call was for odds and playability against her aggression; turn pairs low card, improving some villain 2x trips but also caps her range somewhat (fewer flush draws completed). OOP, betting air risks fold equity loss to her calls/stationing or check-raises from value; pot-sized bet commits too much without equity upside. Checking standard: induces bluffs from her wide range, realizes equity vs floats/overcards, controls pot OOP deep-stacked (17k effective), disguises strength for river. Minni history shows aggression (frequent raises/calls light, e.g., Hand 33,38,39,42) but also checks behind streets; expect mix of bets/checks. Low-variance grind to exploit edges over 150 hands.

Heads-up on the button with K♦7♥. I was the preflop aggressor and c-bet the dry T♥-4♣-2♦ flop (400) and Elon called — his calling range is wide: Tx, small pairs, and lots of overcards/air. The turn pairs the board with 2♣ which makes trips possible only if he held a deuce, but that is uncommon vs a preflop open (and he checked the turn rather than betting for value). I still have one clean overcard (K) and a backdoor diamond, and being in position I can apply pressure to fold out many of his marginal hands that called flop (Jx/Qx/9x, weak pairs). I’ll continue with a sizable but non-committal c-bet (~65% pot) to maximize fold equity while leaving room to fold to a big check-raise. If he folds I take the pot; if he calls I can re-evaluate on the river (and only continue if I improve or get good odds). Therefore bet 900 into a 1400 pot.

Heads-up OOP on turn board Th4c2d2c (paired deuces, two clubs for weak flush draw potential) with Kh8s (king-high, no pair/draw, pure SDV). Pot 2300, facing 900 bet (~39% pot) after mutual checks preflop/flop playability calls. Excellent implied odds (3.55:1 effective) deep-stacked (~17k eff). Minni's button range wide pre (Ax suited, Kx, broadways, pairs, connectors per history of loose calls/raises e.g., H33,38,39,42,44). Flop c-bet called thins to floats/value; turn pairs low 2c (hits some 2x trips ~4%, improves her pairs, weak club draw completion). Her bet sizing polarized: value (TT/44 rare, 2x possible, T4/24 thin), bluffs (missed Ax/Kx/gsd). Kh blocks top of her value; 8s backdoor irrelevant. Equity ~35-40% vs pol range (SDV beats ~30% bluffs/floats Ax<Kx; loses to 2x/sets). Folding too tight given odds/history (she overbluffs post-oop calls per patterns); raising OOP no equity/dead money bad vs her calling station tendencies. Call realizes equity, controls pot, disguises vs turns/river, sets up cheap SD/fold to big river. Low-var grind maximizes EV over 150 hands vs aggressive opp.

River OOP heads-up pot 3200 on Th4c2d2c5d board with Kh8s (king-high, no pair/draw). Called pre/flop/turn for odds/playability vs Minni's wide aggressive button range (Ax/Kx/broadways/pairs/connectors per extensive history: loose raises/calls e.g., H33,38,39,42,44). Board dry/paired lowcards, river 5d blank (no flush/straight new, ~35-40% equity vs pol range). King blocker beats her Ax bluffs/floats/missed overcards (~30-40% of range). OOP first-act: betting thin value risks calls from 2x/Tx, no fold equity as bluff vs stationy history, commits further deep (~16k eff). Checking induces bluffs/bets I snap-call profitably, free SDV vs checks (missed draws), controls pot after 1500 investment. Low-var grind exploits her over-aggression over 150 hands.

Heads‑up on the button with K♦7♥. I opened preflop and took the initiative on the flop (bet 400) and turn (bet 900); Elon called both streets which heavily weights his range toward at least a single pair (T‑x, pocket pair, maybe a deuce) or some showdown‑value holding. The river 5♦ doesn’t improve my hand — I still have just king‑high — and there are very few worse hands that will profitably call a river bet after calling two streets (while many of his likely holdings already beat me). A bluff could fold out pure air, but his line suggests he’s not pure air very often, so the bluff EV is thin. With position and the pot already sizable, the lowest variance, highest EV line is to check and go to showdown rather than bluff a thin river. Therefore I check.

Heads-up pre-flop from the SB/Button with 3d5h, a very weak offsuit hand with poor playability. Deep stacks (nearly 200BB), and while SB ranges are wide in heads-up, 35o is among the bottom of the range and likely a fold to avoid tough post-flop spots OOP or bloating the pot with a weak hand. Minni in BB will defend wide, but folding the SB is standard to preserve stack for better opportunities over 150 hands.

Heads-up on the button with A♥J♣ is a strong, positionally advantaged hand. I have the ace-blocker and good playability postflop, so I want to take the initiative and build the pot while keeping fold equity. The minimum to 200 is a bit small and invites too much aggression; I’ll open to a standard ~3x BB to 300. That size wins the blinds often, gives a healthy pot to play postflop, and preserves plenty of stack for postflop maneuvering. Plan: continuation-bet favorable flops, call a normal-sized 3-bet to play postflop, and fold to very large 4-bets/shoves without good odds.

Preflop in the big blind facing a button minraise to 300 (200 to call into 400). My hand is 3sTd, a weak offsuit hand with poor playability, low cards, and gaps. Even heads-up, Minni's button raising range is wide but still favors her having better hands or better position. Defending this trash hand out of position would lead to difficult postflop spots with minimal equity and reverse implied odds. Pot odds are about 2:1, but the hand lacks the equity or implied odds to justify calling. Raising is not viable with this holding. Folding preserves stack for better spots in this long tournament.

Heads-up on the button/SB position pre-flop with 7c5c, a suited connector with playability and good implied odds. In heads-up, SB opens very wide to steal blinds or build pot in position. Minni has defended wide in previous hands, often calling raises. Folding SB here is too tight; limping is passive. Raising to 300 (3x BB) gives fold equity while keeping pot manageable if called. Strong initiative and post-flop potential with flush/straight draws.

Heads-up vs Elon's 3bb open from the SB. I have A♣3♦ — a playable Ace with decent showdown value and blocker to big Ax combos. Pot is 400 and it’s 200 to me to call (I’ve already posted 100), so calling keeps the pot manageable and lets me realize equity out of position. 3‑betting as a bluff is an option vs a very wide opener, but with a weak kicker and deep stacks I prefer to call: I can continue when I hit an Ace or appropriate texture, and fold to heavy aggression on dry boards. Plan: call 200 more (total commitment 300) and evaluate on the flop.

Check. I’m out of position with A♣3♦ on 4♠4♥8♥. My Ace-high has decent showdown value versus a wide heads‑up open and blocks some strong A‑x combos, but I have no hearts and a weak kicker so I don’t want to build a big pot by barreling here. Checking controls the pot, keeps flush draws and overcards in so I can call a sensible c‑bet and realize equity, and lets me fold to heavy aggression rather than over‑committing with a marginal hand. Plan: check‑call small/medium bets; re‑evaluate on the turn and fold to polarized check‑raises or very large sizing.

Heads-up on the button with initiative after raising pre-flop with a suited connector. Flop is 4s4h8h, a paired board with a heart flush draw, which hits some calling ranges but also favors my perceived strong range (overpairs like AA-JJ, sets, Ax, 88). Minni checks, showing weakness or trapping with a hand like a 4 or 8. With deep stacks and position, a continuation bet denies her equity on draws, takes it down often against a wide defending range (seen in prior hands where Minni calls light but folds to aggression), and keeps pressure on. Half-pot sizing maintains fold equity without overcommitting air. If called, evaluate turn with position.

Call. I have A♣3♦ out of position on 4♠4♥8♥; Elon’s bet is 400 into a 600 pot (pot now 1,000), so calling 400 would make the final pot 1,400 and I need ≈28.6% equity to justify a call. Against a wide SB opener and this flop c‑bet I’m up against a lot of missed hands, overcards and flush/heart semi‑bluffs as well as a handful of value hands (8x, 88, 4x trips). My Ace‑high has real showdown value and blocks some A‑x value combos, so calling keeps the pot manageable and lets me realize equity. Raising as a bluff is unattractive OOP with a weak kicker and no hearts/backdoor; folding surrenders too much. Plan: call to 700 total, see the turn and fold to heavy, polarized aggression unless I pair/improve or pick up a clear read to continue.

Elon did not fire on the turn (no bet to face). I have A♣3♦ — Ace‑high with a very weak kicker and no heart blocker to the board's two hearts. Pot control is the priority out of position: my hand has modest showdown value and blocks some A‑x value hands, but betting OOP as a bluff here is thin (Elon can call with Qx, 8x, any pair, or continue with various floats) and I have poor fold equity. Checking keeps the pot small, lets me see a free river to re‑evaluate, and preserves the option to fold to a polarized river bet or attempt a small value/blur on a favorable river. Plan: check and reassess based on Elon's river action.

Turn board brings the Qc, which doesn't improve my 7c5c at all—still no pair, no straight draw (gutshot buster at best but unlikely), no flush draw realized. The board is paired 4s with a backdoor heart flush draw that hasn't hit and now a Qc adding possible straight possibilities but nothing for me. Minni has checked twice now after calling my flop c-bet, indicating possible weakness, traps like pocket 4s/8s are possible but she'd likely lead or raise somewhere; more likely medium strength like Qx, 87, or stubborn draws/A-high. Deep stacks (18k+ effective), pot 1400. Betting here as a bluff has fold equity against her wide calling range seen in history (e.g., Hands 25,32,46 where she calls light but checks), but risks bloating pot with nothing on a board where calls are sticky (trips, two-pair). Position allows checking to control pot, take free river card, and reassess: if river bricks, can bluff or value thin; if improves board, pot control vital. No showdown value with 7-high, but checking exploits her check and avoids spew. Long-term EV: checking preserves stack for better spots in 150-hand tournament.

Check. Elon c‑bet the flop and then checked the turn — a line consistent with a wide, capped or missed range (weak pairs, floats, or turned-down bluffs). I hold A♣3♦: decent showdown value and a blocker to some A‑x combos, but I have no hearts and poor blockers to two‑heart flushes that the river 7♥ enables. Betting out of position here would be a thin, low fold‑equity bluff that gets called by all better pairs, completed flushes and many one‑pair hands; with deep stacks there’s little to gain and unnecessary variance to take on. Pot control and seeing a showdown is the higher EV line: check and show my Ace; I’ll call small to medium river bets but fold to a large polarized overbet if Elon gives a clear strong read.

River 7h pairs my 7c, giving me a pair of 7s for showdown value, which beats bluff-catchers like A-high, K-high, Q-high without strong kickers, and missed draws in Minni's wide flop calling range (evident from history where she defends light but folds to repeated aggression). Board is paired 4s with completed heart flush and possible straights (e.g., 56 for 4-5-6-7-8), but Minni's triple check (flop check-call, turn/river check) suggests capped range without nuts like flush, trips (88/44), or two-pair (Q8/87/45)—monsters would bet river for value. My image as preflop raiser + flop C-bet + turn check (pot control with air then) still reps some strength (overpairs, sets, draws). Deep stacks (~19k effective), pot 1400: half-pot bet extracts value from worse hands she check-calls with while maintaining fold equity vs air. Checking risks her tabling better showdown equity; betting maximizes EV long-term in 150-hand tourney.

Call 700. Pot is 2,100 and calling 700 gives me pot odds of 700/(2100+700)=25% — I only need ~25% equity to justify a call. Elon's line (SB open, small c‑bet on the flop, check on the turn, small river bet) is consistent with a wide/capped range containing many thin Qx/8x pairs, some missed floats/bluffs and the occasional strong hand (trips 4x or two‑heart flushes). My A♣3♦ has real showdown value here and blocks a number of Ax combos, and the river bet is small enough that there will be a meaningful fraction of bluffs/thin value hands in his range. Raising OOP with A3 is thin and high‑variance; folding would concede too much given the price. I’ll call the 700 to see showdown and fold only if his showdown hand is clearly stronger.

Heads-up on the button with Kc4s — marginal but playable in position. Against a single opponent I want the initiative and to avoid giving a free flop, so I’ll open-raise rather than limp. Size to 300 (≈3x the BB) is large enough to generate fold equity but small enough not to commit a deep stack. Plan: if Elon 3-bets large I will usually fold K4o (it’s often dominated); if he calls I’ll c-bet favorable flops and play cautiously on heavy resistance or coordinated boards.

Heads-up with deep stacks (20k+ effective). Minni raises to 3bb from button/SB, a standard wide opening range for her aggressive style seen throughout history (frequent small raises preflop, calls postflop often). My A7o is a strong defensive hand: ace blocker reduces her AA/AK, good top-pair potential, live cards for straights/flushes. Pot odds are excellent (400 pot, 200 to call, over 3:1 including implied). Calling realizes equity OOP while keeping pot small; folding too tight in BB vs button; 3-betting risks variance against her calling tendencies without domination.

Heads-up on flop OOP with Ad7h vs Minni's button raise range (wide ~35-40% based on history of frequent small preflop raises and postflop calls). Board J95 rainbow-coordinated hits her range (Jx, 9x, Tx8x, 87x, pairs) moderately but misses many Ax floats and bluffs. My ace-high has blockers to AJ/KQ/AK and decent showdown vs missed draws/Ax. Deep stacks (~20bb pot, 200bb effective) favor keeping pot small OOP. Donk betting air is -EV OOP; induces raises from her value. Check realizes equity, sets up check-call/raise, controls pot, free turn card to realize backdoor potential (A,7). Folding too tight vs her wide range.

I opened preflop from the button and have the initiative. Flop is 5c Jh 9s and I hold Kc4s — no pair, no meaningful draw. Heads‑up, my preflop raising range contains many hands that miss this board, so a high-frequency c‑bet is the correct line to extract fold equity and deny free cards. Betting 400 into the 600 pot (~66% pot) requires Elon to fold >40% to be profitable (break‑even fold rate = 400/(600+400)=40%), which is realistic against a wide calling range. Size is large enough to credibly represent a J/9 or a strong draw but not so large that I’m committed if called. Plan: take the pot if he folds; if he calls, proceed cautiously on the turn and give up to significant aggression unless I improve (or get a very cheap card to barrel); if he check‑raises I’ll mostly fold K4o given poor showdown value and his likely strong range.

OOP on J95 rainbow flop after defending wide BB vs button raise. Minni's 400 c-bet into 1000 (40% pot) is standard sizing for her aggressive style (frequent preflop raises, flop bets seen in hands like 42,44,48). Her range: value Jx/9x/pairs/sets, polarized with bluffs/draws (T8,87, floats, Ax). Ad7h blocks AJ/KQ/AK (key value), ace-high has showdown vs missed Ax/bluffs, backdoor equity. Direct odds 3.5:1, excellent implied deep (~200bb eff). Calling realizes equity OOP, induces bluffs, controls pot size vs her calling station tendencies (e.g., hand 33 calls big rivers, hand 39). Folding too tight in position; raising risks variance OOP without strong draw/blockers domination. Pot odds and range interaction favor call.

OOP on turn in heads-up pot after calling flop c-bet with Ad7h (ace-high showdown value, AJ/KQ/AK blockers vs Minni's wide button range). Board Jh9s5c6h worsens slightly (brings 78 straight draws, heart flush draw possibilities), but my hand retains strong blockers to top value (AJ, AK) and showdown vs her Ax floats, missed draws, bluffs. Pot 1400 (~14bb), deep effective stacks (~180bb). Donk betting OOP turn with no made hand/draw is -EV: folds out worse, gets raised by value/draws, builds pot OOP. Checking controls pot size, induces bluffs from her aggressive continuation tendencies (frequent postflop aggression seen in hands 33,38,39,42,44), realizes equity cheaply vs her wide range (~35% button opens), sets up check-call/raise. Excellent implied odds if she bets small. Folding off equity too tight. History shows Minni calls stations rivers but bets turns (e.g., hand 42), so check extracts max value from worse while minimizing losses.

Pot is 1,400 and I hold K♣4♠ on 5♣ J♥ 9♠ 6♥. I have no pair and no meaningful draw; my flop c‑bet got called so Elon’s range contains many hands that beat or at least won’t fold to a second barrel (Jx, 9x, pocket pairs, some turn-checking draws). The turn check from him shows weakness, but it also reduces my fold equity — a sizable turn bluff will often be called. Given my poor showdown value and the desire to control the pot heads‑up, I’ll check back the turn, preserve stack, and re-evaluate the river (look to barrel only on very favorable runouts or give up to real aggression). If he leads into me after I check, I’ll fold K4.

River OOP heads-up with Ad7h (ace-high, AJ/AK/KQ blockers) vs Minni's aggressive button range (~35% opens, frequent c-bets seen in hands 42/44/48, but checked turn suggesting possible missed draw/weakness). Board J-9-6-5-K rainbow brings possible 78/T8 straights (weaker than before), AK/KQ broadway, but Kd often misses her floats/bluffs/Ax. Pot 1400 (~14bb), deep eff stacks (~180bb). Betting thin ace-high OOP is -EV: value from worse (Ax/pairs/draws) folds often, vulnerable to raises from Jx/9x/sets/straights; builds pot OOP. Checking controls pot, realizes showdown equity vs her wide range (beats bluffs, missed T8/78/heart draws, weak Ax/Jx), induces bets from worse (her aggression postflop e.g., hand 38/39 rivers), sets up cheap call/value extract. History shows Minni stations calls big but also bets thin; check maximizes vs both. Folding off equity too tight ~20% equity OOP.

I’ve been checked to on the river and the K♦ pairs my K♣, giving me top pair with a very weak kicker. Elon’s line (call preflop, call my flop c‑bet, check turn and check river) looks passive/medium‑strength — lots of Jx, 9x and small pocket pairs in his range that I now beat. There are some Kx combos that beat me, but those are less likely to have played this exact passive line and check‑checking the turn/river. With the pot at 1,400 a half‑pot value bet (~700) should get thin calls from Jx/9x/small pairs and the odd missed draw while not overcommitting if I run into a stronger hand. Size also keeps fold equity against marginal holdings low and avoids turning my hand into a bluff target. If he check‑raises I’ll strongly consider folding given my poor kicker and the line he took.

River OOP heads-up deep (~20k eff stacks) with Ad7h ace-high + AJ/AK blockers vs Minni's aggressive button range (frequent small preflop raises ~35%, flop c-bets but turn check indicating possible missed draw/weak J9x now thin value/bluff on Kd). Board 5c-Jh-9s-6h-Kd completes broadway (KQ/AK), 78/T8 guts, heart flush possible but rainbow-ish. Her 700 bet into 1400 pot (~50%) after checking turn is polarized: value Jx/9x/KT+ or bluffs (missed Ahxh flush draws, 78/T8, Ax floats). Pot odds 2100:700 (~3:1, need 25% equity); Ad7h crushes bluffs/Ax (~30-40% vs polarized range), blocks top value, history of her thin river bets/station calls (hands 33/38/39/42/48) favors cheap realization vs wide range. Calling controls risk, extracts from worse, consistent with prior calling strategy minimizing variance OOP.

Heads-up preflop from the SB/Button with pocket 2s, a strong hand in this format. Minni has been aggressive historically but calls frequently. Raising for value and to deny equity to her calling range while maintaining initiative postflop. Standard sizing to 3x the BB (300 total) given the pot of 150 and my positional advantage. Deep stacks allow flexibility, but building the pot with a premium hand here maximizes EV over the long tournament.

Heads-up vs an opening raise to 300 from the button. I’m in the big blind with 5h6h suited. Calling 200 more (to 300 total) is cheap — pot is ~400 so I’m getting ~3:1 (~33%) pot odds. 5-6 suited has decent equity vs a wide HU opening range and very good playability postflop (flush/straight potential), and stacks are deep enough to realize that equity. Being out of position is a downside so I don’t want to 3-bet light and bloat the pot unnecessarily; calling preserves pot control and lets me see a flop to act on draws or pairs. Plan: defend by calling, play aggressively on favorable boards, fold to heavy action on dry, adverse boards if I miss.

I hold 5h6h on Qs–6d–8h: second pair (pair of 6s) with a weak 5-kicker and only a backdoor (runner-runner) heart/straight possibility. Heads‑up vs a wide button open, many of Elon’s hands (Ax without a Q, Kx, Jx, smaller pairs, missed broadways) are behind me, though Qx, 88, or sets crush me. I’ll bet for value and protection — a half‑pot bet keeps worse one‑pair hands and drawing hands paying, and denies free cards when OOP. Size (300 into a 600 pot) extracts value from worse hands, charges floats/draws, and sets up a controlled decision on the turn. If he folds I take the pot; if he calls I’ll re‑evaluate on the turn; if he raises large it’s likely very strong (Q/8/set) and I’ll fold or proceed cautiously depending on sizing.

Heads-up on the flop with pocket 22 against Minni's small bet of 300 into 600 (now pot 900). The board is Qs6d8h, rainbow-ish with one heart. My hand is an underpair, blocking sets, and has decent equity (~30-40% vs her calling range including Qx, 98s, flush/straight draws, Ax, mid pairs). Preflop I raised as the button for value/isolation, and she called OOP, so her range is wide but capped somewhat. Her small bet sizing suggests possible weakness, probe, or thin value (Qx, 98). Raising risks bloating pot OOP with a marginal hand; folding is too tight given pot odds (call 300 to win 900, 4.3:1) and implied odds deepstacked (19k effective). Calling controls pot size, realizes equity cheaply, disguises strength if I hit, and allows turn flexibility (check-raise, etc.). Consistent with aggressive preflop line.

I have middle pair (6s) with a weak 5-kicker and a one-card heart flush draw on the turn (Qs–6d–8h–3h). Elon called my flop bet and then checked the turn, showing some potential weakness or a marginal holding. Betting here protects my hand (denies free rivers to overcards and straight cards), extracts value from worse pairs and Kx/Ax-type hands, and charges pure draws. With deep stacks I’ll use a standard half‑pot continuation bet to accomplish that without committing myself. I’ll bet 600 into the 1200 pot. If he folds I win the pot; if he calls I’ll re-evaluate on the river (value-bet big if a heart completes my flush, proceed cautiously or check behind vs heavy action on blank rivers). If he check-raises very large on the turn I’ll assume a strong holding (Q/8/set) and fold rather than bloat the pot with just middle pair.

Heads-up on the turn with 22 on Qs6d8h3h board (pot 1800, facing 600 bet from OOP Minni). My pocket 22 remains an underpair but clean, with 4 outs to a set (~8% equity improvement) and showdown value against her likely Qx-overpairs, mid-pairs, or missed draws. Preflop I raised IP, she called OOP wide; flop bet small (probe/thin value/draw), I flatted IP for pot control and equity realization. Her turn overbet relative to flop sizing (600 into ~1200 post-flop) polarizes to value (QJ/QT/98 str, sets unlikely) or bluffs (heart draws like AhXh, KhJh; str draws 75/97/54). Equity vs range ~35-45% (blocks low sets/pairs); pot odds 3:1 require ~25%, plus deep stacks/implied for set. Raising risks OOP vs her draw-heavy range post-turn and builds pot OOP on river with marginal made hand. Calling realizes equity cheaply IP, induces bluffs on blank rivers, allows check-fold/raise river flexibility. Consistent with patient IP line vs aggressive OOP opponent who frequently donk bets (per history: hands 40-50 show Minni donk-heavy). Fading river value fine with position.

River summary and plan: Board Qs–6d–8h–3h–Ah and I hold 5h6h — the river completes my heart flush (Ah–8h–6h–5h–3h). Elon called my flop and turn bets, so his continuing range is broad (Qx, Kx, Ax, small pairs, combo draws). Very few hands beat my flush: he must hold two hearts in the hole to make a flush as well; the combinatorial chance of that given the known cards is very small (C(8,2)/C(47,2) ≈ 2.6%). Because my hand is nearly always ahead I should be value-betting, not checking. I want a size big enough to extract from top-pairs, A‑x and sticky Kx hands and to charge missed draws, but not so huge that every marginal hand folds. Betting 3000 into a 2400 pot (~1.25× pot) accomplishes that — it extracts maximum value from hands that will call and still keeps reasonable fold equity. If he folds I take down the 2400 pot; if he calls I expect to be ahead most of the time; only a very small portion of his range (two‑heart combos) significantly beats me, which I’m willing to risk for the positive EV. Action: bet 3000.

Heads-up on river with pocket 22 on Qs6d8h3hAh board (pot 5400, facing 3000 bet from OOP Minni). My hand is a clean underpair with showdown value against bluffs/air but crushed by her polarized range: nut flushes (Ahxh, broadway flush draws completed), top pairs (Qx, Ax paired), sets (QQ,66,88 unlikely blocked). Preflop raiser/call station IP line realized equity but river Ah completes obvious flush draws (board had two hearts post-turn) and provides overpair outs, favoring her value-heavy range. Her donk line all streets with increasing sizing (300 flop, 600 turn, 3000 river overbet into 2400) screams polarization: nuts or bluff. Possible bluffs: missed str draws (97,75,54 gutshots), pure air, but limited on this board and her history shows aggressive donks but frequent value calls (hands 33,39,44,49,50). Pot odds ~1.8:1 require ~36% equity; vs polarized range ~20-30% tops (0% vs value, 100% vs bluffs, but bluff freq <36% given sizing/line). No fold equity to raise OOP-crush potential. Stacks deep (18k eff) but no implied odds; folding marginal showdown hand preserves stack for better spots in tournament grind. Consistent with pot control theme, folding to polarized overbet on scary river.

Heads-up on the button with Kc Jd is a very strong opening hand. I’ve already posted the 50 SB; calling 50 (completing) is passive and gives the BB cheap odds. A standard 3x BB open to 300 (total) is the right play: it applies pressure, builds value when I’m ahead of the BB’s defending range, preserves my position postflop, and matches the table’s usual open sizing. Stacks are deep enough to avoid overcommitting — if Elon 3-bets I’ll re-evaluate based on sizing and his tendencies. Plan: raise to take initiative and play position on the flop.

Preflop in BB facing a standard raise to 300 (200 to call) from Minni on the button/SB. Her range is very wide here as SB vs BB, including many speculative hands, broadway, suited connectors, etc. My 87o is an offsuit connector with decent playability, straight potential, and good implied odds given deep stacks (~180bb). Pot odds are excellent: calling 200 into a 400 pot offers about 3:1 (25% equity needed), and 87o realizes well against her wide range heads-up. Folding is too tight; 3-betting is off-table without premium. Standard defense OOP vs button aggression. Will call and play postflop cautiously OOP.

Flop Jc6h7c hits my 87o perfectly for middle pair (sevens) with an open-ended straight draw (5 or 9). Board has two clubs, so backdoor flush draw, but my equity is massive (~60-70% vs her wide SB raise/call range including Ax suited, broadway, suited connectors, pocket pairs). OOP first to act, pot 600, deep stacks. Betting for value and protection against overcards, flush draws, weaker pairs, or pure floats. Her range has many Jx, 88-TT that pay us off, draws that we charge. Half-pot bet sizes well here to build pot without scaring off too much. Bet 400.

I raised preflop to take the initiative and on this flop (Jc 6h 7c) I have top pair with a very strong King kicker. Pot is 1000 and Elon’s small bet of 400 is consistent with a wide c-bet or thin value/float range heads-up. I want to extract value from worse Jx, Kx and medium pairs while charging straight/club draws and preserving fold equity, so I’ll raise for value and protection. I’ll make the minimum raise to 800 total — big enough to punish draws and get value without scaring off thin callers. If Elon 3-bets I'll re-evaluate by sizing; if he calls I’ll proceed cautiously on the turn.

Facing a raise to 800 on a flop that hits my 87 perfectly for bottom pair plus open-ended straight draw (5 or 9 makes the nuts straight). Board Jc6h7c is coordinated with flush draw potential, but my equity remains strong (~45-55%) against Minni's SB raising range post my bet: Jx (often dominated), 88-TT, combo draws (e.g., 85s, 98s suited clubs), two-pair less likely. Her small 2x raise sizing suggests wide range including floats, weaker pairs, and draws we crush or have live. Folding is a clear mistake with this strength and pot odds (400 into 1800, ~18% needed). Calling OOP keeps pot manageable but gives her initiative on turn, free equity realization for her draws, and tough multi-street decisions. Raising seizes back control, builds pot for value when ahead (charges draws/Jx), denies her equity, and represents Jx/sets/overpairs. With deep stacks (~170bb effective), we can realize equity if she jams/shoves (we call profitably). Size to ~3x her raise: 2400 total bet size balances value/protection/fold equity. Her history shows aggression but folding to 3bets in spots (e.g., Hand 35,36). This maximizes EV long-term.

I have top pair (J) with the best remaining kicker (Kc Jd) on Jc 6h 7c. I raised the flop to get value and deny equity to draws; Elon’s big 3-bet to 2400 polarizes him to very strong made hands (sets, two-pair like J7/J6/76) and some polarized bluffs (club/overcard combos). Pot is 3800 and I must call 1600 more to see the turn — calling 1600 into 3800 gives a break-even equity of ~29.6% (1600/(3800+1600)). Against a realistic continue range I’m ahead of many hands and have enough equity to justify a call, plus I retain pot control and the ability to fold to extreme turn aggression if the board develops badly. Raising again (min-raise to 4000) overcommits out of position and only gets value from worse hands that will often fold; folding surrenders too much equity with a very strong heads-up hand. I will call and re-evaluate on the turn.

Turn pairs the 7s, upgrading my 87o to trips (777 with 8 kicker) on a J-6-7-7 board. This is a monster hand, crushing most of Minni's continuing range after calling my flop 3bet (2400 into ~1800): likely Jx (dominated), 88-JJ (behind), 67/76 two pair, sets (behind to FH potential), stubborn draws that missed. Pot 5400 OOP first to act, deep effective stacks (~15k). Value bet sizing critical: too small gets called by worse (Jx, smaller pairs); too big risks folds from 1-pair/overpairs but pot-commits her lighter holdings. Half to 2/3 pot balances value from Jx/88+/67 while protecting against unlikely river fillers (A/K outs, runner-runner FH). Her history shows calling stations in big pots (e.g., Hands 33,39,50), so 3500 builds pot, induces calls/shoves from worse, retains fold equity vs pure air/nuts. Raising off here unnecessary; check risks free card to her draws/A-high. Max value long-term.

Turn analysis: the 7d gives me two pair (J7) with a K kicker. Elon’s line (small c‑bet, big flop 3‑bet to 2400, now a 3500 turn bet) is polarized toward very strong value (sets, 76/7x/66, JJ, full houses) but also contains some bluffs. Pot is 8900; calling 3500 requires ~28% equity (3500/12400). Two pair on this paired turn has >28% equity versus a realistic continuing range and beats many of Elon’s bluffs and single‑pair hands, so folding would be too nitty and raising now would overcommit and fold out worse hands while risking a large 3‑bet shove from the small portion of his range that dominates me. I’ll call to control the pot size, deny folds from worse two‑pair/top‑pair hands, and evaluate the river — calling keeps bluffs in and lets me fold to extreme river action if the board runs out to full houses or he jams. Action: call the 3500 (total committed this hand = 2700 pre/ flop + 3500 turn = 6200).

River Ts is a blank for most ranges: doesn't complete straights (board 6-7-7-J-T has no broadway straight possibilities beyond what was already there, and gutshots unlikely after calling massive bets). My 87o has morphed into trips 7s with 8 kicker (777), crushing Minni's calling range after flop 3bet-call (2400) and turn call (3500 into 5400 pot): primarily Jx (AJ-KJ, suited clubs), overpairs 88-JJ (now two pair at best for JJ), 67/76 (full houses, but combo-specific), sets (JJ/66 rare). Beats all one-pair, two-pair hands; only loses to FH like JJ, 67, J7, 66 (low probability given her SB raise range and action). Pot 12400 OOP first to act, effective stack 11950 (~1 pot-sized). Check risks free showdown (if she has Jx/88 she checks back) and missed value; she check-calls big pots historically (33: called river 2600 with likely worse; 39: called 2000 river; 50: river 700). All-in maximizes value from Jx, 88-QQ, two-pair that she stations with; fold equity vs air/bluffs (though range polarized strong); pot-commits, but we're ahead vs calling range (~75-85% equity). Half-pot ~6000 risks check-behind or small call; overbet shove extracts max EV long-term in tournament.

Board: Jc 6h 7c 7d Ts. I hold Kc Jd — only a single pair of Jacks with a King kicker. Elon has shoved 11,950 into the 24,350 pot. Calling 11,950 would make the final pot 36,300, so I need ~11,950/36,300 ≈ 32.9% equity to break even. Elon’s line (small c‑bet on the flop, very large flop 3‑bet to 2,400, continued 3,500 on the paired turn, now a large river shove) is heavily value‑biased: sets (66,77,JJ), two‑pair/full house combos (76, 7x + board pairs), and strong continuing pairs are all very plausible. My single pair is behind most of that range and likely only ahead of thin bluffs or worse one‑pair hands that are not folding often to this line. Given the required ~33% equity and the villain’s polarizing, value‑heavy line, a call would be a negative‑EV play and would cripple my stack (leaving ~3,700). I fold to preserve chips and wait for a better spot.

Heads-up preflop from SB with 3s2c offsuit. This is one of the worst possible hands: no high cards, poor connectivity, offsuit, minimal showdown value or bluff potential postflop. Minni's BB calling range will dominate it heavily, and implied odds for any straight/flush draws are low. Deep stacks (243BB effective) but folding trash preserves chips for premium spots. Standard HU strategy folds bottom ~25-30% including 32o. Raising as a bluff is suboptimal here due to reverse implied odds and poor playability. Wait for better positions.

Heads-up on the button (I’m SB) with K♦Q♥ — a strong, positionally advantaged hand. Against a wide BB opening/calling range KQo plays very well postflop and has good equity, so I want to take the initiative and avoid letting Elon see cheap flops. A standard heads-up open to ~3x the BB is appropriate and consistent with the table action sizes, giving fold equity while leaving plenty of stack for postflop play. I’ll raise to 300 total to build the pot and play position; if he 3-bets I’ll re-evaluate based on sizing and reads.

Heads-up in BB vs aggressive SB/Button raise to 300. KJo is a strong hand with good equity against her wide button opening range, solid blockers to strong hands like KK/AA, and plays well post-flop. Her frequent raising pattern from history suggests a wide range. 3-betting applies pressure, has fold equity, and builds the pot when ahead. Sizing to 900 (3x her raise) is standard for positionally disadvantaged BB 3-bet. Deep stacks allow for post-flop play if called. Calling is viable but 3-betting maximizes value and fold equity early in tournament.

Elon 3-bet to 900 after my button open to 300. I have K♦Q♥ — a strong heads‑up hand that plays very well in position. The 3‑bet sizing is relatively small, indicating a fairly wide range (broadways, some pairs, suited connectors and bluffs). Pot is 1,200 and I need to call 600 (total 900), which gives me ~3:1 pot odds; with 15.4k effective stack I prefer to keep the pot manageable and realize equity in position rather than bloating the pot with a marginal 4‑bet. Plan: call, see the flop, and continue aggressively with top pair or strong draws; if I miss and face big aggression on dry boards I will fold. If reads later show Elon 3‑betting very light, I can adjust to 4‑bet more often, but for now calling is the optimal, balanced line.

Flop of 8d-2h-7h completely misses my KJo, leaving me with king-high and no pair, straight draw, or flush draw. The board is draw-heavy with a heart flush draw and possible straight draws (e.g., 65, T9 suited). Out of position in a 3-bet pot after Minni called my preflop raise from her button/SB range, which includes pairs (77-88+), suited connectors (65s-98s, hearts), and broadway cards that connect well here. My hand has poor playability OOP with limited fold equity on this texture—Minni's history shows she bets draws and medium strength frequently but can check weak floats. Betting as a bluff risks bloating the pot (pot 1800, stacks 23k/14k) when behind most of her range and no strong backup equity or blockers to her value. Checking preserves stack, induces bluffs/checks from her missed hands, and allows cheap fold to her likely continuation bet, aligning with GTO in OOP 3-bet pots with air. Deep tournament stage favors discipline over spew.

Heads‑up on the button with K♦Q♥ on a very dry flop 8♦ 2♥ 7♥. Elon 3‑bet preflop (smallish) but checked the flop — his checking range is wide and this board misses a lot of 3‑betting hands. I have two overcards and a backdoor heart, and I have positional advantage. I want to take the initiative, pick up folds from missed broadways/small pairs and get value from worse Kx/Qx/floaters. I'll make a constructive half‑pot continuation bet (900 into 1800). If he check‑raises big, I'll respect the strength and fold; if he calls I'll proceed cautiously on the turn and re‑evaluate.

Facing a half-pot bet of 900 into 1800 on a 872 rainbow-ish board with hearts (8d2h7h) that misses my KJo completely, leaving pure king-high air with no draws or blockers to her key holdings. In this 3-bet pot OOP, Minni's button calling range includes pairs like 77/88 (sets), suited connectors (e.g., 65s/T9s for straight draws), heart flush draws (JTs, QJs suited hearts), and floats with broadway/post-flop equity. My check-back preflop reps weakness outside top of range, but her bet targets that while protecting draws/medium strength. Pot odds require ~25% equity to call (900 into 2700), but K-high has <20% vs her range (per GTO solvers in similar spots), with horrible playability OOP on turn (any paint/heart/brick worsens position). Raising bluff unprofitable OOP without fold equity (her history shows calling down draws/medium pairs like in hands 42,51) and stacks allow (23k/14k effective). Folding preserves stack for better spots; discipline key in deep tournament vs aggressive Minni who frequently bets thin.

Heads-up on the button/SB with 98o, a playable speculative hand with good post-flop potential due to straight draws and position advantage. Minni's BB is effectively a limp, and raising from SB heads-up is standard to steal blinds or build pot in position with a wide range. History shows Minni calls raises often but folds to aggression sometimes. Raising 3x BB (to 300) balances value, bluffs, and initiative while keeping stack deep.

Heads-up, deep stacks (16500 vs 23100) and I’m in the big blind with 8♥9♦ facing a button/SB open to 300. Pot is 400, I need to put in 200 more (total commitment 300). 8-9o has good equity and playability versus a wide opening range; pot odds after a call are favorable (~600 to win for a 200 call → ~3:1, ~25% required equity). A small 3-bet to 500 would bloat the pot out of position with a marginal hand and is easily called or 4-bet by an aggressive opener, so I prefer to see a flop and realize equity. Plan: call, evaluate flop texture (raise/continue on connected/paired boards or strong draws; check and fold to heavy aggression on dry high-card boards).

Check. I’m out of position with 8♥9♦ on a dry A♦ K♣ 4♠ flop — I’ve missed completely and only have remote backdoor outs. Pot is 600 and stacks are deep (Minni 16.3k vs Elon 23.1k). Elon’s opening range will include lots of Ax/Kx and broadways that dominate my hand; my equity is very low. Bluffing out of position here is risky and will often be called, so I’ll control the pot and preserve chips. Plan: check and fold to meaningful aggression; I’ll only entertain a call to a very small C‑bet (roughly ≤1/3 pot, ~200) for pot‑odds to see a turn, but fold to larger bets or sustained pressure. If Elon checks back I’ll reassess on the turn for any improvement or bluffing opportunities.

Post-flop on a dry Ad Kc 4s board with 9h 8d (complete air, backdoor diamond draw). Minni checked her BB after calling my button raise, giving me initiative as pre-flop aggressor. Board favors my wide raising range (hits Ax, Kx, some broadways), and history shows Minni passive post-flop, often checking and calling small bets but folding to continued aggression. Half-pot c-bet exploits fold equity, builds pot if strong, and deep stacks (23k effective) allow fold equity. If called, re-eval turn with position.

Heads‑up out of position with 8♥9♦ on A♦ K♣ 4♠. I checked the flop and Elon bets 400 into a 1000 pot (≈40% pot). To call 400 I need ≈28.6% equity. My hand has only ~6 clean outs to pair by the river (~24% to improve) and even if I hit I’m often dominated by Ax/Kx or still behind two pair/top pair. The bet size is larger than my call threshold (≈1/3 pot) and a raise/bluff OOP here is thin and likely to get me in trouble with deep stacks. Long‑term the correct, low‑variance play is to fold and wait for a better spot. Therefore: fold.

Raise to 300 total. Heads-up from the button I should be opening a wide range — 8♦5♦ has decent playability (backdoor flush/straight potential) and is worth applying pressure with position. A 300 open is a standard sizing at this table and gives me initiative while leaving plenty of stack behind (16,250) to play postflop. If Elon 3-bets large I’ll fold and preserve chips; if he calls I’ll use position to c-bet favorable flops and pot-control on coordinated/wet boards. Calling is too passive and folding is too tight in this spot.

Heads-up situation with deep stacks (23.6k effective 16k). Facing a standard 3x raise from Minni in the SB/Button position, who has been aggressive and raised frequently in previous hands with a wide range. T4d is suited with straight and flush potential, offering good playability postflop. Pot odds are excellent: calling 200 into 400 (33% equity needed), and T4s has solid equity (~35-40%) against Minni's wide opening range. Folding is too tight heads-up; 3-betting is possible as a bluff but risks playing OOP with a marginal hand postflop where calling allows positionally flexible play. Prefer calling to realize equity and control pot size.

Heads-up on a highly dangerous flop: 5h-Qh-Jh, monotone hearts giving numerous flushes possible to villain's button raising range (any two hearts, Qx, Jx, broadway draws). My Td4d picks up only a gutshot straight draw to Kd (~8% equity) and backdoor diamond flush/straight, no showdown value. OOP, donk betting is suboptimal as it bloats pot with marginal hand and invites raises from strong holdings. Checking exploits times villain checks behind missed draws or weak made hands, realizes thin equity passively, and preserves stack by folding to likely aggression. Consistent with deep-stack heads-up strategy: play positionally aware postflop after loose preflop call.

Check. I have bottom pair (5s) with a weak 8 kicker on a very wet flop (Qh Jh 5h) — two overcards plus three hearts make this a dangerous texture for a c-bet. As the preflop aggressor I could barreL, but a bet here will be called by lots of Q/J and heart hands and puts me in awkward spots vs check-raises or large turn barrels. With position and a healthy stack I should control the pot: check to see what Elon does and realize equity. Plan: call a small-to-medium probe (roughly ≤ ~1/3–1/2 pot) and re-evaluate on the turn; fold to large bets or check-raises; if he checks back we get to showdown cheaply and can look to extract thin value or bluff-catch on safe runouts.

Heads-up OOP on a scary monotone heart flop (5h-Qh-Jh) where I checked and Minni checked behind, indicating her range likely missed or trapping very strong. Turn 2d is a low, off-suit card that doesn't improve me (Td4d now has only a weak backdoor diamond flush draw with three diamonds total and minimal straight outs). No made hand, showdown value, or strong draws. Equity remains low (~10-15%) against villain's polarized checking range (missed draws, weak Ax, or nuts like sets/flushes). Betting as a bluff risks bloating the pot OOP with 16k effective stacks, facing easy folds from air but raises from value. Checking realizes equity passively, takes free river card (possible runner-runner outs), and exploits position by inducing bluffs or check-behinds from weak hands. Consistent with deep-stack play: pot control with marginal nothing on dangerous board.

I have bottom pair (5♢5 with an 8 kicker) on a very wet, dangerous board: Q♥ J♥ 5♥ 2♦ — there are already three hearts on the flop so many hands in Elon's range (any heart, Q, J, A/Kx) beat me. I was the preflop aggressor but both checked the flop and Elon checked the turn, so I should not bloat the pot with a marginal hand and poor kicker. Checking preserves stack, lets me realize equity, and keeps weaker hands and bluffs in his checking/ betting range. Plan: check here; call a small probe (≈≤1/3 pot ≈200) or a moderate bet (up to ~1/2 pot ≈300) to see showdown, but fold to large turn bets or check‑raises. If he checks back we go to the river and re-evaluate for thin value on safe runouts or fold to sizable aggression on dangerous runouts (a heart or overcard).

River action OOP heads-up with Td4d on a terrifying board: 5h-Qh-Jh-2d-6h (four hearts, paired low cards possible, flushes dominant). My hand is pure air—no pair, no flush, no straight (gutshot missed), just ten-high with irrelevant backdoor diamond draw. Villain checked flop and turn behind on the monotone heart flop, capping her range to either complete misses (draws bricked), weak showdown hands (Ax, underpairs unlikely here), or rare monsters (sets, two-pair trips unlikely without board pairing strongly). Pot control is paramount with 16k effective stacks: betting as a bluff risks 600 into 600 (or more) facing hero-calls from weak hands or raises from nuts, poor given my zero equity/showdown. Checking induces bluffs from her air, takes free showdown if she checks (villain checked twice, likely weak), and preserves stack for better spots in this long tournament (56/150 hands). Folding equity from a bet is mediocre against a checking range; passive play exploits position and board texture optimally.

Check. I have bottom pair (5♦) with a weak 8 kicker on a very wet, three‑heart board (Qh Jh 5h 2d 6h). Elon has been passive (checked flop/turn/river), which makes strong hands and showdown value (Qx, Jx, any heart, or better) more likely and reduces the frequency of river bluffs that would call a value bet. Betting risks getting called by hands that beat me and folding out only the exact air hands I beat. Checking preserves my stack and takes the cheapest line to showdown; plan is to call a small river bet if he suddenly fires (~≤200) but fold to large river aggression (>~300).

Heads-up on the button/SB with K7o, a playable hand with king high and backdoor potential. Standard to raise wide from this position pre-flop to steal blinds or build pot in position. Minni defends BB frequently based on history but folds to aggression at times. Raise to 3x BB (300 total) for value and fold equity, avoiding limping which plays poorly OOP if checked to.

Heads-up in the big blind vs a 3x open to 300 from the button. I hold J♦ T♠ — an offsuit connected hand with good playability heads-up and plenty of postflop equity against a wide opening range. Pot is 400 and it costs me 200 to call; calling 200 to win ~600 after my call gives ~3:1 pot odds (break-even equity ~33%). Versus a typical button open here my hand's equity (~40%+) and postflop playability make a flat call the +EV line. A min‑3bet to 500 would be tiny and gains little; a larger 3bet is too committal with a marginal hand. Plan: see the flop, realize equity, and either continue aggressively on favorable flops (pair/draws) or exercise pot control and fold to heavy aggression on dry boards. I will call.

Heads‑up vs a wide button opening I flatted preflop to see a flop and I hit top pair. Board (5♠ J♠ Q♥) is relatively dry with only two spades and no immediate straight draw for me, so my J♦T♠ is a clear continue — good showdown value and worth protecting. Betting gets value from worse Jx, many underpairs and draws, and charges any Qx or Kx equity; checking just gives free cards and misses value. I'll size to ~2/3 pot to deny correct odds to draws and extract value from weaker hands while still keeping the pot manageable if raised. If Elon calls I’ll re‑evaluate on the turn; a large raise from him will force a careful fold/call decision depending on sizing and reads.

Preflop raise with K7o from button was standard for position and fold equity. Flop Qh Js 5s misses me completely - just king high overcard with no draws. Minni's OOP donk-bet of 400 into 1000 pot reps a polarized range: strong made hands like top pair (QJ, QTs, AJs) or draws (spades like KsTs, AsXs; straights like KT, T9).
