My hand has minimal showdown value, no fold equity on raise, and calling builds pot OOP to worse boards. Deep stacks but folding weak ace-high equivalent here preserves stack for better spots in heads-up play. History shows Minni defends wide but aggresses on connected boards.

Heads-up on the button with J♦8♣ is a hand worth putting pressure on preflop. I have the positional edge and a wide opening range in HU, so I want initiative rather than a limp/call. I'll raise to 300 (3x BB) — a standard, constructive size that maximizes fold equity while keeping the pot controllable with deep stacks. 300 is total committed (I add 250 more to the pot); calling 50 (100 total) would concede initiative. If Elon 3-bets large I'll tighten up and re-evaluate; if he calls I can play postflop in position.

Heads-up pre-flop from the BB facing a button min-raise to 300 (200 to call into 400 pot, getting over 2:1 odds). 74d is suited with straight and flush potential, playable in position heads-up with deep stacks (23k effective ~166x BB). Minni raises frequently from button per history, wide range. Defend wide OOP heads-up; can realize equity post-flop by check-raising strong hands or check-folding weak boards. Calling maintains pot control and balances range.

Heads-up pot on a low, uncoordinated flop (2d Th 3c) with 600 in pot and deep effective stacks (~16k). OOP with 74d suited, holding a live diamond flush draw (9 outs) plus backdoor straight potential (e.g., running diamonds or fillers). Board favors my wide defending range including wheel draws (A5, 45), gutshots, and weak pairs. Minni's BTN min-raise range is very wide (per history of frequent button aggression). Standard OOP play: check to control pot size, induce c-bets from her wide range where I have ~35-40% equity on average (flush draw realizes well). Can check-call small/medium bets or check-fold if oversized. Donk betting polarizes too much OOP and risks folding out better; checking balances value and bluffs post-flop. Pot control and multi-way realization over 150-hand tournament.

I was the preflop raiser and have the positional advantage heads‑up. The flop 2♦‑10♥‑3♣ is very dry and misses a big chunk of the BB’s calling range, so a continuation bet will pick up a lot of pots and extract equity when called. My J♦8♣ has one overcard (J) and a backdoor club — not strong, but a good candidate for a standard c‑bet. I’ll bet ~1/2 pot to apply pressure while keeping the pot controllable: 300 into a 600 pot. If Elon folds we take it down; if he calls I can continue with any improvement or re‑assess on a blank turn; if he raises big I’ll usually fold J8 as it has poor showdown value against strong aggression.

Heads-up pot on 2d Th 3c flop, low and uncoordinated, favoring my wide BB defending range including wheel draws, pairs, and suited connectors/gutshots. Facing Minni's small 300 bet into 900 (1/3 pot) from her wide button opening range (frequent aggression per history). 74d holds a strong diamond flush draw (9 outs, ~35% equity vs random), backdoor straight/flush potential, and overcards somewhat. Pot odds excellent (300 into 1200, 4:1). OOP, calling small bet realizes equity well: see turn cheaply, hit flush and stack off, or fold to turn aggression if missed. Raising risks bloating pot OOP with draw or folding out her weak c-bets; check-raise too polarized early. Minni's sizing small suggests probing wide range, so calling balances my range (value like sets/pairs calling too). Deep stacks (~16k effective), multi-street play over tournament.

Heads-up on turn with 1200 pot, deep effective stacks (~16k). Board 2d Th 3c 3s pairs the trey, low paired board favoring small-ball and draws. OOP with 74dd flush draw (9 diamond outs ~19% to river), minimal straight potential, overcards. Flop saw check-call small 1/3 pot bet from Minni's wide button range (frequent aggression). Turn pairs deuces her straight draws (e.g., 45,A5) and gives trips to 33. Standard OOP: check to control pot, induce from her wide continuation range where equity realizes well (flush draw OOP but multi-street value). Donk betting polarizes (nuts like sets/draws) and risks folding out her weak Ax, pairs, bluffs; check balances value (trips? unlikely) and air/draws. If she bets small/medium, call for odds; fold oversized. Tournament stage, preserve stack, exploit her aggression post-check.

I c-bet the dry 2♦‑10♥‑3♣ flop and Elon just called, showing a fairly wide but often pair-heavy/calling range (10x, pocket pairs, occasional float). The turn pares the board (3♠) which actually helps many of his calling hands and reduces my fold equity — I have only J♦8♣ (two low overcards to a 10) with ~6% to hit a J and ~6% to hit an 8 on the river (~12–13% combined), and even if I pair I’m often behind. Betting now would be a thin bluff against a calling range that frequently continues, and getting raised would put me in a losing, bloated pot. In position I should control the pot and see a free river where I can make a cleaner decision. I’ll check and re-evaluate on the river (look to fold to meaningful aggression, consider a small bluff if he checks again and the river is blank, or value-bet only if I improve).

River OOP heads-up, pot 1200, deep stacks (~16k effective). Board 2d Th 3c 3s 6h: paired low board favoring made hands like trips (33), straights (45), two-pair (T3/23/36). Held 74dd: missed flush draw completely (no diamond turn/river), no pair, no straight (4 gives gutshot to 5 but irrelevant). Pure 7-high air after check-calling flop small bet (as draw) and check-check turn. Flop check-call + turn check-check represents showdown value (weak pairs, stubborn draws, wheel blocks) or traps, but betting air OOP river is -EV: low fold equity vs Minni's aggressive button range (history of value-betting thin/river aggression but checked turn suggesting weakness), risks calling stations. Standard OOP river play with air: check-fold to any reasonable bet, preserving stack for better spots in 150-hand tournament. No value, no strong semi-bluff incentive. Check maintains range balance without commitment.

I c-bet the dry 2♦-T♥-3♣ flop with J♦8♣, got called, then checked the turn. The river (6♥) is a blank for both of us and Elon checks — a line consistent with a lot of marginal pairs (10x, small pocket pairs) or missed overcards/floats. I have virtually no showdown value (jack-high) so checking simply lets him take the pot when he has a pair; a small, well-sized river bluff here can profitably fold out many of his marginal hands. The pot is 1,200 — a bet of 400 (~1/3 pot) applies pressure while keeping risk limited. Rough math: against a realistic calling range my equity is very low, but with only ~15–25% fold frequency this size is +EV (with even modest showdown equity the required fold rate is lower). Plan: bet 400 to try to take it down; if he raises I’ll fold this river bluff (no showdown value).

River OOP heads-up, pot 1600 facing 400 bet (~1/4 pot) from Minni's wide button range. Board 2d Th 3c 3s 6h pairs treys, favors trips (33), two-pair (T3/23/36/T6), sets (22/TT), or stubborn pairs/draws. Held 74dd: pure 7-high air, missed diamond flush draw (no D turn/river), no pair/straight. Preflop call + flop check-call small bet + turn check-check as draw was standard for equity realization (~35% flop equity), but river miss leaves zero showdown value or draw. Minni's small flop c-bet, turn check (weakness?), small river bet suggests thin value (mid pairs like 77-JJ, T9, weak trips) or probe; fold equity low OOP vs her aggression history, but calling 20% odds needs ~ her range <7-high (unlikely, <10% bluffs given sizing). Folding preserves 22.5k stack for better spots in 150-hand tournament vs her 15.9k; balances range after check-call history (didn't rep nuts always). No semi-bluff value, pure fold.

Heads-up from the button (SB), preflop with pot of 150 (blinds posted). Ah2c is a standard open-raise hand in this spot due to the ace blocker, high card strength, and position. Wide opening range ~50-70% of hands includes A2o. Raising 3x the BB (300 total) applies pressure on Minni's BB, steals dead money frequently, and allows aggressive postflop play in position if called. Minni defends wide but folds some raises. Folding or limping is suboptimal here.

I’m in the big blind with Td6h facing Elon’s raise to 300 (pot 400, 200 to call). Heads-up the button’s opening range is wide and with deep stacks (~174bb) I want to defend fairly liberally. Td6h is marginal but playable; 3-betting out of position with this hand is suboptimal and a tiny raise to 500 just bloats the pot. Folding is too tight here against a frequent opener. I’ll call (commit total 300) to see a flop and proceed cautiously — continue with top pair, decent draws or favorable turn cards; concede to heavy aggression on dry, face-card heavy boards or a large 3-bet/shove.

Heads‑up vs an early opener with a wide range, my preflop call was fine and the flop gives me top pair (T♦) with a weak kicker. Pot is 600 and there’s no bet, so I want to both extract value from worse pairs/weak tens and deny cheap equity to any backdoor or overcard draws. A half‑pot bet (~300) is the right balance — big enough to charge draws and get value from worse hands, small enough to allow me to call a reasonable raise and preserve stack for later streets. Plan: bet 300; if called I’ll re-evaluate on the turn (protect/continue on safe cards, be cautious to heavy action if overcards or 9/4 improve the villian), if I face a large check‑raise or shove I’ll give credit to two‑pair/sets and fold or proceed carefully depending on sizing.

Preflop raise with Ah2c from button/SB was standard to steal blinds and play in position. Flop 9cTs4d is dry, rainbow, with a straight draw possible (J8, QJ etc.) but mostly favors Minni's BB defending range which includes pairs, suited connectors, and broadways she flats pre. My hand has zero equity: no pair, no flush draw (2c but board 9c, ace hearts blocks nothing relevant), no straight draw (gutshot would need specific like 3 for wheel but no). Ace-high has poor showdown value. Minni's 300 bet (1/3 pot) looks like standard continuation or thin value, and calling commits us to tough river spots OOP with worse. Raising has fold equity but risks spew vs her calling station tendencies seen in prior hands (frequently calls big bets). Deep stacks (22k/16k eff), but folding marginal ace-high to BB lead preserves stack for better spots. Long-term EV positive to fold here.

Heads-up on the button with QcTd (offsuit). This is a strong heads-up hand and I want to seize initiative and build the pot with position. Calling 50 is passive and lets the BB see a cheap flop; a 3xBB open to 300 narrows Elon's defense range, gives me fold equity, and keeps postflop decision-making in position with deep stacks (~17.8k effective). I’m prepared to continuation-bet most non-wet flops and to proceed cautiously if Elon 3-bets big (I’ll fold to heavy 3-bets or re-evaluate if the 3-bet is small enough to call for favorable pot odds). Raise to 300 to open the hand aggressively and control the action.

Preflop in the big blind facing a standard 3x open from Minni on the button (SB position in heads-up). My hand is K6o, which is a decent king-high hand with good blocker value against her range. Minni has been aggressive with frequent preflop raises across the history, often opening wide from late position. Pot odds are excellent: calling 200 into a 400 pot for immediate odds of 3:1, and postflop SPR will be deep (~30bb effective after). Equity realization against her wide SB range (any two, ~50%+ of hands) is strong enough to defend profitably rather than folding. No reason to raise this marginal hand without position postflop. Folding is too tight given the price.

On the flop of 6s-7c-9c, I have paired my 6c with the board's 6s for top pair on this board (sixes with king kicker) plus a strong club flush draw (holding the 6c with 7c and 9c on board). This is a very strong hand in a heads-up pot against Minni's wide button opening range. The board is drawy with flush and straight draws possible (e.g., 5-8, 10-8, T-8, flush draws), so betting protects my hand and extracts value from overcards, weaker pairs, and draws she might float with. Checking risks giving free cards to her aggression or draws. As first to act (BB postflop), a standard 2/3 pot bet of 400 builds the pot while keeping SPR manageable (~40:1 post-bet) for multi-street play. Minni's history shows frequent button aggression and calling stations in spots, so value betting thin is profitable. No fold or check here; this hand crushes her range.

Flop 6s-7c-9c is fairly connected and includes two clubs. I have Qc Td — no pair, but a gutshot to an 8 (4 outs) plus 3 queens and 3 tens = ~10 outs to improve to at least a pair/straight by river (roughly ~40% equity to the river). Elon bet 400 into a 1000 pot; calling 400 would make the final pot 1800, so I only need ~22% equity to justify a call. In position I can realize my equity, keep the pot manageable, and gather info for the turn. A raise here would be a thin bluff on a board that hits many hands in his defending range (pockets, 9x/7x, club draws), so I prefer to call and re-evaluate on the turn (if I pair or complete the straight I’ll apply pressure; if I miss and face further aggression I can fold).

On the turn, the board is 6s-7c-9c-5c, giving me bottom pair (6s-6c) with a strong king kicker and a powerful club flush draw (6c with three clubs on board: 7c,9c,5c). This is an extremely strong combination in a heads-up pot OOP against Minni's wide button calling range, which includes overcards, weaker pairs, straight/flush draws, and floats. The turn 5c completes some gutshot straight draws (e.g., 8x) and increases flush draw pressure, but my hand blocks low clubs and has excellent equity realization (~70%+ vs. her range). Minni called my flop c-bet of 400 into ~700, showing some strength or draw, but her history indicates she's a calling station who pays off value bets frequently (e.g., Hands 33,39,44,49,50). Checking risks giving a free card to her draws or overcards completing, and pot control isn't necessary with this monster. Betting ~70% pot (1000) builds the pot for value from Ax, Tx, 8x draws, weaker pairs (77,99? unlikely), and any two clubs while keeping SPR manageable (~10:1 post-bet with deep stacks). This maximizes EV by extracting from her loose range while protecting against runner-runner outs. No reason to check or slowplay given board texture and villain tendencies.

The turn bet is 1000 into a 2400 pot. Calling 1000 to compete for a 3400 final pot requires ~29% equity. I have QcTd on 6s-7c-9c-5c. My clean count: any club (9 cards), non-club Qs (3), non-club Tens (2) and non-club 8s (3) = 17 outs ≈ 17/46 ≈ 37% to improve by the river. That comfortably beats the pot odds, so a call is +EV. Some outs are “dirty” (a club may still be dominated if Elon holds two clubs), and Elon’s line (call preflop, bet flop, continue on turn) shows real strength, so I don’t want to bloat the pot with just a draw by raising to 2000. In position I can realize my equity, see the river, and then decide: if I make the flush/straight I will extract value; if I pair Q/T I’ll choose sizing based on river texture; if I miss and face heavy river aggression I’ll fold. Call 1000.

River 10h is a relatively blank card that doesn't complete obvious new draws beyond what's already possible, but the board is extremely coordinated with four clubs (5c-7c-9c and my 6c blocker), straight possibilities like 8x for 5-6-7-8-9 (which villain could have called with on turn), two-pair combos (e.g., 65s, 67s, 95s clubs), sets (55,66 unlikely since I have it, 77,99), or any club flush. My holding is exactly one pair (6s) with K-high kicker, giving showdown value against pure air, missed draws (e.g., spade flush draw chasing 68s/T8s), or jack/high bluffs, but I'm crushed by her likely calling range after two IP calls on this draw-heavy board: any improved club, 8x straights, 7x/9x pairs, T-high pairs. Minni's history shows she's a calling station (e.g., Hands 33,39,44,49,50,52 where she calls big bets with marginal holdings), but also folds to river aggression sometimes (e.g., 51,52,55). OOP first to act into a 3400 pot with deep stacks, betting thin value risks getting raised/folded out by better (low EV), while checking induces bluffs from her wide missed draw range, allows pot control, and retains the option to call a reasonable bet if she values thin or bluffs (my K blocker helps vs. overcard floats). No strong value range to bomb here; check maximizes EV by balancing showdown and bluff-catching.

I improved to a pair of Tens on the river (Td + Th on board). Pot is 3400 and Elon checked after betting the flop and turn — that check can be either pot control with a marginal made hand or a missed/medium-strength holding trying to see a free showdown. My hand loses to straights (any 8), turned/rivered two-card club flushes (villain would need two clubs in hand) and some two-pair/full-house combos, but it beats a large portion of Elon’s checking range (weaker pairs like 9x/7x/6x, many missed draws and some K/Q/J high hands). I want to extract thin value while keeping the price low to get calls from worse hands and avoid bloating the pot where I’m behind. A small value bet (~1/3–1/2 pot) is appropriate: I’ll bet 1200 chips to get called by worse and fold out pure air. If Elon check-raises big I’ll fold (that line strongly represents a made straight/flush/two-pair), but if he calls small I expect to have the best hand often enough to make this +EV.

Facing a 1/3 pot river bet of 1200 into 4600 after calling my flop and turn value bets on a highly coordinated board (6s7c9c5cTh), Minni's range is polarized: value hands like 8x straights (e.g., 8c blocking my flush block), club flushes (but I block 6c and board has three clubs), top pairs (T9, T7 unlikely given calls), mid pairs improved (77,99 sets unlikely). Bluffs include missed club draws without 6c (e.g., AcXc, JcXc), missed straight draws (48s), or pure floats turning into bets. Her history as a calling station (Hands 33,39,44,49,50 calling large bets with showdown value) but also river aggression when checked to suggests thin value or bluffs here. My pair of 6s with K kicker has excellent showdown value against her bluffing range (overcards, missed spades like 68s/T8s), blocks some of her value (Kx clubs?), and pot odds are massive (1200 to win 5800, needing ~17% equity; I realize ~30-40% vs. her overall range including bluffs). Raising overplays one pair OOP deep-stacked; folding is nitty given the spot we constructed by checking to induce. Calling balances bluff-catching with pot control, exploits her loose tendencies, and maximizes EV long-term.

Heads-up on the button/SB with T5o, a marginal hand with some straight potential but offsuit and weak. Stacks are deep (~190BB), pot small at 150, only 50 more to call to complete the blind for a cheap flop. Minni in BB defends wide based on history. Limping keeps the pot small, realizes equity post-flop where I act last on later streets, and avoids folding equity battle with a weak hand. Folding too tight pre-flop in heads-up loses long-term EV; raising is suboptimal without better blockers or playability. Plan to play aggressively post-flop if hits or as bluff.

Heads-up in the big blind with 8♦2♠ — essentially the worst hand. Elon just limped (called 50) from the small blind and the pot is already 200; there’s no price to see a flop. With deep stacks (~200bb) and a very weak holding, the correct default is to check and see the flop rather than invest more preflop or attempt a bluff-raise with garbage. Checking preserves stack, gains information, and allows me to continue only with a pair, a reasonable draw, or a favorable texture; I’ll fold to significant aggression on dry flops unless I improve.

I have middle pair (8♦2♠) on Q♥ 8♠ T♣. Elon limped preflop so his range is wide (many missed broadways, small pairs, some Qx/TX, occasional 9J straights). Betting here extracts value from worse pairs and many overcards that will fold, while also protecting my hand against turn cards that give free equity to draws. I'll make a small, half‑pot c‑bet (100) to keep the pot manageable if I get raised — plan to fold to a large, polarized raise but continue cautiously if called (re-evaluate on the turn).

Middle pair with tens (Td pairs Tc), weak 5 kicker on Qh8sTc flop. Backdoor spade draw with 5s. Deep stacks (~192BB), acting last in position vs Minni's small 100 bet into ~200 pot (~50% pot). Minni defends wide preflop and bets flops frequently in history, range includes draws (J9, 97s for gutshots/straights), weaker pairs, bluffs. Folding loses too much EV; raising risks bloating pot OOP with marginal hand vulnerable to Qx, sets, better T. Calling cheap realizes equity, maintains pot control, sets up turns where I can bluff or value thin if improves (e.g., any T, runner-runner str8/flush). Plan: Call flop, evaluate turn based on texture and action.

I’m on a blank-ish turn (Qh 8s Tc 7h) with only middle pair (8♦2♠). I barreled the flop and Elon called, so his range includes Qx/Tx, tens, some 8x, and occasional made straights (9J) or draws. The turn 7 is mostly a brick vs that range — checking is the prudent, default play to control the pot and avoid committing chips with a vulnerable one-pair hand. By checking I keep the pot manageable, allow bluffs to be induced on the river, and can call a small-to-medium bet for showdown value while folding to a large, polarized bet. If Elon checks back I can re-evaluate the river for a small value bet or a check‑back to showdown. Plan on calling modest bets (roughly ≤ the pot) but folding to big, polarized pressure.

Turn board 7h completes some straight draws (J9 now open-ender to Broadway) and adds second heart for flush draws, increasing action potential. I have middle pair tens with poor kicker (T5sds backdoor), no draw, but strong position post-oop check from Minni. Her flop c-bet small into small pot suggests wide range: Qx, 8x, Tx (worse kickers like T9/T7/T6), gutshots (J9/97), missed draws (KJs, A9s), bluffs. Checking turn after small flop bet often indicates weakness, pot control, or giving up on draws (now more scary). Deep stacks (190+BB) favor aggressive play in position to deny equity/steal. Checking risks giving free river to draws/improvers and missing value from Ax floats/worse pairs. Small bet (~50-60% pot, 200-250) targets folds from air/draws, thin values worse Tx/8x, builds pot if she has showdown value (she calls flops wide but folds turns often in history). Avoids overbetting marginal hand vulnerable to raises from Qx/sets. Plan: bet small, c/r raise if she shoves/jam unlikely.

I have middle pair (8♦2♠). I c‑bet the flop for value/protection and Elon called, so his range includes Qx, Tx, some 8x, occasional made straights (9‑J) and bluffs. The 7♥ on the turn is a blank; he now bets 225 — a modest (~half‑pot) size that gives me roughly 36% pot‑odds to call. Folding here would be too nitty because I’m ahead of many of his missed broadway hands and bluffs; raising is unattractive because he called the flop and is likely to continue with many hands that beat or have good equity vs a raise. I’ll call the 225 to control the pot, realize equity, and re‑evaluate on the river (plan to fold to large, polarized aggression but extract value or check‑back depending on river action).

River (Qh 8s Tc 7h Ah). I have second pair (8♢2♠). Elon checked the river after calling my flop bet and betting the turn — his line contains Qx/Tx/8x, some A‑x, occasional made straights or two‑heart combos, and bluffs. The A on the river realistically improves a lot of his range (Ax now beats me) and many of his checking hands will simply call a small bet for value; the hands I beat (small pocket pairs, missed overcards) are the ones most likely to fold. A thin value bet here would get called too often by better hands and won’t fold enough worse hands to be +EV. With deep stacks and only a medium pot (850), the prudent line is to control the pot and realize my equity — check to showdown rather than bloat the pot or bluff into a range that dominates me.

River Ah completes heart flushes (Qh-7h-Ah possible nuts) and improves Ax to top pair or better. Board: Qh-8s-Tc-7h-Ah; straights like J9 intact. My Td5s = middle pair Tens (weak 5 kicker), no draw/improver. Minni checked turn (post small flop bet), called my 225 (~64% pot), now checks river again vs my positional bet/call preference. Her range post-turn call/check: Qx (top pair), weaker Tx/8x, 87/77/TT sets (unlikely), J9 straight, missed draws turning into floats (KJ,97), some hearts. Checking river suggests caution (ace scares Qx/Ax), pot control, or weakness vs traps unlikely in BB defender spot. Deep stacks (~190BB); betting risks raises from better (Qx/J9/flushes), thin value from 8x/A-high unlikely after turn call. Small bet (half pot ~400-500) vulnerable to calls from superiors, bluffs blocked poorly (no A/h). Checking realizes SD value vs bluffs/missed draws/A-high/weak pairs I beat, controls pot OOP check history, avoids cooler variance. Tournament stage favors +EV showdown over marginal hero calls post-miss; history shows Minni check-calls stations but folds turns/rivers to aggression when weak. Plan: check-behind, expect frequent wins.

I'm on the button/small blind with K♥8♠ — a playable, reasonably strong heads-up hand. Pot is 150 and it's only 50 to call. I want to seize initiative and play the hand in position, so I open-raise to a standard size (3x the big blind area) to build the pot and apply pressure. Raise to 300 is a conventional sizing that folds out weaker hands and keeps my range wide. Plan: if Elon 3-bets large I'll fold K8s and preserve my stack; if he calls I’ll proceed postflop in position — c-betting on dry flops, pot-control or check-call on dangerous runouts, and extract value when I hit top pair or strong draws.

Heads-up against Minni's button raise to 300 (3x BB). My J9s is a strong speculative hand with excellent playability: suited connector potential for straights, flushes, and two overs. Effective stacks are deep (~200BB), favoring calling wide from BB vs button open range, which is wide. Folding is too tight; raising OOP risks too much with a hand that wants multiway pots or set-mining but plays well post-flop here. Equity realization good heads-up. Will call and play pot in position post-flop? No, OOP but closing preflop. Still, standard defend.

OOP on A-Q-2 rainbow-ish flop (two hearts) with J♠9♦ offsuit. Complete air: no pair, no straight draw (gutshot? No, J9 needs T for straight but board A Q 2 doesn't help), no flush draw. Equity realization poor OOP against Minni's wide button opening range, which includes many Ax, Qx, draws, pairs. Her position allows wide c-betting. Deep stacks but no reason to bluff here with worst hand. Pot 600, check to pot control and induce bluffs or see cheap river if she checks back. Folding preflop would have been better hindsight, but post-flop standard check-fold to bet.

I was the preflop aggressor and am in position with K♥8♠ on A♣ Q♥ 2♥. I have no pair — just a heart blocker and a weak backdoor — but heads-up my range advantage means a continuation bet will fold a lot of missed hands (Jx, Tx, small pairs, air) and keep the initiative. I’ll bet a half‑pot-sized c-bet (300) to apply pressure while keeping the pot manageable if called. Plan: fold to a large raise (I have no showdown value), and if called proceed cautiously on the turn (check/call or check/fold depending on card and action).

OOP on Ac Qh 2h flop with Js9d: complete air, no pair, no straight draw (gutshot at best but unlikely), no flush draw. Minni's button c-bet of 300 into 900 (1/3 pot) is standard with her wide range including Ax, Qx, pairs, heart draws. My preflop call with J9o was loose, but now folding to avoid realizing equity poorly OOP deep-stacked. No fold equity on raise OOP vs aggressive Minni; calling bloats pot OOP with nothing. Pot odds ~3:1 but reverse implied odds terrible. Standard fold to preserve stack for better spots.

Heads-up on the button (SB) with deep stacks (~190BB effective). 5c2c is a suited connector with decent implied odds and playability for flush draws, straight draws, or two-pair potential. In position, raising wide is standard to apply pressure, steal the BB, or play post-flop profitably with initiative. Minni defends wide but folding pre-flop SB is too tight. Standard open-raise size of 3x BB (300 total).

I’m on the big blind with 8♣5♠ facing a button open to 300 (pot 400, I must call 200 to reach 300). Calling gives me ~2:1 pot odds (I need ~33% equity), but 8‑5 offsuit is a weak, disconnected hand that plays poorly out of position against a button open range. A min‑raise to 500 would be a pure bluff without good blockers and could get me into larger, difficult spots versus a capable opponent. With deep stacks and many hands left in the tournament I’ll avoid marginal, OOP spots and fold this hand.

Heads-up on the button with A♣2♦ — this is a strong open-raising hand. I have position postflop and a lot of fold equity versus the big blind, so I should be aggressive rather than limp/call. A standard 3xBB to 300 builds the pot when I'm likely ahead of many of Elon’s calling hands, preserves fold equity, and keeps the initiative. If he 3-bets big I can fold or re-evaluate based on sizing; if he calls I’ll use position to c-bet favorable flops and get value from worse aces and broadways. Total commitment shown is 300 chips.

Heads-up in BB vs button raise. 42o is a complete trash hand with no high card value, poor connectivity, offsuit, and terrible playability out of position. Minni is aggressive and raises wide from button/SB based on hand history. Pot odds are 3:1 but equity and postflop prospects are dismal. Folding preserves stack for better spots in deepstack HU play.

Heads-up preflop from SB/Button with deep stacks (~190bb). 6c3c is a suited gapped connector with decent playability and blockers. Standard to raise wide from SB vs BB to steal blinds or play postflop in position. Minni has shown aggression but also folds to raises (e.g., recent hands). Raising to 3x BB (300 total) applies pressure, builds pot if called, and maintains initiative. Folding or limping wastes position/equity.

Elon opened to 300 from the button/SB. I’m in the big blind with 9♥7♣ (offsuit) — marginal but defensible heads-up. With both stacks ~200bb deep and the open only 3bb, calling is the lowest-variance, +EV play: the price to see a flop is cheap and I can realize equity without bloating a pot out of position. 9-7o is a poor 3-bet target (offsuit, disconnected) so I’ll avoid inflating the pot OOP; plan is to play cautiously on the flop (check/fold dry boards, continue with pairs/strong draws). Action: call the raise to 300 (i.e. put 200 more).

I have 9♥7♣ on K♦7♠2♠ — middle pair with a decent kicker heads-up. Elon opened wide from the button preflop, so his range contains lots of Kx/top-pair combos but also many missed overcards. I’m out of position and stacks are very deep relative to the tiny pot (600), so I want to keep the pot controlled with a one-pair hand. Checking achieves that: it lets worse hands and bluffs barrel into me (which I can call) while avoiding bloating the pot OOP against strong Kx. Plan: check, call a standard small-to-medium continuation bet and re-evaluate on the turn; fold to large overbets/raise lines that polarize him to two-pair/sets or strong value hands.

Heads-up on the flop with position, preflop initiative as the raiser. Flop is Kd7s2s, which is somewhat dry but with a spade flush draw. My 6c3c has no pair, no strong draw (backdoor club flush and straight possibilities), but excellent playability and fold equity against Minni's BB calling range, which includes many hands missing this board (no king or 7, weak spade draws). Minni checked, indicating weakness or trapping, but standard to c-bet ~50% pot to deny equity, build pot when ahead (overcards/live cards), and apply pressure deep-stacked. Bet 300 maintains sizing discipline, gets value from weaker hands/draws, and pot control if raised. Folding or checking away equity wastes position/range advantage. History shows Minni calls light but folds to aggression postflop often (e.g., recent folds).

I have 9♥7♣ on K♦7♠2♠ — middle pair with a decent kicker heads‑up. Elon opened wide preflop and his 300 into the ~600 pot is a standard small/standard c‑bet that will contain a lot of missed overcards and bluffs as well as Kx. I’m out of position and stacks are very deep, so pot control is important: calling keeps worse hands and bluffs in, realizes equity, and avoids bloating the pot OOP with a marginal hand. Call 300 now and re‑evaluate on the turn: call small/medium bets, fold to large overbets or raises that polarize to two‑pair/sets, and only start building the pot if I pick up clear additional equity (trip 7/9 or a strong read).

I have 9♥7♣ on 2♠ K♦ 7♠ 5♣ — a middle pair with a reasonable kicker heads‑up. Elon opened wide preflop and c‑bet a small amount on the flop, so his range contains lots of Kx and many bluffs/missed overcards. The 5♣ is mostly a blank; it doesn’t improve typical c‑bet bluffs but does give a few two‑pair/5x combos. I’m out of position and both stacks are very deep relative to the pot (1200), so pot control is priority with this marginal made hand. Checking keeps worse hands and bluffs in, avoids bloating the pot OOP, and lets me see his plan — I can call small/medium bets and fold to large, polarized overbets. If he checks back I’ll re‑evaluate the river and look for a thin value bet on safe cards or take showdown. Action: check.

Heads-up on turn with position and initiative from preflop raise and flop c-bet. Board Kd7s2s5c is draw-heavy (spade flush draw, gutshots like 34/46/89 for straights). My 6c3c has a gutshot to 4 (2-3-4-5-6 straight, 4 outs), backdoor club flush draw, and blocks some straight draws/sets. Minni's twice-checking range is weak: missed draws, weak pairs, unlikely strong hands (would bet for value/protection). Deep stacks (~190bb), betting ~1/2 pot (600 into 1200) continues my aggressive range, denies equity to her draws/improvers, exploits fold equity (Minni folds to postflop aggression frequently per history, e.g., recent folds on turns/rivers), and gets called by worse (e.g., 7x, small pairs). Checking risks free river card where she improves or bluffs; betting balances range with value hands like Kx/sets. Favorable vs her calling range.

I have 9♥7♣ on 2♠ K♦ 7♠ 5♣ — middle pair heads‑up. Elon opened preflop and c‑bet the flop, and his 600 turn into the ~1800 pot is a moderate continuation bet (~25% pot odds to call). Against his wide opening/c‑betting range I am ahead of a lot of missed overcards and bluffs and behind Kx/top pair — with deep stacks pot control OOP is important. Raising here would polarize and commit a lot of chips with a marginal hand, so the lowest‑variance, +EV line is to call and keep worse hands and bluffs in. I will call 600 and re‑evaluate on the river: call small/medium barrels, fold to a large polarized raise, and only start building the pot with a clear improvement or strong read.

I hold 7♣9♥ — only middle pair on 2♠ K♦ 7♠ 5♣ 5♥. The river paired the board, which creates many full‑house/quads combinations (pocket 5s, 7‑5 combos, pocket 77, K5, etc.) that now beat me. Elon has been barreling with a wide range, but betting here would polarize the pot and force me to commit chips with a marginal one‑pair hand that has very little value and lots of ways to be crushed. With deep effective stacks relative to this small pot (≈2.4k) I prefer pot control OOP and a showdown. I check to avoid bloating the pot; if Elon now bets a small amount I can call for pot odds against bluffs/overcards, but I will fold to a large polarized river raise/shove.

River pairs the 5s, which completes some unlikely straight draws (e.g., 34 for gutshot) but bricks the spade flush draw and doesn't heavily favor calls. Minni has checked flop, turn, and river, indicating a capped weak range: likely weak Kx (KJo-QJ), 77 (overpair turned two pair but passive), mid pocket pairs (88-JJ missing), or stubborn Ax/draws that whiffed further improvement. My range as preflop raiser/c-betting flop/turning continuation is polarized: strong value (Kx top pair+, sets, straights) or bluffs (suited connectors/gutshots like 6c3c with gutshot blocker to 34 straight draw). Deep stacks (~180bb), but history shows Minni calls light postflop but folds rivers to aggression (e.g., Hand 52 massive fold, Hand 54 fold, Hand 55 fold, etc.). Betting ~50% pot (1200 into 2400) exploits her passivity/fold equity, gets value from stubborn Ax/weak pairs, balances with value bets (K7/K5/two pairs), and denies her cheap showdown. Checking risks her hero-calling any pair or improving to check-behind. Favorable bluff spot to continue aggression and maximize stack by stealing pots vs passive BB.

I hold 7♣9♥ on 2♠ K♦ 7♠ 5♣ 5♥ — my best hand is two pair (7s & 5s). Elon has been barreling a wide button range (small c‑bet on the flop, another small bet on the turn) and now fires a small 1,200 into a 3,600 pot. Calling 1,200 risks 1,200 to win a final pot of 6,000, so I only need ~20% equity to make a call break‑even. Yes, the river pair brings a lot of hands that now beat me (any 5, pocket 7s, many Kx two‑pair combinations), but his line and tiny sizing also allow a healthy number of bluffs / missed overcards and weaker holdings I beat. Raising would polarize the pot out of position and commit chips with a marginal hand that is frequently behind, so it’s not attractive. Given the pot odds and his wide barreling range, the +EV, low‑variance line is to call and take it to showdown.

Heads-up on the button (small blind) with K♦4♣. Against a single opponent my opening range should be wide — K4o is marginal but playable in position. Limping gives up initiative and lets the BB see a cheap flop; calling 50 is an option but I prefer to apply pressure and take the lead. I’ll make a standard min-raise to 200 to isolate/take the pot down preflop or play postflop in position. Plan: if Elon folds I win the pot; if he calls I’ll use position to C‑bet favorable flops and proceed cautiously with one-pair or draw holdings; if he 3-bets large I’ll fold K4o (unless the 3-bet is small and I can exploit).

Heads-up with deep stacks (169BB effective). In BB facing a min-raise to 200 (2x) from aggressive SB/Button Minni, who raises frequently preflop based on history. J9o is a profitable defend: excellent pot odds (pot 300, call 100 for 3:1), decent equity (~40%+ vs wide button range), and postflop playability with broadway potential, straight draws, etc. Folding too tight; 3-betting marginal offsuit hand risks playing OOP postflop awkwardly. Call to realize equity and control pot size.

Flopped top set with Jh on Jc Js 4s board – the stone nuts, vulnerable only to runner-runner full house or quads. OOP vs aggressive Button Minni who raised wide preflop and often continues aggressively. Pot 400 deep (~168BB effective), check to induce bluff/raise but risks giving free cards to draws (spade flush draw possible, live 9 for str8). Bet for thin value from overpairs (KK/QQ/TT/AA), weaker Jx, Ax, any two. Size 2/3 pot (~267) rounded to 300 builds pot without scaring off calling range. Maximizes EV by charging draws and extracting from worse.

Flop: Jc Js 4s. I hold K♦4♣ — I have one pair (4s) on a paired J‑J board, which is a weak hand here. Elon’s bet of 300 into a 700 pot gives me ~30% break‑even equity (300/(700+300)). Heads‑up his preflop calling range is wide and includes lots of Jx (which now make trips and crush me), Ax/Kx, small pairs and many missed hands. A raise to 600 would be a pure bluff and is risky: it could fold some marginal hands but will get called or crushed by any Jack/trip hands and commits chips with little equity. Folding is too tight given the cheap price and my showdown equity against his non‑Jack bluffs/weak hands. 

Decision: call 300. This keeps the pot manageable, realizes my equity, and lets me reevaluate on the turn. Plan: if I improve (hit a King or 4) or Elon shows weakness on the turn I can apply pressure; if he barrels large and I don’t improve I’ll fold.

Top set of Jacks remains extremely strong on this board (4s Jc Js 7s), vulnerable mainly to runner-runner full house, quads, or a flush that beat trips (unlikely given flop action). Minni's button raise-call-flop call range includes Jx, overpairs (QQ/TT/AA unlikely to flat so wide pre), Ax, KQ, suited connectors/draws. Turn spade completes some flush draws (spades present since flop: 4s/Js +7s), but many were live preflop and she called small flop bet without raising. Deep stacks (~160BB effective), OOP first to act: betting continues thin value from weaker Jx (AJ/KJ/QJ), pairs, and charges remaining draws (str8 draws like 10x, flush draws without nuts). Checking risks free river card to OESD (10-8/5-10 etc.) or blank that allows her bluff/value bet. Bet 2/3 pot (700 into 1000) builds pot for river value/extraction, denies equity, and fits with flop line. Her history shows calling stations tendencies (frequent calls in pots vs us), so expect calls from worse.

Recap: I hold K♦4♣ and the board is J♣ J♠ 4♠ 7♠. I actually have two pair (Jacks and Fours) on the turn — stronger than my earlier misread. Pot is 1,700 and Elon bet 700; calling 700 gets me to a 2,400 pot (break‑even equity ≈29%).

Range/read: Elon called pre, bet the flop and now barrels the turn — that line certainly contains some Jx (trips) and 7x/full‑house combos that beat me, but it also contains lots of missed broadways and weaker holdings that I currently beat. Counting combos roughly, only a minority of his holdings (J’s, many 7 combos, the one pocket‑4 combo) are currently ahead.

Decision logic: My two pair is ahead of a large portion of his continuing range and my required equity to call is ~29%, so folding would be too nitty. A raise is a thin bluff/value mix that risks getting crushed or re‑raised by trips/quads and will fold out hands I want to keep. Calling controls the pot size, realizes equity now, and lets me re‑evaluate on the river (I have small but real full‑house outs — and if he overbets the river I can fold if I don’t improve).

Action: Call 700 and decide on the river based on his action (value bet if he checks, fold to an overbearing jam if I miss and he represents a Jack strongly).

River 8s brings the fourth spade on board (4s, Js, 7s, 8s), putting a flush out that beats my top set of jacks (Jh with Jc/Js). Any spade in Minni's hand makes a flush, and given her wide button opening range (frequent raiser) and calls on flop (paired board with 2 spades) and turn (3rd spade), her range includes many spades: suited aces/spades, KQs, suited connectors/gappers with spades, etc. Remaining jacks extremely thin (only Jd left). Value from overpairs (QQ/TT/AA/KK, though some unlikely to flat so passively) or weaker pairs/draws unlikely to call a third bet on this scary board. Her calling station tendencies noted historically, but betting risks only getting called by flushes (bad) or folds (missed value). OOP check first after two value bets induces bluffs from missed draws (non-nut flush draws? unlikely, str8 draws like 9T/10x) or thinner value from overpairs/pairs, while checking represents possible flush/strength and protects stack deep in tournament (~158BB). Pot 2400 not worth risking into likely flush; realize equity by checking and snapping off bluffs/value bets from worse, maximizing EV long-term over 150 hands.

Board: 4s Jc Js 7s 8s. I hold Kd4c — two pair (Jacks and Fours). Pot is 2,400 and Elon checked the river after betting flop and turn. What beats me: any Jack (trips/full house) and any spade in his hand (the river completed a four‑spade board so a single spade in his hole is a flush). What I beat: most Kx/Qx/Tx-type holdings, missed broadways and other medium hands that will often check behind or call a modest bet.

Elon’s line (bet flop, bet turn, check river) is somewhat polar — it contains strong Jx hands and completed spade draws, but also missed bluffs and medium showdown hands that he’s willing to check. Given that, a small‑to‑medium value bet extracts from worse hands and thin calls while keeping the size manageable against the hands that beat me. I don’t want to jam (the board is dangerous), and checking behind surrenders value to hands that will call a small bet. Plan: bet about 50% pot to get value from weaker calls, and fold to any large check‑raise.

Action: bet 1,200 (≈½ pot).

River check faced with Minni's 1200 bet into 2400 (~50% pot) on flush-completing 4s Jc Js 7s 8s board. Top set (Jh) with 9d kicker crushed by any spade flush (possible in her wide button range: suited broadways, connectors, Ax spades etc., given calls on two streets with spades falling). However, beats overpairs (QQ/AA unlikely to flat so passively preflop-wide), unpaired hands, weaker Jx (AJ/KJ/QJ without spade), missed straight draws (5-6/9-10/T9 etc.), and bluffs. Pot odds excellent (3600:1200 requires ~25% equity; we have ~40-50% vs polarized range estimating 35% flushes, 65% worse). Minni's history: calls light (e.g., Hands 33,39,52) but bluffs rivers (e.g., 51,54,57); passive calls here suggest not trapped monsters preflop/call-heavy lines. Deep stacks (158BB/206BB), calling ~8% stack low variance; folding misses value from worse, raising risks only flushes jamming back. Hero call maximizes EV long-term in tournament.

Heads-up on the button (SB) with deep stacks (19k+). 9d3d is a suited hand with straight and flush potential, well within the wide opening range for button vs BB in heads-up play. Standard to raise to steal the BB or play post-flop in position. Minni defends wide based on history but we'll apply pressure. Raising 3x BB (300 total) is balanced and common sizing seen in prior hands.

Elon opened to 300 from the button (SB) heads-up. I hold K♥Q♦ (KQ offsuit) in the big blind — a strong, playable hand heads-up and often ahead of the SB opening range. With deep stacks (~20k) I want to take the initiative, build a pot with a hand that plays well postflop, and get fold equity against weaker holdings. I choose a standard 3-bet to 1200 (roughly 3–4x his open): large enough to extract value and force some folds, but small enough not to commit my stack. Plan: fold to a large 4-bet shove; if called, continue with aggression on K/Q or dry flops and pot-control on A-high or very coordinated boards.

Heads-up on button (SB) facing a 4x 3-bet to 1200 after my open to 300 with 9d3d. Suited with some straight/flush potential, but gappy low cards mean it needs to improve significantly postflop. OOP (SB acts first postflop) deep-stacked (~19k effective), realizing equity with speculative hand is difficult against Minni's likely 3-bet range (Ax, broadways, pockets, strong suited). Calling 900 into 1500 (~2.67:1 pot odds) commits ~5% stack but poor implied odds OOP if miss. 4-bet bluff possible but 93s bad candidate (low blockers, fold equity low vs aggressive Minni). Folding preserves stack for superior positions/hand strengths; standard discipline in heads-up.

Heads-up on the button with 5♦8♠. In heads-up play I should open a wide range — I have position and a playable, spewy hand. I’ll raise to 300 (a standard 3x BB sizing at this table) to apply pressure and gain initiative. If Elon folds I take the pot; if he 3-bets big I’ll fold this marginal hand; if he calls I can C-bet favorable flops and play quietly on dry boards, using position to realize equity and extract value when appropriate.

Heads-up in BB vs aggressive SB/Button raiser Minni who frequently opens wide (3x=300). JTo offsuit is a strong defending hand heads-up: ~45% equity vs typical button open range (high cards, suited, broadways, Ax). Pot odds excellent (200 to call into 400, getting 3:1). Deep stacks (~190BB), prefer calling to control pot size OOP postflop, realize equity with straight/flush potential, top pair/top kicker possible, and ability to check-raise strong flops. Folding too tight; 3-betting risks bloating pot OOP with speculative hand better suited for multiway/set-mining but here heads-up call optimal for implied odds and playability.

Monster flop with top two pair (JJTT), very strong hand heads-up OOP vs Minni's wide button opening range which includes many Jx, Tx, broadways, and straight draws like 98, Q9, KQ that hit this board. Paired Tens reduce some full house threats but protect against bluffs. Deep stacks (~30BB effective? Wait, 18825/100=188BB deep). Bet for thin value from worse pairs (AJ, AT, JT worse two pair? No, my kickerless but top), overpairs, and charge draws heavily present (open-enders 98/Q9, gutshots). Check risks giving free card to draws or check-raise spots, but betting builds pot OOP with nuts-ish hand and denies equity. Size 2/3 pot (400) balances value/protection/inducement without overbetting vs aggressive villain.

Flop: Td Js Th. I hold 5♦8♠ — no pair and essentially zero immediate equity. Elon leads for 400 into a ~1000 pot (small probe bet). As the preflop raiser I could attempt a bluff-raise, but Elon has shown he’s willing to call down and be sticky; raising here with pure air risks too much and will often get called or check-raised. Calling 400 keeps the pot manageable, preserves position (I act last on turn/river), allows me to float and potentially take the pot away on a favorable turn or fold to significant aggression. Plan: call now, re-evaluate on the turn — if he barrels big on a blank I’ll fold; if he checks or gives a blank I can fire a turn bluff or take it down. Safer, lower-variance play that preserves fold equity and uses position.

Monster full house (Tens full of Jacks) on a paired board, nuts or near-nuts heads-up vs Minni's wide button calling range which includes overpairs (AA/KK/QQ), weaker two pairs (AJ/AT/AQ), top pair (KJ/QJ), and draws that called flop (e.g., 98, Q9 for str8 draws, flush draws unlikely). Turn 4c is a low brick that doesn't complete obvious draws (no flush, str8 draws miss except unlikely KQ) and improves few hands (34/set). OOP with deep stacks (~180BB), betting extracts max value from worse made hands and any lingering draws while building huge pot with our lock. Check risks free river card or giving villain chance to bluff, but betting polarizes range appropriately (villain folds trash, calls worse). Size ~3/4 pot (1050) for thin value without scaring off Jx/Tx/overpairs; larger pot bet possible but prefer this to induce calls from medium strength.

Board is Td Js Th 4c and I hold 5♦8♠ — no pair, no meaningful draw. Elon called pre, led the flop (400) and now barrels a much larger turn bet/raise to 1050 into a 2450 pot. That line is heavily weighted to value (tens, Jx, sets/full houses, even quads combos) and not one I can profitably chase. I have ~6 outs to pair on the river (~13% raw), and many of those will still lose to trips/full houses. Pot odds for a call are ~30% (1050/(2450+1050)) but my true equity vs his range is far lower. A bluff-raise is too risky against an opponent who calls down; folding preserves chips and follows my plan to not call big turn aggression with pure air. Fold.

Heads-up preflop from SB (button), 67h is a strong suited connector with good playability, high equity against BB calling range. Minni defends wide based on history, often calling raises and playing multi streets. Standard aggressive open: raise to 3x BB (300 total) to build pot with position and strong hand. Deep stacks support this. Avoid limping to maintain initiative.

I have A♣K♣ — the best non-pair hand preflop in heads-up. Elon opened to 300 from the button; calling 200 more OOP lets him take the initiative and c-bet wide. With deep stacks (~195bb) and a strong hand that plays well both IP and vs a wide calling range, I want to seize initiative and build the pot while preserving maneuverability postflop. I will 3-bet to 1200 (total) — large enough to gain fold equity and price out some of his weaker hands, but small enough to leave room for postflop play and implied odds if called. Plan: if he folds I take the pot; if he calls I'll continuation-bet favorable flops and proceed cautiously on coordinated boards; if he 4-bets big I will re-evaluate (likely fold to extreme 4-bets, call or shove depending on sizing).

Heads-up preflop on button with 67h suited connector after opening to 300 and facing a 4x 3bet to 1200 from BB. 67h has excellent playability, straight/flush potential, and high equity against Minni's likely polarized 3bet range (QQ+, AKo/s, some bluffs like suited Ax/connectors). Deep stacks (~190BB) provide strong implied odds for set-mining/speculative hands. Pot odds to call 900 into 1500 (~1.7:1) require ~37% equity, but postflop dynamics in position make this profitable. Minni defends wide historically and plays pots deep, often checking flops or calling bets. Calling maintains initiative flexibility postflop (cbet strong draws, float, stack-off with monsters). Folding too tight for this hand; 4betting risks bloating pot OOP disadvantage with non-nutted hand. Standard play: call and play postflop aggressively.

I have A♣K♣ in a 3‑bet pot and the flop comes 4♥ K♦ 5♦ — top pair with the best kicker in a favorable, relatively uncoordinated board. Elon’s calling range to my 3‑bet is wide (Kx, medium pocket pairs, diamond draws, and many floats), so this is an excellent spot to extract value and charge the diamond draws. I’ll c‑bet a substantive but not overly large size to deny equity and keep weaker K’s and pairs in. Betting 1,500 into the 2,400 pot (~60% pot) accomplishes that while leaving room to maneuver on the turn. If he calls I’ll proceed based on the turn (revalue on diamonds or two‑pair cards); if he check‑raises big I’ll put him on a set/two‑pair and re‑evaluate (call smaller raises, fold to extreme overbets without improvement).

Heads-up on button with 6h7h vs Minni's flop bet of 1500 into 3900 pot (~38% pot, but sizing large relative to preflop action). Board: 4h Kd 5d. Holding nut flush draw (10 hearts remaining for flush) + gutshot straight draw (3x or 8x, ~8 outs) for ~16-18 total outs (~30-35% equity to any improvement on turn). Pot odds excellent: 1500 to call into 3900 (post-call pot 5400), requiring ~22% equity, which we crush. Position allows controlling turn: bet if miss to rep showdown value/steal, check-raise draws/value, or stack off if hit. Minni's range polarized post-3bet/cbet: Kdxx, sets, two-pair, QQ+/JJ, bluffs (Ax suited, connectors); our hand has great playability, crushes bluffs/worse draws, live vs overpairs. Deep stacks (~110BB effective post-call) provide massive implied odds. Raising risks bloating pot vs monsters (KK/KQds/K5ds etc.), folding equity low vs calling station tendencies (history shows wide defense, multi-street calls/checks). Calling realizes equity, maintains flexibility OOP disadvantage avoided since pos. History: Minni aggressive but folds to aggression sometimes (e.g., Hand 51,54), but calls deep. Standard: call and play turn dynamically.

Turn is 9♦ making the board 4♥ K♦ 5♦ 9♦ — the third diamond. I hold A♣K♣ (top pair, best kicker) but the 9♦ completes any two‑diamond combos in Elon’s wide 3‑bet/call range (AdQd, QdJd, JdTd, etc.). Betting here would bloat the pot and charge hands I beat, but it also builds a big pot where I’m now vulnerable to a made flush and could get check‑raised off my hand. With deep stacks and being out of position, I prefer pot control: check the turn, see how Elon responds, and preserve fold equity vs a large turn shove. Plan: if Elon leads small‑to‑medium I’ll call and re‑evaluate on the river; if he checks back I’ll take a free card and value/target on the river; if he jams or overbets the pot I’ll give strong credit to a made flush and fold.

Heads-up on button, deep stacks (~170BB). Holding 67h suited connector on 4h Kd 5d 9d turn board. We have a strong gutshot straight draw (8x completes 4-5-6-7-8, 4 outs ~9%), backdoor heart flush draw, and overall good playability/live cards (blocks some straights). No pair but high equity realization in position. Preflop: opened button, called 4x 3bet (good vs wide BB defense). Flop: called large cbet into K45 as semi-bluff/draw (double gutshot + backdoor). Turn 9d bricks us but adds diamond flush draw pressure (3 diamonds on board), improving some draws but scaring value ranges without diamond. Minni cbets flop aggressively (1500 into 2400 ~63% pot, polarized: Kx, sets/two-pair, QQ+/bluffs) then checks turn, indicating potential weakness (missed draw, overpair slowing down, trap unlikely deep). History shows Minni calls multiple streets wide but folds to aggression at times (e.g., Hand 51 river fold, 54 flop fold, 55 flop fold). Position + initiative: betting exploits her check (fold equity high vs air/overpairs wary of flush/straight), gets value from worse draws/pairs (6x/7x, floats), builds pot OOP disadvantage avoided. Checking risks giving free river card to her draws/improvements, loses pot if she had air. Pot 5400, bet ~55% pot (3000) balances value/bluff freq in our calling/3bet range, strong SPR post-bet (~4:1), sets up river shove if improves or thin value. EV+ vs passive checkdown long-term stack maximization.

I checked the turn to control the pot with A♣K♣ and Elon leads 3,000 into ~5,400 (making the pot 8,400). I still have top pair with the best kicker, and calling costs 3,000 for ~8,400 in the pot (≈26.8% required equity). His turn bet is consistent with made diamond flushes, sets/two‑pair, but also with Kx and some bluffs in his wide call-to‑3bet range. Raising OOP here without diamond blockers or a clear bluff plan would bloat the pot and put me in a very awkward spot against a flush — folding is too weak given the pot odds and my equity. I will call the 3,000 to see the river and reevaluate (if a diamond arrives I’ll be prepared to fold to heavy action; if a blank comes I can extract value or target medium river lines).

Board is 4♥ K♦ 5♦ 9♦ 2♠ and I hold A♣K♣ — top pair with the best kicker. Elon’s line (open-call of my 3‑bet, calling the flop c‑bet, betting the diamond turn) contains a mix of Kx, medium pairs, sets and several diamond draws. The river 2♠ is a blank — most diamond draws missed — so my hand is too strong to just check behind. I want to extract value from worse K’s and pocket pairs while keeping the sizing small enough to fold to a big raise representing a completed flush. Betting ~60% of the pot (7,000 into 11,400) accomplishes that: it charges missed draws and gets calls from worse hands, but doesn’t overcommit me if Elon jams. If he check‑raises heavily I’ll heavily weigh a fold to a likely flush; if he calls I expect to win most showdowns.

River decision heads-up on button with 67h (missed open-ender from flop, now pure air with 7-high). Board: 4h-Kd-5d-9d-2s runs out scary with possible straights (3-6 no, but 3x/6x/Ax/Tx straights unlikely given action, Kd/flush, sets, Kx). Pot 18400, facing 7000 donk (~38% pot) from Minni who 3bet pre, cbets flop large (polarized), checks turn (weakens?), calls my turn bluff-bet 3000, then rivers huge. Her line screams strength: Kx value (Kd/K9s/K5ds), sets (KK/44/55), two-pair/combos, completed diamond flush (AdTd/QdJd/10d etc., since turn 9d completes), unlikely bluffs (missed draws fold river or check). My hand has 0 showdown value (beats pure bluffs <7-high, <1% range), poor blocker value (no K/9/5/4/2), equity ~5-10% vs her value-heavy donk range. Pot odds ~3.6:1 require ~22% equity, but we have far less; calling commits ~50% stack (14125) OOP next time. History: Minni calls wide but fires strong lines (e.g., Hand 52 massive river value, Hand 66 river value), rarely bluffs big river after calling turn. Folding maximizes stack preservation for better spots in H2H deep tournament; no fold equity on shove (minraise 14000 > stack). Standard exploitative fold vs aggressive BB value.

Heads-up on the button with 8h7d offsuit and deep stacks — this is a perfectly acceptable hand to open. I want initiative and fold equity and to play the post‑flop in position, so I will open-raise rather than limp. A standard 3xBB open (to 300 total) is appropriate: it pressures the big blind, builds a pot when I have position and postflop playability, and keeps the range wide. If Elon 3‑bets big I will let this marginal hand go; if he calls I’ll c‑bet favorable flops and use position to extract value or control pot size.

Heads-up against Minni on the button who raised to 300 (3x BB). My hand is 2h Td, a very weak offsuit hand with poor playability, low equity against a typical button opening range which includes a wide variety of hands. Calling 200 into a 400 pot offers 3:1 odds, but 2T offsuit hits flops poorly, has bad implied odds, and is difficult to play postflop from BB OOP. Folding preserves my stack for better spots in this deep-stacked heads-up dynamic (14025 effective). Consistent with GTO heads-up strategy where such trash hands are folded to button opens.

3h5c is a very weak offsuit hand with low connectivity and poor post-flop playability, especially out of position in the small blind heads-up. Heads-up opening ranges from the button/SB are wide (around 40-50%), but this hand is below that threshold due to its low card strength, lack of suit, and limited equity against a defending BB range. Minni defends wide and aggressively based on history, but limping or raising risks getting dominated OOP deep-stacked (140BB effective). Folding preserves stack for superior spots to maximize EV over 150 hands.

I have pocket Aces (Ah As) heads-up on the button with pot = 150 and only 50 to call. This is the best starting hand — I want to build the pot and isolate Elon while keeping worse hands and callers in. Min-raise is 200, but a standard 3xBB sizing to 300 gives good value, protects against giving free cards, and keeps post-flop leverage in position. If Elon 3-bets large I can reassess (4-bet or call depending on size) — but for now raise to 300 total.

Preflop in the big blind facing a button/SB raise to 300 (call 200 into 400 pot). My hand 9d3s is extremely weak: offsuit, disconnected low cards with no high card value or straight potential against a typical button raising range. Pot odds are about 3:1 (25% equity needed), but 93o has far less than that equity vs Minni's likely wide but polarized range (premiums, suited connectors, Ax). Defending wide in BB is standard, but this is a clear fold to preserve stack. Minni's history shows frequent button raises, often stealing blinds, but no need to defend trash. Folding maximizes EV long-term.

Heads-up on the button/SB with Ad7s, a strong ace offsuit hand for this spot. In heads-up play, the SB raising range is very wide, and A7o has good playability, blockers, and high card strength. Minni in BB has been aggressive historically, frequently raising pre-flop and calling down light post-flop, but folding to SB raises isn't uncommon. Pot is 150, dead money from blinds. Raising standard 3x BB to 300 builds the pot when ahead of her calling range and applies pressure. Limping is weak and invites her to take initiative; folding is too tight pre-flop heads-up. My stack is 13k effective, deep enough for post-flop flexibility. Plan to c-bet flops well-represented by range.

Heads-up vs a 300 open from the button while I'm in the big blind with K♥6♠. Calling costs 200 into a 400 pot (≈33% break-even), and vs a wide button opening range K6o has reasonable equity and good showdown value. I'm out of position so I don't want to 3‑bet and bloat the pot with a weak kicker; calling keeps the pot controllable and lets me realize equity or pot‑control postflop. Plan: call to 300 total, see the flop, fold to heavy aggression on dry boards, and play for value if I pair the King or improve significantly.

I have K♥6♠ on K♦6♣3♠ — two pair (Kings and Sixes) in a heads‑up pot after calling preflop. Elon's button opening range is wide, so I’m likely ahead of most single‑pair hands and many bluffs. The flop is pretty dry (few strong draws), so I should start building the pot for value and charge worse Kings, single pairs, and bluffs. A half‑pot value bet (~300 into 600) keeps weaker hands and floats in, while avoiding bloating the pot out of position. If he raises I’ll re‑evaluate (likely a set or a strong draw/bluff); for a reasonable raise I’ll lean to calling to realize equity. Betting also denies free cards that could give him two‑pair/set on the turn. Bet 300.

Heads-up on a dry K-6-3 rainbow board. Ad7s has ace-high with backdoor potential and position. Minni's 1/3 pot donk bet after calling preflop raise is often polarized toward weak Kx, gutshots, or bluffs trying to seize initiative; strong hands like sets or two-pair would typically check-raise. My button raising range includes many Ax, Kx, and broadways that beat her bluffing range. Calling the small bet is +EV: low cost (33% pot), preserves stack, realizes equity against her floats/value, allows check-fold/raise turns as needed. Raising has fold equity but risks villain calling with medium strength and putting me in tough river spots OOP later. Folding ace-high is too tight in position vs small bet. Pot control key with no strong hand.

I have K♥6♠ on K♦6♣3♠5♦ — two pair in a heads‑up pot after Elon called my flop bet. Elon opened wide from the button and called my flop raise, so his continuing range is full of worse Kings, single pairs (6x, 3x), pocket pairs and bluffs. The turn is a relatively blank, so this is a clear value‑bet spot. I want to charge worse Kings and pairs and deny free cards, while keeping the pot manageable out of position. Betting ~2/3 pot should get called by thinner hands and pocket pairs while folding out some bluffs. If he raises big I’ll re‑evaluate (large check‑raise is likely a set or very strong K); I’ll be willing to call a small raise but fold to a polarized shove. Bet 800 into 1200.

Facing a second donk bet on turn from Minni in BB after calling preflop raise. Board Kd6c3s5d is somewhat drawy now with diamonds and possible gutshots (e.g., 4x for wheel straight draw). Ad7s is ace-high with Ad blocker to nut flush draws and some backdoor spade equity, but no made hand or strong draw. My button raising range pre includes many Ax, Kx, broadways, and sets that beat her likely polarized donk range (weak Kx, bluffs, draws). Her flop donk was small (1/3 pot), now turn bet ~2/3 pot into ~1200 suggests possible medium strength, thin value, or bluff to charge draws/continue pressure. Pot odds excellent (800 to call into 2000 pot, ~28% needing ~25% equity which ace-high achieves vs her range). Calling realizes equity, controls pot size OOP for her on river, allows check-fold/value bet rivers profitably. Folding ace-high too tight in position vs aggressive villain who overbets bluffs historically. Raising risks bloating pot OOP with marginal hand vs calling range (Kd, two-pair, sets); fold equity not huge given pot commitment. Deep stacks (13k eff) favor realizing equity over spew. Plan: call, evaluate river board/texture.

I have two pair (K♣? K♦ on board + K♥ in my hand and 6♣ on board + 6♠ in my hand = Kings & Sixes) on a K♦-6♣-3♠-5♦-4♥ board. Heads‑up vs Elon's wide button raising range (he raised preflop and then called both my flop and turn bets), his continuing range is loaded with worse Kings, single pairs (6x, 3x) and some bluffs. The river 4 completes a few straights (any 2 or 7) and gives a few trip combos, but those are a relatively small portion of his range. I want to extract value from worse Kx, 6x and thin pair calls while keeping the pot from getting bloated where I’d be crushed by the rarer straights/sets. A small value bet (~1/3–1/2 pot) accomplishes that — it gets called by many worse hands while folding some pure air. I’ll bet a compact amount (1000) to maximize thin value; if he raises big/shoves I’ll assume a polarized, very-strong range and fold/closely re-evaluate (don’t bloat the pot further).

River 4h completes potential gutshots like A2 (wheel straight 2-3-4-5-6) or 2-7 (3-4-5-6-7 straight), but my Ad7s blocks A2 wheel and 7 for higher straight. Ace-high with diamond blocker on Kd-6c-3s-5d-4h board (two diamonds, low connected). Minni's small donk bets all streets (flop 300 ~1/3 pot, turn 800 ~1/2 pot, river 1000 ~1/4 into 3800) suggest polarized range: thin value (weak Kx like K6/K5/K3, pairs like 66/55/44/33, completed draws) or bluffs (missed draws like 24/27/47 gutshots, diamond draws). Her history shows aggression heads-up, calling down light, but small sizing indicates not nuts (sets/two-pair would bet larger). Position allows cheap call (1000 into 3800, ~21% equity needed; ace-high has ~25-35% vs her range realizing equity well OOP for her). Raising risks bloating pot OOP with air vs calling range (Kx, straights); fold equity low given pot investment. Folding ace-high too tight vs small bet from frequent bluffer. Call realizes equity, pot controls for showdown value if she has worse.

Heads-up on the button (SB) with Q9♥ — a very playable hand in position. Pot is 150 and I only need to put in 50 to complete, but raising to 200 (the min raise) is the best play: it seizes the initiative, builds the pot when I have positional advantage, and creates fold equity against a wide BB defending range. Q9s has good postflop playability (top-pair potential, backdoor nut-flush/straight possibilities) so I want to play the hand relatively aggressively and keep the BB guessing. A flat call is too passive here and folding is overly tight. I’ll raise to 200 to apply pressure while keeping future options with deep stacks.

Preflop in BB facing a small raise to 200 from Minni on the button/SB. Pot is 300, need to call 100 more for 3:1 odds. A2o blocks AA/AK and has decent equity against her wide button raising range, which from history includes suited connectors, broadway cards, Ax, pairs, and speculative hands. Minni is aggressive preflop but often checks postflop or gets called down, suggesting a polarized but bluff-heavy range. Defending OOP with Ax is standard; folding is too tight in heads-up deepstack. Raising OOP with A2o is suboptimal due to reverse implied odds and position disadvantage. Plan to call and play postflop cautiously, folding to heavy aggression or cbetting if I connect well.

Flopped top pair with weak kicker (A2o) OOP against Minni's wide button opening range, which includes many Ax, broadways, suited connectors, and bluffs based on history. Board 7h Ad 4c is somewhat dry but features a backdoor heart draw (holding 2h). Checking is standard for pot control, value from worse Ax/pairs/draws when she cbets (likely wide), and sets up check-raise bluffs or calls. Donk betting OOP is suboptimal as it risks raising off equity against her floats/strength, and positionally disadvantaged. Plan: check, call small cbets, raise big ones for value/protection, fold to heavy aggression if miss improvement.

Heads-up I was the preflop aggressor and have position with Q♥9♥ on a dry 7♥ A♦ 4♣ flop. This is a textbook spot to continuation-bet: the board is ace-high but otherwise harmless, my range as raiser contains many A‑x and broadways so a bet can fold out lots of hands the BB called preflop with. Q9s has some backdoor flush potential and a little showdown value, so I’m happy to apply pressure rather than check and allow free cards. I’ll size to ~50% pot to get decent fold equity while keeping the pot manageable with deep stacks. Plan: take the pot here if he folds; if he calls I’ll re-evaluate on the turn (continue carefully on safe cards, fold to heavy aggression when I’ve missed), and fold to a large raise since I’m unlikely to have the ace or a made hand.

Flop: Hold As2h on 7h Ad 4c board facing a small 200 bet into 400 pot (now pot 600, calling 200 for ~3.5:1 odds). Top pair weak kicker (TPWK) OOP against button's wide cbet range, which includes bluffs, worse Ax (AJ-AT, suited), pairs, draws (backdoor flush/straight). Blocker to Asx and nuts AA reduces her value heavily; 2h backdoor heart draw adds equity. Small bet size screams polarized (weak value/draws/bluffs), perfect spot for pot control calling small cbets as planned, denying her equity realization cheaply while keeping bluffs/live and extracting from worse. Raising risks reverse implied odds OOP (worse Aces stack off) and folds out too many bluffs; folding too tight given price/equity (~45-55% vs range). Call, reevaluate turn OOP cautiously, folding to strong aggression without improvement.

Turn: OOP with As2h (TPWK) on 7h Ad 4c Kd board, pot 800. King is a terrible card, improving strong parts of Minni's wide button range (AK, KQ, KJ, KTs etc.) that crush me, while my hand has poor reverse implied odds. Preflop small raise and flop small cbet (which I called, representing strength) suggest her range is polarized: value (AJ+, sets, two-pair unlikely), bluffs/draws (flush/straight draws, missed broadways). Betting OOP risks bloating pot OOP, folding out better bluffs/equity, or facing raises from monsters I can't call profitably. Checking maintains pot control, induces bluffs from missed draws/value, allows cheap realization of showdown equity (~40-50% vs range given blockers/position), and sets up river decisions. Plan: check, call small bets if continuation of thin value, fold to big aggression post-K unless river binks 2/A. Consistent with preflop/flop pot control strategy OOP deepstacked.

Check. I was the preflop aggressor and c‑bet the flop, but the K♦ on the turn is a bad card for my hand — Q9♥ missed and the backdoor heart potential is now dead with only one card to come, so I effectively have only queen‑high. Elon’s check is ambiguous (pot‑control with A/x or a trap with K/x or a pair), and barreling the turn would be a pure bluff with limited fold equity and the risk of getting raised off the hand. With pot control and deep stacks, the +EV line is to check and avoid bloating the pot with air; I’ll fold to significant aggression and re‑evaluate on the river only if a very favorable card or a small, manageable bluff opportunity appears.

River pairs the 4s on a 7h Ad 4c Kd 4s board, completing no flush but possibly pairing speculative hands like 4x or giving trips. Holding As2h for top pair weak kicker (TPWK), OOP in BB vs Minni's button range after preflop small raise, small flop cbet (called), mutual turn checks on scary Kd. Her turn check behind suggests capped range: missed draws (hearts, backdoor straights), weaker Ax (AJ-A5 suited/off), mid pairs (88-99,77? unlikely), air/bluffs, few monsters (AK blocked somewhat, sets value bet flop likely). Betting OOP risks folding out worse equity/bluffs to her folds/check-raises from better Ax/trips, bloating pot without much reverse implied odds left, and positionally disadvantaged deepstacked (16k effective). Checking realizes showdown equity cheaply (~40-50% vs her capped range given As blocker/reduced AA/AK), induces bluffs/thin value bets from her aggressive history (often cbets rivers), allows hero-calling small bets with blockers/equity while folding to big ones. Consistent with pot control strategy throughout hand OOP on draw-heavy/scare-card board. No strong value/protection needed as board pairs low/misses big draws.

I raised preflop and c‑bet the flop but checked the turn; the river 4♠ gives me no pair (Q9♥, queen‑high) and only marginal blockers. Elon’s line — calling my flop c‑bet then checking turn and river — looks like a medium‑strength calling range (A‑x, K‑x, some pairs) that will often call a river bluff. With no showdown value and poor fold equity (I don’t block enough of his value combos), a river bluff is thin and likely −EV. Best play is pot‑control and take the free showdown: check and avoid risking chips on a low‑prospect bluff.

Hand #75, heads-up pre-flop on the button/SB with 2d7c, one of the worst possible starting hands (27 offsuit). No straight or flush potential, dominated by virtually any pair or better hand. Pot is 150, to call 50 (completing SB), but even in heads-up where ranges widen, this hand has negative EV long-term due to poor playability and low equity against Minni's calling range. Minni has been aggressive but folding preserves stack for premium spots in this tournament with 75/150 hands played. Standard fold.

Heads-up on the button with Jh7h (suited hearts) — a playable hand with good postflop playability and backdoor flush/straight potential. In heads-up I want to seize the initiative rather than limp-call out of position, so I open-raise. I’ll raise to 300 (3x BB): it’s a standard, table-consistent size that gives solid fold equity, keeps the pot manageable relative to our deep stack, and lets me c-bet many flops from position. If Elon 3-bets big I’ll be willing to fold; versus a small 3-bet I may call and play cautiously postflop. Overall this is an aggressive, position-focused open to maximize long-term value.

KJo is a strong hand heads-up against a button raise, which is likely wide given Minni's frequent opening from late position based on hand history. Equity is around 42-45% vs. a typical 40% button range. Calling OOP gets good pot odds (200 to call into 400 pot, plus implied odds with deep stacks). Post-flop, King-high blocker, straight potential, and positionally we can play well. Folding is too tight; 3-betting is possible but unnecessary as it commits us OOP with a hand that plays better multiway or in position post-flop. Goal is stack maximization, so defend wide profitably.

Flop of AcQc5c is a nightmare for KJo: monotone club board heavily favors villain's wide button raising range, which includes many Ax, Qx, suited broadways, and flush draws/combos. My hand has zero equity – no pair, no flush draw, no straight draw (KQ would broadway but J doesn't connect usefully), just king-high with irrelevant blockers. OOP, betting is suicidal as it gets called by entire range and folds out worse. Checking allows potential check-back or induces bluffs, but realistically prepares to fold to any bet. Spewing chips here risks stack; folding to aggression preserves for better spots. Long-term stack maximization demands discipline on such wet boards.

Board is A♣ Q♣ 5♣ — two overcards to my J7 and a monotone club runout. Elon’s cold-call preflop in heads-up is very wide and includes many A/Q combinations and club hands that have me dominated or crushed. I have almost no showdown equity and limited backdoor outs, so a dry c-bet has poor expected value: I’ll often get called by better hands and can be exploited by a check-raise with a flush. With position I prefer pot control and flexibility — check to either induce a bluff, call small probes, or fold to large aggression. Plan: if Elon checks back we go to the turn and reassess; if he fires a small bet I’ll call to realize equity and keep the pot manageable; if he bets big or raises I’ll fold.

Heads-up deep-stacked on a scary AcQc5c flop, we checked through with KJo (king-high, no draws), preserving stack on a board favoring villain's button raising range heavily (Ax, suited broadways, flush draws). Turn 10d is relatively neutral: brings gutshot straight draws (e.g., J9, 89), possible backdoor diamonds, but no flush completion and doesn't improve our hand (still just king-high, gutshot to nothing useful; blockers to AKo/s, KQs, KJ).
