Pot 600, acting OOP first. Betting risks folding out her weak Ax/Qx that might check back or calling our wide range with better; bluffs get called by floats/draws/monsters. Checking keeps pot small, induces bluffs from missed draws/floats (her flop check suggests possible weakness like Kx, Qx, suited connectors without club), realizes equity by getting to showdown cheaply if she checks again, or gets good odds on a bet (deep stacks allow implied if we hit). Discipline key for long-term stack growth in 150-hand tournament; don't spew OOP on wet board without equity.

Board A♣ Q♣ 5♣ T♦, I'm on the button with J♥7♥. Pot 600, effective stacks deep. I have no pair, no club and only 6 raw outs (three J, three 7) — several of which are ‘dirty’ because a club J/7 could also give a flush to a caller. Versus a typical heads‑up cold‑call range (lots of A/Q, club combos, some Tx) my showdown equity is very poor (~10–12%).

Preflop I opened and checked the flop to control the size of the pot; the turn gives me no additional equity and presents significant risk (many hands in Elon’s range still beat me or have a flush). Betting here would be a thin bluff with limited fold equity and high chance of getting called by better hands or check‑raised by a slowplayed strong holding. With position and deep stacks I prefer pot control and flexibility.

Decision: check. Plan: if Elon checks back we go to the river and can reassess; if he fires a small probe (≈≤1/3 pot) I’ll usually call and see the river; if he bets medium/large (>~1/2 pot) or raises I’ll fold and preserve my stack.

River Qs pairs the board's second-highest card (Q), creating possible full houses (sets/trips Q/A), two-pair combos (AQ, AQs), and improves Qx hands from villain's button raising range (broadways, suited connectors, Ax). My KdJs is pure king-high air: no pair, no flush, no straight (broadway requires precise blockers we lack), gutshot worthless now. Flop/turn check-check from both sides suggests caution — her range likely capped at mid Ax/Qx/KQ without clubs, missed flush draws (e.g., suited broadways/gappers), or floats; nuts less likely given passivity. OOP on river with 600 pot and deep stacks (27bb+ effective), betting as bluff folds out her worse (Kx, floats) but gets called by improved Qx/Ax/value; poor EV as range advantage favors her. Checking realizes equity by showdown vs. her weak check-backs (preserves stack for better spots in 150-hand tourney), induces bluffs/bets from missed draws, and sets up cheap fold to aggression (king-high rarely good). Discipline over spew: history shows Minni aggressive pre but checks streets often when weak; stack maximization demands folding air OOP on wet/paired boards.

River: A♣ Q♣ 5♣ T♦ Q♠. Pot 600, both players checked the flop and turn. I hold J♥7♥ — no pair, no club, essentially air with only dirty J/7 outs. Elon cold‑called preflop in heads‑up so his range is wide but weighted toward A/Q/x, club combos, some T/K/middle pairs. His passive line (check‑check‑check) makes a flop flush less likely (someone with the nut or near‑nut clubs would often bet the flop), and checking down looks like middle/top pair or a showdown‑oriented hand. Betting here would be a thin river bluff with very limited fold equity — many hands in his range will call a small probe — so expected value is negative. Pot is tiny relative to stacks, but that doesn’t justify risking chips when the opponent’s line indicates he’ll call or show. I preserve chips and go to showdown. Action: check.

Heads-up on the button/SB with deep stacks (168BB effective). Kc4h is a playable open-raising hand from SB against BB due to king blocker (blocks strong Kx hands), decent high-card strength, and ~58% equity versus a typical BB calling range. Wide SB opening range (~45-50% of hands) is standard to apply pressure and steal blinds frequently with position. Minni has shown aggression but also calls wide from BB in history. Raising builds pot with initiative; limping is suboptimal as it cedes advantage. Standard raise size ~3x BB (300 total). Folding too tight pre-flop; many hands left to exploit position.

Elon opened to 300 (3bb) heads-up. I’m in the big blind with J♠2♣ — a weak hand but not a complete stone. Calling requires 200 more (total 300 committed) which gives me roughly 200 to win ~600 (≈33% equity needed). Versus a wide button open I have enough equity to defend and with deep stacks I can realize equity postflop. I don’t want to 3-bet bluff with junk, so I’ll call, see a flop, and play cautiously out of position — folding to heavy aggression unless I connect or pick up good equity.

Out of position vs the button opener with J♠2♣ on T♦5♣5♠. I have no pair and no realistic draws (flush is impossible from my hand), so I have very little showdown equity versus a normal opening range that contains tens, pocket pairs and broadways. With deep stacks I prefer pot control — check and avoid bloating the pot with pure air. Plan: fold to any meaningful c-bet or sustained aggression, only entertain calling a very small probe (~≤1/3 pot) or continue if I pair (J or 2) or pick up real equity on the turn. For now: check.

Flop board Td5c5s is dry and paired, favoring my pre-flop raising range which includes overpairs (QQ+), AK, AQ, ATs+, KQs, etc., that have strong showdown value or better (trips/overpairs). My Kc4h is king-high with backdoor club potential, providing decent bluff blocker (K blocks strong Kx) and ~35-40% equity vs Minni's BB calling range (wide: low pairs, suited connectors, Ax, broadway). She checked, indicating weakness or trap, but her history shows wide calls pre but frequent checks/folds post-flop. Pot 600, deep stacks (~166BB eff.); standard small c-bet ~40-50% pot (240-300) exploits fold equity while inducing calls from weaker kings/draws/A-high. Larger risks overbetting weak range; check-back too passive, misses value from Ax/Tx. Bet 300 builds pot, denies equity to her floats/OESD (e.g., 76, 89).

I’m OOP in the big blind with J♠2♣ on T♦5♣5♠. Elon’s flop bet is small (300 into ~900 ≈ 1/3 pot). I have no pair and almost no showdown equity, but the price is cheap: calling 300 to win ~1500 gives ~20% break-even equity. I won’t 3‑bet-bluff with junk out of position, and folding here surrenders too much against a wide button C‑bet range. Call to keep bluffs in his range and see a turn. Plan: if I pair a J or 2 or pick up real equity on the turn I can proceed; otherwise fold to significant turn aggression (pot‑sized bet or a raise).

I have J♠2♣ on T♦-5♣-5♠-6♠ and I'm out of position. I missed — no pair and no real draw — and Elon has shown a standard heads‑up line: open preflop and a small flop c‑bet. With the pot only 1,200 and no bet to call on the turn, I prefer pot control: betting or bluffing OOP with pure air has very little fold equity and will only get me raised off the pot. I’ll check to keep the pot small, fold to meaningful turn aggression (pot‑sized bets or raises), and only call very small probes (~≤1/3 pot) to see a river where I can reassess.

Turn board 6s adds spade flush draw completion and some straight draws (e.g., 7-4 for gutshot, 7-3 wheel-ish but unlikely). Minni's flop call after check suggests 5x trips, Tx, Ax, Kx worse, lower pairs, or spade draws that called small bet. Her second check shows no strong hand, likely weak or trapping slowplayed 5s. My Kc4h king-high blocks her strong Kx; no made hand but position + initiative + fold equity vs her history of calling wide pre/flop but folding turns/rivers to pressure (e.g., Hands 43,45,55). Pot 1200, deep ~160BB eff.; small c-bet ~50% pot (600) continues low-frequency bluff line, charges draws (spades ~18% equity now), extracts from weaker showdown hands (A-high, low pairs), builds pot for river value if strong. Checking risks giving free card to draws/improves and misses value; overbetting unbalanced. Standard semi-bluff turn bet exploits her passive check-check line.

Fold. I’m out of position with J♠2♣ on T♦-5♣-5♠-6♠. Elon’s line (open pre, flop c‑bet, turn 600 into 1200) is consistent with tens, pocket fives/sixes or other made hands. Calling 600 would make the pot 2400, so I need ≈25% equity to break even (600/2400). I only have 6 outs (3 Jacks + 3 Twos) to pair on the river → 6/46 ≈ 13% equity, well below the required 25%. Bluff‑raising OOP with no fold equity is too risky. This is a clear fold to preserve stack and wait for a better spot.

Heads-up on the button (I have postflop position) so I should open a very wide range to seize initiative and apply pressure. 9c5s is marginal, but against a single big blind it’s fine to raise for fold equity and to take advantage of position postflop. Min-raise to 200 (2BB) is standard here: it keeps the pot manageable while forcing Elon to decide. Plan: fold to a large 3-bet, proceed postflop using position if called and c-bet favorable boards.

Heads-up play with deep stacks (174BB effective). Button (Minni) min-raises to 200 preflop, pot is 300, calling 100 gives excellent 4:1 pot odds. 87o is a playable offsuit connector with straight potential and decent postflop playability. In BB vs Button, defend wide against frequent opens. History shows Minni aggressive from button, so her range is wide. Folding too tight leaks money long-term; prefer cheap call to realize equity.

Flop board Ks4d2s is dry with a king high and spade flush draw possible. Heads-up OOP with 87o: complete air, no pair, no flush draw, no straight draw (backdoors at best). Pre-called wide from BB vs button minraise, but this misses completely. No showdown value. Betting OOP risks getting raised off equity with fold equity not worth it on this texture vs aggro Minni who can have Kx/draws. Standard check to pot control and realize fold equity post-check if she bets weak. Deep stacks, no need to bluff recklessly.

Heads-up on the button and villain checked a dry K♠-4♦-2♠ flop. I opened wide preflop so my c-bet range is strong here; this board misses a lot of the big blind’s calling range. I hold 9♣5♠ — no pair, only backdoor spade potential and 6 outs to a pair, so my equity is modest. My primary plan is to seize the initiative and use fold equity: a half-pot c-bet will fold out many missed broadways and small unpaired hands while keeping the pot manageable if called. I’ll bet 200 (½ pot). If Elon raises big I’ll give up (fold) since my hand has little showdown value; if called I’ll re-evaluate on the turn and continue aggressively only on favorable cards or clear weakness.

Heads-up on flop facing 200 bet into 600 pot (3:1 odds). Holding 87o, which whiffs the Ks4d2s board completely: no pair, no draws, no relevant backdoors, zero showdown value. Pre-flop call was wide BB defense vs button min-raise, but this texture favors Minni's range (Kx, pocket pairs, some draws). Calling OOP with air risks difficult turn spots deep-stacked (~170BB); she'd continue often with value. Raising as bluff possible but min-bet suggests weakness or trap, and board doesn't scream bluff spot (dry K-high). History shows Minni aggressive but profitable to fold air here to avoid spew. Preserve stack for better spots.

Heads-up preflop from SB/Button with T6s, a suited connector with good playability and postflop potential due to position. Opponent in BB defends wide. Standard to raise to 3x BB (300 total) to build pot, take initiative, and deny equity. Deep stacks support aggression with this hand. History shows frequent small raises preflop, so this fits.

I have pocket fours in the big blind facing a 300 open from the button. Stacks are very deep (~225BB vs ~170BB), so the implied odds for set-mining are excellent. Calling 200 more (300 total committed) keeps the pot manageable out of position and preserves the ability to fold to heavy action if I miss, while giving me the chance to extract significant value if I hit a set. A small 3-bet would bloat the pot and commit more out of position against an opponent who calls wide and can play big pots aggressively, so the most +EV line here is to call and evaluate the flop.

I called preflop to set-mine, but the flop (Kh 9d Ad) completely misses my pocket 4s. I have only 2 outs to make a set (≈4.3% to hit on the turn, ≈8.4% to hit by the river). With the pot at 600 any realistic c-bet (min ~100) would offer only ~14.3% pot odds to call, which is larger than my ~8.4% chance to improve — calling is -EV. Betting or raising OOP with no equity or fold equity is a losing line against an opener who shows a wide but calling-prone range. Plan: check to control the pot and fold to meaningful aggression; if Elon checks back I keep the free-card chance to hit a set and can re-evaluate on the turn.

Preflop raise from button with T6d suited connector was standard aggression, and Minni's call from BB widens her range to include Ax suited (esp diamonds), broadway, pairs, suited connectors. Flop Kh9dAd is highly coordinated: diamond flush draws, wheel straight draws (QJ), gutshots (QT,JT), strong Ax like AK/AQ/AT. I flop a strong 9-out diamond flush draw (~35% equity to improve to nuts by river, plus position and fold equity). No showdown value with T-high, so semi-bluff bet exploits her check (likely weak Ax, floats, or missed draws from wide calling range). History shows Minni checks flops frequently then bets turns/rivers, so betting now denies equity, builds pot when ahead, and takes initiative. Pot 600, bet 2/3 pot (400) for balanced sizing: charges draws, gets value from weaker Ax/pairs, fold equity from air. Deep stacks (17025 effective) support aggression with this equity. Checking risks giving free card to her improving draws or inducing bluffs later, suboptimal with position and range advantage.

Fold. I called preflop as a set-mining call, but the flop (Kh 9d Ad) completely misses my pocket 4s — I have only 2 outs (≈4.26% to hit on the turn, ≈8.42% to hit by the river). Elon bets 400 into a 1000 pot; calling 400 to win 1400 requires ~28.6% equity, far more than my ~8.4% chance to improve, so a call is -EV. Raising or bluffing OOP with virtually no equity and limited fold equity is also a losing line here. Villain’s range (preflop opener + flop bet) has lots of A/K/diamond combos that already beat me or pay poorly if I hit. With deep stacks I wanted implied odds for set-mining, but this flop bet makes continuing too expensive — I’ll fold and wait for a better spot.

Heads-up on the button with KsQc — a strong, playable hand that fares well against a BB’s wide defending range. I want to take the initiative, build the pot when ahead and use position post‑flop, so I’ll raise. I’ll size to 300 (standard ~3x here): big enough to get value and fold equity but small relative to our deep stacks, leaving room to maneuver if Elon 3‑bets or calls. If he calls I’ll c‑bet favorable flops and play position; if he 3‑bets large I’ll re-evaluate and often fold to heavy aggression.

Heads-up pre-flop in the big blind facing a standard button raise to 300 (3x the big blind). My hand is 9h5d, which is offsuit, poorly connected, and has very little playability or equity against a wide button opening range. Minni has been aggressive from the button in recent hands, raising frequently (e.g., hands 79, 78, 76, 74, etc.), but defending the big blind with such a weak hand out of position risks committing chips to a dominated spot post-flop with minimal showdown value or drawing potential. Folding preserves my stack (17,525, deep with 175BB effective) for better spots. No fold equity from a 3-bet bluff justifies it here, as 95o has poor blocker value and post-flop playability. Standard fold to maximize long-term EV over 150 hands.

Preflop in SB vs BB heads-up with 2d4h, one of the worst possible hands. No equity, poor playability, and low showdown value. Pot odds of calling 50 into 150 (3:1) are not sufficient given the hand's trash nature and Minni's history of wide ranges but aggressive postflop play. Folding the SB is standard to preserve stack for better spots in this long tournament (81/150 hands), avoiding speculative calls that bleed chips over time.

Heads-up on the button with Qs4s — marginal but playable in position and suited, and stacks are deep enough to play post-flop. Standard tactic is to open here to seize the initiative and steal the blind; a raise to 300 (about 3x the BB and consistent with table sizing) gives useful fold equity while keeping the pot manageable. If Elon 3-bets large I'll fold this hand; if he calls I'll use position to c-bet favorable flops and proceed cautiously on dry/overcard boards. Committing 300 total (includes my posted SB 50).

Heads-up deepstack play against Minni's aggressive button raise. Her range is extremely wide from SB/Button, including any two cards frequently based on history. J8o has solid equity (~42% vs typical button open), straight potential, backdoor flush draws, and blocks Js which she might raise wide with. Excellent pot odds: calling 200 into 400 (pot will be 600, ~3:1). OOP but playable postflop; prefer calling over folding marginal defend hand or 3-betting speculative offsuit hand. Builds pot in position for her, but we defend wide to maximize EV.

Flop Kd 2d 5s is fairly dry with a king-high board favoring Minni's wide button raising range, which includes many Kx, Ax, and broadways. J8o has minimal showdown value (overcard J, live 8 potentially), backdoor spade draw, but no immediate draws or pairs. First to act OOP deepstacked (~170BB), standard play is to check weak air hands to realize equity and pot control. Donk betting would be suboptimal as it bloats the pot OOP with a bluff catcher at best and gets called by better. Checking allows seeing a cbet frequency from Minni (historically aggressive cbettor) and defending profitably with implied odds if she misses or bluffs. Folding not applicable pre-action. Prioritizes long-term EV over forcing action.

Heads‑up on the button, I opened pre and BB checked the dry K♦ 2♦ 5♠ flop. My Q♠4♠ has no pair and only a backdoor spade runner‑runner (~3% to make by river), so my equity is poor — this is a classic spot to leverage positional advantage and fold equity. A small continuation bet about half the pot (≈300 into 600) will fold many of Elon’s missed hands and protect my perceived raising range while keeping the pot controllable if called. Plan: bet 300; if called I’ll proceed cautiously on the turn (check for pot control unless I improve), and if I face a meaningful check‑raise I’ll fold — I don’t have showdown value to continue vs big aggression.

Facing a standard-sized cbet of 300 into 900 (pot odds ~4:1, requiring ~20% equity) on a dry Kd2d5s flop OOP deepstacked (~170BB). J8o blocks broadway Js and has a live J overcard for showdown value against her wide button opening range, which includes many missed Ax, broadways, and bluffs. Backdoor spade draw adds minor equity. Historical aggression from Minni suggests frequent cbets with air, and calling realizes equity profitably while controlling pot size. Folding is too tight given odds and range advantage; raising lacks sufficient fold equity on this king-high texture where she continues wide with top pair/overpair/draws. Plan to check turns unimproved or bet if improves to gutshot/draw.

Paired my 8 on the turn for middle pair with Jack kicker on a Kd2d5s8c board. Minni's wide button raising range includes many Kx (worse kickers), Ax, broadways, lower pairs, diamond/spade draws, and air. The 8c turn is a relatively blank card that doesn't complete many strong draws but keeps flush draws live. OOP deepstacked (~140BB effective post-flop action), checking is optimal for pot control with a thin value hand that has showdown value. It induces bluffs from her frequent aggression (historical cbets and barrels), allows check-behind from floats, and sets up profitable calls or check-raises to her continuation bets. Betting risks getting raised by Kx/sets/draws while folding out her worse bluffs/marginal hands; checking realizes equity maximally long-term.

I have Q♠4♠ and after my flop c‑bet was called the turn (8♣) bricks for me — my runner‑runner spade draw is gone and I have no pair. Elon’s check on the turn looks like pot control with a pair or a float; with essentially zero clean equity (only small pair outs that often lose) betting the turn is a thin bluff that’s vulnerable to a raise. Pot is only 1,200 vs deep stacks, so I’ll control the size and evaluate the river instead of inflating the pot with a near‑nothing hand. Plan: check and fold to significant turn aggression; if Elon checks back I’ll see the river and look to bluff or take showdown depending on the card and action.

River Ace is a scary card that improves many Ax, Kx combos, and broadways in Minni's wide BTN opening range. I have middle pair Js kicker, which has showdown value but is vulnerable to better pairs, two-pair combos, and any Ace. She donk bet flop (missed by many overcards/draws), I called with float/pair on turn, she checked turn (polarizes to value or weakness). OOP deepstacked with small pot (1200), checking extracts max value from her bluffs/missed draws while pot-controlling vs better hands. Betting thin value risks folds from worse (pairs/draws) and raises from better (Ax/Kx); showdown is profitable long-term vs her frequent aggression history (e.g., Hands 52,69 where she overplays). Plan: check, showdown if she checks, call reasonable value bets if she barrels.

Both checked the turn and Elon checked the river. I hold Q♠4♠ with no pair and my only realistic outs were runner‑runner spades (gone). The river A♣ actually helps a lot of hands in his calling range (A×, K×, pocket fives, small pairs, or a diamond draw turned into a showdown check), and Elon has shown a tendency to call flop bets and see streets rather than fold to river aggression. To make a profitable bluff here I’d need a fairly high fold frequency (≈33% for a ~600 half‑pot bet, ≈20% for a 300 blocker bet), which I don’t expect versus this opponent. Given the small pot relative to stacks and my very thin equity, the least-risk, highest-expected-value play is to check and take the showdown; I will fold to any sizable bet.

Heads-up from the SB (button) against Minni in BB. Stacks are deep (180BB+). Q4o is a marginal hand but playable in position heads-up. Standard strategy is to raise a wide range preflop from SB to steal blinds or build pot OOP for opponent. Minni defends wide based on history (frequent calls and 3-bets). Raising to 3x (300 total) is standard sizing seen in prior hands. Postflop position advantage allows maneuvering weak hand. Limp risks giving free flop or facing raise; fold too tight.

Elon (button/small blind) opened to 300 heads-up — wide range. I’m in the big blind with T♠2♣, a weak offsuit hand with poor playability and few backdoor draws, but pot is 400 and I only need to call 200 more (total commit 300). That gives me ~2:1 pot odds (≈33% equity required). Versus a typical wide SB open T2o has roughly that equity and, with both stacks deep (~200+ BB), defending the BB is standard. I won’t raise — the hand is a poor bluff candidate OOP and raising creates tough post‑flop decisions. Folding is too tight heads‑up. Plan: call to see the flop and proceed cautiously (fold to heavy aggression unless I pair or pick up real equity; turn/river only continue with improvement or a clear bluffing line).

Check. I have T♠2♣ on a 5♦7♣7♦ board — no pair, no meaningful draw (only one club on board, so no realistic flush draw, no straight possibilities). Elon opened wide preflop so his range contains many hands that beat me here (7x, 5x, overpairs, overcards with a pair or pair+draw), and I’m out of position. I don’t want to bluff or build a pot with essentially nothing, so I’ll control the size by checking and see how he proceeds. If he bets I’ll fold to significant aggression unless I pair or pick up real equity; if he checks back I’ll re-evaluate on the turn and continue only with improvement or a clear, justified bluffing line.

Heads-up on the flop, OOP with Q4o on a paired 7-7-5d board. I raised preflop from SB with a wide range, and Minni defended wide from BB as per history (frequent calls pre and postflop). My hand has queen-high, no pair, no draw, and minimal showdown value. The board is dry but paired, reducing straight draw possibilities, though flush draw exists. Minni's check suggests possible weakness, but betting air OOP risks her calling/raising with better pairs/draws or trapping. Checking preserves stack, controls pot, and allows turn realization if she has worse (e.g., Ax, low cards). Bluffing here has lower equity than checking/folding to her turn bet or bluff-catching later. Deep stacks favor pot control with marginal hand. Will check and evaluate turn.

I check. The turn paired my 2 (T♠2♣ = a small pair) but it’s still a marginal holding heads‑up. Elon checked the flop and showed no aggression; his range is wide but contains many hands that currently beat me (7x, 5x, overpairs) and some two‑diamond combos that now make a flush on this three‑diamond board. Betting here would mainly fold out worse hands and get called (or raised) by hands that already beat me, so it doesn’t accomplish much. Pot control is the priority: check to see a river, then re‑evaluate. If Elon bets the river I’ll fold to large bets and consider calling small/medium bluffs; if he checks back I’ll go to showdown with my pair.

Heads-up on turn with Q4o (queen-high, no pair, no draw) on a paired 7-7-5-2d board now with three diamonds, completing flush draws. Preflop, I raised wide from SB (button), Minni defended wide from BB as per extensive history of frequent calls pre/postflop. Both checked flop for pot control (dry paired board favored checking marginal hand OOP for her). Minni checks turn again, indicating possible weakness (missed draws, ace-high, or traps with 7x), but board texture favors her calling/raising bets with pairs (77, sets, two-pair unlikely), completed flushes (diamond draws), or strong draws. My range as PFR includes overpairs, Ax, Kx, draws, bluffs; queen-high has little showdown value and blocks few calling hands. Position allows free river card or bluff-catch if she bets. Betting bluff has poor equity: flush draws complete, pairs/value call stations per history (Minni calls rivers with weak holdings frequently, e.g., hands 49,50,51). Thin value bet unprofitable as queen rarely best. Checking maximizes EV: pot control with deep stacks (~180BB), realizes equity vs. her worse high-card hands (J-high, etc.), avoids bloating pot OOP to her potential river shove/value. River decision based on card: fold to bets if scary, value/call if checks. Consistent with flop check strategy.

I have a very weak one-pair (deuces). Elon’s line — opening preflop then checking flop, turn and river — is very passive and likely contains a lot of missed overcards and some medium pairs; the obvious stronger holdings (trip 7s, 5x, or a diamond flush) would more often have produced betting earlier. A river bet is thin: it will get called by many hands that actually beat me or fold the hands I beat, and it exposes me to a raise that I shouldn’t call. With no money to call and a small pot, pot control and stack preservation heads‑up is the right play — check to showdown and take my equity.

River OOP check from Minni after checking all streets on a paired 7-7-5d-2d-As board. Preflop as button/SB PFR with wide range (Ax, KQ+, suited connectors, bluffs), Minni wide BB defense per history (frequent calls pre/postflop, station tendencies on rivers e.g., hands 49-51,65). Qh4s is queen-high no pair/draw, weak showdown but blocks Qx. Board hits value (77, sets, two-pair 75/27 rare, Ad flush, As trips/pair A). Checks through suggest missed draws (diamond flush pre-turn), weaker Ax/Jx/Tx, or traps (unlikely given passivity). Betting bluff poor: completed flush calls, 77/Ax/As call stations per history; thin value Q-high rarely best. Bet sizing risks overbet vs. deep stacks (~180BB). Check realizes equity vs. worse highs (Jx-9x missed), pot control avoids bloating OOP traps/value, leverages range advantage at showdown (PFR > caller). Consistent with flop/turn checks for marginal hand OOP realization. Check best EV.

Raise to 300 (total). Ad6d is a strong heads-up opening hand — suited ace with decent kicker and good post‑flop playability in position. I want to take the initiative, build a pot with a hand that fares well against a wide BB defending range, and apply pressure consistent with the table’s typical 300 opens. Size = standard 3x-ish open: enough fold equity but leaves deep stacks to outplay postflop.
Plan: If Elon just calls I’ll use position to c‑bet favorable flops, protect diamonds and extract value when I hit. If he 3‑bets large (900+), I’ll usually fold and preserve a healthy stack; versus a smaller 3‑bet I may call and play postflop in position.

Heads-up against Minni on the button/SB who raises frequently preflop with a wide range. My 8c9s is a suited connector with good playability, straight and flush potential, and strong implied odds deep-stacked (effective ~170BB). Pot odds are favorable: calling 200 into 400 (pot will be 600 post-call) gives ~3:1 direct odds plus significant implied. Defending BB wide vs button aggression is standard. 3-betting has some fold equity but 89s plays better postflop by calling out of position to realize equity. Folding is too tight for this spot.

Flop: 9c 3c Jh. I have 8c9s for middle pair (pair of 9s with 8 kicker), backdoor club flush draw, and some straight potential (gutshot to JT9 with a 10). Board is coordinated with two clubs and possible straight draws (e.g., KQ, T7 suited). Heads-up OOP vs aggressive button raiser Minni who has a wide preflop range including many floats, bluffs, and weak Ax/KQ. Pot is 600; betting ~2/3 pot (400) for value from worse pairs/draws (e.g., Jx, flush/straight draws), protection against overcards/club turns, and to build pot with my medium strength hand. Checking invites bluffs/check-raises but risks giving free cards to draws/improvers. Betting maintains initiative and realizes equity effectively deep-stacked (~170BB eff). Avoid overbetting as range includes stronger Jx/sets but also misses.

Flop (9c 3c Jh) is fairly dry but with two clubs – there are some club draws and Jx combos in Elon’s calling range. I hold Ad6d: no pair but Ace‑high with a diamond backdoor and an Ace blocker vs many value Ax combos. Elon, as the preflop caller, leading out for 400 into a 1000 pot is consistent with a wide range (pairs, single Jack, club draws, and bluffs). Calling 400 into the 1400 pot gives me ~22% break‑even pot odds. Versus a wide OOP barreling range my Ace‑high usually has >22% equity and plays well in position — plus I can see the turn and control pot size. Raising to 800 here would bloat the pot out of position and commit with a weak hand; folding is too nitty heads‑up. Plan: call, use position to evaluate the turn (raise on a safe Ace/diamond or bet for value if he shows weakness; fold to heavy turn aggression).

Turn card Ks is a scary card that likely hits villain's button calling range hard (KQ, KJ, AK, K9s, etc. for top pair or better; also Q10 for straight). My 9s (pair of 9s with 8c kicker) is now medium strength at best OOP heads-up deep-stacked (~170BB eff, pot 1400). Preflop wide range + flop call suggests she has Jx/9x/draws, but king improves many strong hands while my hand has showdown value against bluffs/misses. Betting risks check-raises from improved hands and poor EV vs her range (low fold equity on turn). Checking controls pot, induces bluffs from floats/missed draws (her aggression noted in history), allows hero calls/folds based on sizing, and keeps playability. Standard play OOP with marginal made hand post-scare card.

Elon checked the turn (K♠) and it’s checked to me. I have A♦6♦ — only Ace‑high and no backdoor diamond now. The turn improves some of Elon’s range (Kx) but also gives him reason to check with missed club draws or medium hands after he barreled the flop. Betting here would be a thin value attempt or a bloated bluff into a wide calling range; with deep stacks and a marginal hand I prefer pot control and to realize equity in position. Check behind to keep the pot manageable, see the river for a clearer decision (bet for value on an Ace/diamond or use river pot‑control/bluff depending on Elon’s action and sizing).

River Qh completes some straight draws (primarily 10x for 9TJQK straight) and improves hands like AQ/KQ/JQ to two pair or better, but also bricks many floats and missed club draws from Minni's wide button calling range. My 89s gives middle pair + weak kicker with some backdoor equity realized earlier, but showdown value against her bluffs, weak Jx (JT-J8), Ax high, and missed draws (e.g., KQ that didn't connect fully, club draws without club). She checked turn behind after my check on scary K, suggesting caution with monsters and possible medium strength or draws. OOP first on river deep-stacked (~170BB eff, pot 1400 small), betting risks check-raises from improved value (KJ/KQ/JQ/10x/AQ) and thin calls from better showdown; thin value from worse unlikely given board and her aggression history. Checking induces bluffs from missed hands (standard vs aggressive opp who floated flop), controls pot, and realizes equity at showdown vs her capping range. Hero call vs small bet if she bluffs. Consistent with turn check to pot control marginal made hand post-scare cards.

I opened pre with A♦6♦ and called Elon's 400 flop c-bet to use position and my Ace blocker. On the K♠ turn he checked and I checked behind to control the pot and avoid bloating the pot with a marginal hand. The Q♥ river pairs the board but does not improve me — I still only have Ace‑high and no diamonds. Elon checked the river, showing weakness or pot control with a marginal holding. Betting here would be a thin value attempt (half‑pot requires him to fold >~33% to be profitable) or a bluff that’s likely to be called by worse or check‑raised by better; given stack sizes and the line he took I prefer to keep the pot small. I check to realize my equity and avoid getting exploited or committed with a marginal hand.

Heads-up on the button/SB with deep stacks (18k+). 42o is a marginal hand but in position advantage heads-up, and completing the small blind for 50 into 150 pot offers great implied odds to see a flop. The hand has some potential for straights, two-pair, or hitting hard. Opponent Minni defends wide from BB based on history of calling small raises and limps frequently. Folding trash OOP isn't optimal heads-up; better to play postflop or take it down sometimes. Will call and play cautiously postflop if needed.

Heads-up with pocket 7s vs a limp from the SB is a clear isolation/value spot. Elon’s limp represents a wide and often weak range (Ax, Kx, small pairs, suited connectors). I want to take the initiative preflop and build a pot with a hand that plays well postflop, but not shove — a moderate-sized raise both extracts value and prices out some speculative hands. I’ll raise to 400 total (4x BB). If called I’ll C-bet on flops that favor my range and proceed cautiously OOP on dangerous boards; if I face a large 3-bet/shove I’ll re-evaluate given this is a medium pocket pair.

Facing a raise to 400 after my SB limp in heads-up with deep stacks. 42o is a speculative hand with poor playability OOP postflop, low straight potential, and minimal showdown value. Minni's BB limp-raise range includes many strong hands and bluffs, but calling commits 300 into a 500 pot (pot odds ~3:1) with <30% equity against a typical raising range. Folding marginal hands preflop preserves stack for better positions and spots in this long tournament (hand 85/150). Previous limp was for position/playability, but raise warrants fold to avoid tough postflop OOP decisions with reverse position heads-up.

Heads-up on the button with Q♦ J♠ is a strong, playable hand — I have position and initiative. Raise rather than limp/call to pick up the blind, build a pot when I’m likely ahead of BB’s defense range, and preserve fold equity. I’ll raise to 300 (3× the big blind; total commitment = 300 including my SB 50). This size is standard at the table, keeps the pot manageable if called, and lets me play postflop in position or fold to a large 3-bet if Elon shows heavy aggression.

Heads-up vs Minni's button raise to 300 (3x BB). My 9dTd is a premium suited connector, excellent for multiway or heads-up pots with great playability for straights and flush draws. Pot odds are outstanding: calling 200 into 400 (3:1). Deep stacks allow for implied odds postflop. Defending wide OOP vs SB/Button is standard. Folding is too tight; raising OOP risks bloating pot with marginal equity realization. Plan to call and play postflop dynamically based on board texture.

Heads-up on the flop after Minni's button raise and my BB defend with 9dTd. She checks the paired 8c8sKh board, putting action on me OOP. My suited connectors have excellent playability preflop but flop pure air: no pair, no flush draw (no diamonds on board), no straight draw (gutshot at best but unlikely). Board is dry and paired, favoring checkers' ranges (overpairs, missed K-high, or monsters slowplaying). Betting air OOP risks getting raised or called wide; fold equity is low vs aggressive button who checks weak hands to pot control or induce. Checking realizes equity for free on turn, maintains pot control, and allows free cards to improve to straights/flushes. Deep stacks (18k effective) support seeing turn cheaply. Consistent with GTO heads-up BB vs BTN dynamics on paired boards.

I was the preflop aggressor (raised to 300) and have position. The flop (8c 8s Kh) is a paired, K‑high board that hits the BB’s calling range (many Kx and medium pocket pairs), but it also contains a lot of missed hands (A‑high, broadways) that can fold. My Q♦J♠ has little showdown value here and no real draw, so I want to use my initiative to take the pot down now with a standard half‑pot cbet. Betting 300 (into the 600 pot) applies pressure, folds out some worse high‑card hands and small pockets, and keeps the pot manageable if called. Plan: fold to a large check‑raise, and if called evaluate the turn — continue only with improvement or a very good turn scenario, otherwise control the pot or concede.

Heads-up on 8c8sKh flop after defending BB wide with suited connector 9dTd. Board is paired and dry, favoring Minni's BTN range which includes Kx, overpairs, sets, and some bluffs. My hand is complete air: no pair, no flush draw, no straight draw (T9 needs JQ or 67 specifically, unlikely). Facing 300 bet into 900 (3:1 odds), but calling OOP commits to tough multi-street decisions with zero equity realization on turns/rivers that don't dramatically improve it. Raising as a bluff is suboptimal deep (~18BB effective? Wait, stacks deep 180bb+, but principle holds): low fold equity vs aggressive Minni who bets wide; she continues with better. Folding realizes no equity loss, preserves stack for better spots. Consistent with exploitative GTO: fold marginal floats to aggression on unfavorable boards.

Heads-up on the button (SB) pre-flop with deep stacks (~180bb effective). My hand T5o is playable in this spot: decent high card (Ten), straight potential with wheel or broadway, and offsuit but position allows post-flop control. Opening range from BTN/SB heads-up is very wide (60%+ of hands), and folding the SB would leak chips over 150 hands. Minni frequently raises from BB but also defends wide and checks down many pots post-flop, giving me positional edge. History shows many small pots and steals work. Standard open to 3x BB (300 total) builds pot and applies pressure. Min-raise too small, call (limp) invites trouble OOP post-flop. Raise to steal blinds or play profitably.

Heads-up vs Elon’s open to 300 from the button. I’m in the big blind with 2h3s — a very weak, unsuited hand but with some backdoor potential. Calling the extra 200 (total commitment 300) gives me ~2:1 pot odds to see a flop and defend relatively wide heads-up. 3-betting OOP with garbage is unnecessary and folding surrenders too much equity. Plan: call to see the flop and proceed cautiously — continue only with a pair, two pair, strong draw, or very favorable textures; otherwise check-fold to large C-bets.

I paired my trey on the flop (board 8♠ 9♣ 3♣; my 2♥3♠). In heads-up this bottom/mid pair is often still best vs an opening range that checks the flop. Elon checked, showing weakness or pot-control — I should continue to extract value from worse hands and charge club/straight draws while keeping the pot manageable. I’ll make a small-to-medium value bet (about 1/2 pot) to accomplish that and plan to fold if faced with a large check-raise.

Facing a small 1/3 pot donk bet (300 into 900) on a flop of 8s9c3c from the BB after calling my BTN raise pre-flop. Deep effective stacks (~170bb). My Th5d is air: no pair, no flush draw, no straight draw, just ten-high with minimal backdoors. However, villain's range for BB defense and donk is wide: weak pairs, overcards, draws, bluffs. History shows Minni frequently small-bets thin value or for protection and checks down showdowns. In position with initiative from pre-flop raise, a cheap call floats effectively, keeps pot small, denies equity to villain's floats, and sets up turn steals (80% of villain's range will check turn). Folding leaks value over 150 hands as my range hits this board decently (e.g., AxTx, JTs, suited connectors) and villain bets wide. Raising as bluff too thin with no blockers and board favors calls; better post-flop dynamics. Call to realize positional advantage and exploit villain's passive tendencies.

Check. I have a marginal middle pair (3♠) on 8♠–9♣–3♣–A♠. I was the aggressor on the flop and got called, but the turn Ace strongly improves the preflop raiser’s continuing range (A‑x, A9/A8, some made hands and many turn equity combos). Betting here would be thin value and will get called or raised by hands that currently beat me; I don’t want to bloat the pot with a vulnerable pair. With deep stacks relative to the 1,200 pot it’s correct to control the size: check to either induce bluffs or take a cheap showdown. Plan: if Elon leads a small bet (roughly ≤ half‑pot) I’ll consider calling; fold to large turn raises and re-evaluate on the river if checked to.

Deep heads-up stacks (~170bb effective). Flop donk-call dynamic: villain's check on scary As turn (spades flush draw completes, ace high board, straight possibilities like 10-7) represents weakness, capping her range at medium strength or missed draws (e.g., flush/straight draws whiffed, weak pairs). My preflop raise + flop call range includes strong aces, sets, two-pair, flush draws, which hit this turn hard. Th5d is a profitable bluff: ten-high blocks broadway, backdoor equity, zero showdown value. History confirms Minni passive postflop (frequent checks/showdowns, folds to aggression). Betting ~50% pot denies equity to her floats/draws, extracts from trapped weak pairs, exploits positional leverage. Checking risks giving free card to draws/improvers or cheap river. Half-pot size balances value/bluff freq, induces folds from ~70% of her checking range while keeping pot manageable.

I have 2♥3♠ and paired my 3 on the flop (8♠ 9♣ 3♣). I bet the flop and Elon called. The turn A♠ hugely improves Elon’s continuing range (A‑x, A8/A9, some strong hands, and some floats/bluffs), but his lead is only 600 into a 1,200 pot (pot shown as 1,800 including his bet) — roughly a half‑pot bet. Calling 600 gives me good pot odds (I’m risking 600 to win ~2,400, needing ~20% equity) and keeps worse hands and bluffs in the pot. Folding is too timid with a made pair heads‑up; raising would be a pure bluff with poor blockers and a weak kicker and unnecessarily bloats the pot out of position. Plan: call the 600 to see the river and re‑evaluate based on Elon’s river action — fold to a large raise now, but call a small/medium river bet if the board/runout and sizing justify it.

I’m checking to take this to showdown. Holding 2♥3♠ on 8♠–9♣–3♣–A♠–Q♣ I only have a small pair with no club blocker and a weak kicker. Elon was the preflop raiser, called my flop bet and then led the turn for 600 — that line strongly favors Ax, A9/A8, 9/8/paired hands or club draws that the Q♣ or earlier action can now beat. Betting river for thin value is poor because most hands that would call a river bet beat my 3; bluffing is unattractive because I don’t block clubs, A’s or Q’s and a raise would be a pure gamble. With the pot at 2,400 and no bet to call, the lowest-variance + highest-EV line is to check and see the showdown rather than bloating the pot with a marginal hand.

Deep heads-up pot on river (2400), villain checks after calling my turn bet on an ace that completes spades flush draw and adds straight possibilities. My T5o is pure bluff: ten-high, no pair, no flush, no straight, but Th blocks key straight combos like T-J or Q-J for broadway. Villain's line (call pre, small flop donk with clubs draw heavy board, check-call turn on scary A) suggests capped range: medium pairs (88-99 whiffed set potential), weak aces, missed clubs draws, or stubborn floats. History confirms Minni's passivity (frequent check-downs, small bets, calls light but folds to river aggression often, e.g., hands 51,54,57,58,59,62). My aggressive line (PFR, call flop donk, bet turn) reps strong hands like A-high, sets, two-pair, nut flush draw. Bluffing exploits her checking weakness, as ~60%+ of her range (missed draws, weak pairs) folds to river bet. 2/3 pot size (1600) balances with value bets, maximizes fold equity, keeps SPR low post-flop dynamics in mind for tournament survival. Checking loses to her any showdown value and misses bluff EV over 150 hands.

Fold. Elon was the pre‑flop raiser, called my flop bet, then led the turn and now barrels the river for 1,600 into a 4,000 pot. Calling 1,600 to win a 5,600 pot after a call requires ~28.6% equity (1,600 / 5,600). My hand (2♥3♠) is a marginal single pair of threes with a weak kicker and no club blocker; Elon's line is strongly value‑heavy (A‑x, A3/A9/A8, 9x/8x, Qx, medium+ pocket pairs) and only a small portion of his continuing range are pure bluffs or worse pairs I beat. Raising would be a pure bluff with poor blockers and would bloat the pot out of position. Economic fold preserves ~20k stack chips for better spots rather than paying for a showdown when my equity is almost certainly below the required ~29%.

Heads-up on the button (small blind) with K♣J♥ — a strong, playable hand in position. Against a single big blind I should be opening a wide range to capitalize on position and fold equity. Elon has been defending and raising, but KJ is ahead of most calling ranges and plays well postflop. Standard, practical sizing here is ~3x the BB to apply pressure without bloating the pot with a marginal hand, so raise to 300 total. Plan: if Elon folds we scoop the 150 pot; if he calls we play postflop in position and c-bet favorable textures; if he 3-bets big I’ll re-evaluate (likely fold to a large 3-bet, call or 4-bet selectively vs small/committed 3-bets).

Heads-up in BB facing BTN raise to 300 (200 to call into ~600 pot). Hand is 3s9h: offsuit, gapped, low cards, poor playability OOP. Minni aggressive from button as per history, but this hand in bottom range for defending. Pot odds insufficient given postflop disadvantages. Fold to preserve stack for better spots over 150 hands.

Q2 offsuit is a very weak hand with poor connectivity and no high card strength. In the SB (button) heads-up vs BB, we act first pre-flop and will be OOP post-flop if called. Pot odds to call the additional 50 into 150 are decent (~25% equity needed), but our hand has low equity against Minni's wide BB defending range and terrible playability. Raising to steal is suboptimal as Q2o doesn't block strong hands and performs poorly when called or 3-bet. With deep stacks (191BB), folding preserves our stack for premium spots where we can apply pressure effectively. Opponent history shows Minni plays many hands but is capable of folding; no need to speculate with trash.

Heads-up on the button (small blind) with Q♥5♥ — a marginal but playable suited queen. Pot is 150 and it only costs 50 to see the flop, but in heads-up you should be opening a wide range to seize initiative and use post‑flop position. I want some fold equity and to discourage easy defenses, so I’ll open‑raise to a standard 3xBB (300 total). If called I’ll play postflop in position: continuation‑bet decent-sized when the flop connects with my range or gives me backdoor equity (hearts/queen/low coordinated cards), check and control the pot on obvious overcards or dry boards, and use positional advantage to realize equity or bluff selectively. If Elon 3‑bets a large size I’ll fold Q5s vs a polarised 3‑bet; I may re-evaluate vs a very small 3‑bet depending on sizing and his tendencies.

Heads-up play, 190BB deep stacks. K8o is a playable hand against a wide SB/Button open range (likely 60-80% of hands). Equity realization is good heads-up, even OOP, with king-high blocker and some straight potential. Pot odds are excellent (200 to call into 400 pot, ~3:1). Prefer calling over 3-betting as K8o performs better postflop multiway or seeing flops cheaply. Folding too tight in this spot. Will defend wide to maintain pressure and avoid exploitable BB defense.

Heads-up on a paired board 6h6sKs, with deep stacks (~190BB). Holding Kh8c for top pair top kicker (TPTK), blocking one K, which is strong against Button's wide raising range (likely 40-60% including many Ax, broadways, suited connectors). Board has some backdoor straight/flush potential and paired low cards, reducing opponent's set-mining equity. As OOP aggressor now, betting ~2/3 pot (400 into 600) balances value from worse Kx (KQ,KJ,KT,K9), Ax, bluffs/draws while protecting against live draws (spades, 75o/s, 45). Checking risks giving free cards to improvers or allowing cheap realization. Size extracts value without overbetting into possible 66/K6 monsters (low frequency). History shows Minni calls stations light, so expect calls/folds appropriately.

Call 400. Heads‑up on the button with Qh5h on K♠6♥6♠ — I have no pair but I do have two hearts in hand with a heart on the flop (a small backdoor/turn heart equity) and 6 clean outs to pair (3 queens + 3 fives). Elon’s 400 into ~1000 is a standard, fairly small c‑bet and his range is wide after calling preflop, so my hand has reasonable equity to continue. Pot odds for the call are about 3.5:1 (≈22% needed); my combined equity to improve by river vs a wide c‑betting range is in that neighborhood, and I have positional advantage to float and apply pressure on later streets. A raise here would be a thin bluff with poor blockers (he likely continues with Kx/trips), so I’ll call, see the turn, and either take the pot away if he shows weakness or fold to heavy action on later streets.

Turn card 8d pairs the board with my 8c, upgrading me from TPTK to top two pair (kings and eights) with Kh blocker. Extremely strong heads-up on a paired board where Minni's flop calling range (after Button minraise and my c-bet) includes weaker Kx (KQ/KJ/KT/K9/K7), Ax, 66/K6 (capped), draws (spade flush draws, 75/45 straight draws). Deep stacks (~190BB), pot 1400. As OOP aggressor, betting continues extracting value from worse made hands and charges draws/improvers (remaining spades, diamonds, 79o/s). Checking risks free cards to OESD (79) or runner-runner threats. Size ~2/3 pot (1000) balances value/thin value while denying equity. Minni's history shows calling stations tendencies (e.g., Hand 33,39,52), likely pays off with worse. Avoids overbetting into nuts like 66/68/K8s.

Heads‑up on the button with Q♥5♥ vs Elon. Board K♠ 6♥ 6♠ 8♦. Elon’s line (call preflop, small bet 400 on flop, continuing with 1000 on turn) looks value‑heavy (Kx, a six, or other made hands). I have only 6 clean outs to pair (3 queens + 3 fives) and no realistic backdoor flush (only one heart on board now) — equity to improve on the river ≈ 6/46 ≈ 13.0%. The pot is 2400 and it costs 1000 to call, so I need ≈29.4% equity to break even (1000/(2400+1000)). With my equity far below the required threshold and poor blockers, calling is -EV and a raise would be a thin, high‑variance bluff into a line that usually shows strength. Preserve the stack and fold.

Heads-up on the button (SB) with K8o, a solid hand in position with king blocker. Deep stacks (~20k effective). Standard to raise wide from SB vs BB to take initiative, build pot with good equity vs calling range. Minni defends wide from history. Raise to 300 (3x BB) for value and fold equity.

I’m heads-up in the big blind with 5♥2♣ — a very weak, unsuited hand with terrible playability out of position. Elon opened to 300; the pot is 400 and it costs me 200 to call (calling would make the pot 600, so I’d need ~33% equity to break even). 5-2 offsuit has far less equity vs a standard button opening range and is frequently dominated. A squeeze/raise OOP with this hand is high-variance and unnecessary with deep stacks early in the tournament. I’ll fold and wait for a better spot out of position.

Heads-up on the button (I’m SB) with 3♠10♥ — a weak hand but in heads-up play my opening range should be very wide. I want to seize the initiative, use position and fold equity to pick up the blinds or play a heads-up pot on my terms. A standard 2xBB open to 200 total accomplishes that while keeping the pot and SPR reasonable. If Elon 3-bets big I’ll give up (this hand has poor equity vs a 3-bet range); if he calls I’ll use position to c-bet or probe on favorable textures. Raise to 200.

Js2h is a trash hand with very poor equity and playability, especially out of position in the BB facing a button/SB raise. Minni has been aggressive preflop, raising frequently, but defending this wide isn't +EV given the domination risk and postflop OOP disadvantages. Pot odds are 3:1 (calling 100 into 300), requiring ~25% equity, but J2o achieves that only vs. an unrealistically wide range and plays poorly afterward. Standard fold to preserve stack for premium spots in this deep-stack tournament.

Heads-up pre-flop from SB/BTN with Kd5h. K5o is a playable hand in this spot with deep stacks (~200BB). Standard strategy is to raise a wide range to take initiative, steal the BB, or play post-flop with position advantage (act last post-flop). Minni has been aggressive in history but defends wide. Raising to 3x BB (300) is standard sizing. Folding too tight, limping passive.

Heads-up vs a standard 300 open from the button, A♣J♣ is a very strong hand. I want to take the initiative, get value from weaker Axs and broadways, and avoid playing a big pot out of position by just calling. A 3-bet to a standard size (~3x the open) gives fold equity, isolates the opener, and keeps SPR reasonable with our hand. If Elon 4-bets big I can fold and preserve deep stack, or call a smaller 4-bet and proceed postflop. Raise to 1000 to balance value and pressure.

Facing a 3-bet to 1000 after my button open-raise to 300 with Kd5h. K5o is a marginal opening hand heads-up but weak to a 3-bet range, which likely includes QQ+, AKo/s, AQs+, and some bluffs from Minni's aggressive history. Equity realization is poor post-flop due to domination risks (e.g., vs KQ, KJ), reverse implied odds, and offsuit nature. Calling 700 into 1300 pot (~35% equity needed) commits too much of a weak hand deepstacked (~195BB), leading to difficult spots OOP for opponent but still low EV. 4-betting as bluff possible with K blocker, but sizing to 1700 min or larger risks variance with fold equity uncertainty; Minni defends/4-bets back wide per history. Best is to fold and preserve stack for better positions, maintaining tight 3-bet defense while exploiting her aggression later.

Heads-up with deep stacks and I'm on the button (postflop position). 5♦4♥ is a marginal, non-premium hand but has some playability; I don't want to 3-bet or build a big pot preflop with it. Pot is 150 and I need to call 50 — I'm getting ~25% break-even equity to see the flop, so I'll call to realize equity and use position. Plan: fold to large preflop/turn aggression on dry boards, but continue/raise if I hit pair/two-pair/strong draw or can apply pressure postflop.

Preflop in BB vs SB limp/call. Pot 200, to call 0. Hand 32d suited is speculative but has some flop playability with flush and straight potential. Deep stacks (~190BB effective). Raising out of position with this weak hand is not ideal as it plays poorly postflop OOP vs a calling range. Checking realizes equity cheaply heads-up, allows realizing fold equity postflop if we hit, or folding to aggression if we miss badly. Defending BB wide vs SB limp is standard. Opponent has been aggressive historically but limped here, possibly weak. Goal to maximize stack long-term favors playing postflop over spew pre.

Flop Kh-7d-Jh completely misses my 3d2d. No pair, no straight draw (gaps too big), only backdoor diamond flush draw and runner-runner straight possibilities. OOP heads-up vs aggressive Minni who limped pre (possibly weak or trapping range). Pot 200, deep stacks. Bluff betting OOP with air is high variance and poor EV as villain calls wide or raises. Checking realizes minimal equity cheaply, allows pot control, and sets up fold to bet (likely value or semi-bluff from villain). Consistent with wide BB defense but folding equity postflop if we improve. Long-term stack maximization favors discipline over heroics.

Heads-up in position on a dry K♥ J♥ 7♦ flop. I hold 5♦4♥ — no pair, just a weak backdoor heart and little showdown value, but calling preflop leaves me a wide range to c-bet. Opponent checked, so I can apply pressure and try to fold out medium-strength hands (small pairs, Jx/Kx without hearts, and other floats). I’ll make a standard-sized continuation bet around 60% pot (120 chips) to maximize fold equity while keeping future decisions manageable with deep stacks. If he folds I take the pot; if he calls I’ll proceed cautiously on the turn and fold to significant aggression unless I improve to a pair or a strong draw.

Flop bet from Minni into 200 pot for 120 after limping preflop and my check. My 3d2d has zero equity: no pair, no straight draw (board Kh7dJh, gaps too large for runner-runner), only backdoor diamond flush draw. OOP heads-up with deep stacks (~190BB), calling commits chips with no showdown value or implied odds potential. Pot odds require ~27% equity (120 into 440), but range advantage favors villain's bet (wide value like Jx, Kx, sets, draws). Historical aggression from Minni suggests folding air is disciplined; spew calling risks stack variance without upside. Prior thoughts confirm miss; folding maximizes long-term EV.

I am in the small blind (button) with 8s4c, a very weak offsuit hand with poor connectivity, low cards, and no high-card strength. Facing the big blind, the pot is 150 after blinds, and I need to call 50 more. With deep stacks (~194BB effective), this hand has minimal playability even in position post-flop. Limping or raising would be exploitable and commit chips unprofitably. Standard play is to fold trash hands pre-flop to preserve stack and wait for better spots. Opponent tendencies from history show aggression, but equity is too low to justify any investment.

Heads-up on the button with Tc9d — a playable, positionally advantaged hand. In heads-up play you should be opening a very wide range from the button to seize initiative and apply pressure to a single opponent. A standard 3x BB sizing (300 total) gives good fold equity, keeps the pot manageable postflop, and builds a pot when my hand has decent playability (straight + T-high potential). Limping/calling 50 surrenders initiative and invites squeezes; min-raising to 200 is too small and invites lots of 3-bets. I’ll raise to 300 to open the action and play the rest of the hand in position; I’ll re-evaluate if Elon 3-bets or jams.

Heads-up vs aggressive Button raiser Minni who opens wide from SB/Button position based on history (frequent raises with marginal hands, calls postflop often but folds to aggression sometimes). A7s is a strong defending hand: suited Ace blocker to AA/AK/AQ, excellent playability, straight/flush potential. Deep stacks (19k+), so 3-betting for value/thin value and fold equity maximizes EV over calling (which gives her position postflop? Wait, no: postflop BB has position). Standard 3x her raise size to 900 total, putting 800 more, building pot OOP but with superior range advantage. Calling too passive, folding -EV.

Call 900. In heads-up play with T9o on the button I have position and decent postflop playability, so I’ll take the flop rather than 4-bet or lay down. Elon’s 3-bet to 900 is wide enough that calling for ~600 more (pot odds ~33%) plus strong implied odds is reasonable. This keeps the pot manageable with deep stacks and lets me continue aggressively on favorable, connected flops or control/weed out hands on dry boards.

Top pair aces with 7 kicker OOP on a draw-heavy flop (spade flush draw, gutshots like 45/56/9T). Ah blocker reduces opponent's nutted spade hands (no As), strong playability with heart flush draw blocker and straight potential. Minni's button calling range vs my 3bet includes Ax (weaker kickers), pocket pairs, suited connectors/draws. Betting for thin value (vs worse Ax/pairs), protection (vs draws), and denial of equity. Half-pot bet sizes well (builds pot without overbetting vs calling station tendencies from history). Check too passive OOP, risks giving free cards to draws or allowing bluffs/check-raises. Deep stacks allow multi-street play.

Heads-up on the button with Tc9d on A♠ 8♠ 3♣. I opened preflop and called Elon's 3-bet, so his range contains a mix of strong A-x, medium pairs and some bluffs. His flop bet of 900 into a 2700 pot is fairly small (I get ~25% pot odds to call). I have position, no pair and no meaningful draw, but deep stacks and positional advantage make a call the best line: it lets me realize equity, fold to significant turn aggression, and use position to bluff or value-bet later if a T/9 or a favorable runout appears. Raising here would be a thin bluff with little fold equity versus a 3-bettor and would unnecessarily inflate the pot. Plan: call the 900, re-evaluate on the turn and fold to heavy aggression if I don't improve or face strong action.

Strong hand OOP on turn: pair of Aces (top pair) with nut heart flush draw blocker (Ah) and backdoor straight potential. Board: 8s As 3c Kh features completed spade flush draw (still live), possible straight draws (e.g., 56 for gutshot), king pairs, but my range advantage from 3bet pre-flop and flop c-bet. Minni's calling range includes weaker Ax (KQ/KT/etc.), mid pairs, suited connectors/draws that hit flop somewhat. History shows her as aggressive opener but calls post-flop frequently, folding to heavy aggression occasionally. Betting continues thin value (vs worse Ax/pairs/draws), protection (vs pure spades/chasing draws), and pot building OOP. Check risks giving free river cards to draws or facing check-raise/value bets. Half-pot size (1800) balances value/extraction without overbetting deep stacks (~17k effective), invites calls from her worse holdings while denying equity. Consistent with flop sizing.

Elon has taken a very value-heavy line this hand (3‑bet pre, a flop bet and now a second barrel on the turn). My T♣9♦ missed the board completely: no pair, no spade, only 6 outs (three tens + three nines) ≈ 13% to improve on the river. Pot is 5400 and the call is 1800, so I need roughly 25% equity to make a pure call profitable. My raw equity (≈13%) is well below that threshold, so calling is a losing play unless I can credibly apply significant fold equity later. Raising to 3600 as a bluff is very thin here — Elon’s prior aggression and preflop 3‑bet make him unlikely to fold many of the hands I need him to (Ax, Kx, medium pairs). With deep stacks I should preserve chips and wait for a better spot in position rather than bluffing into a polarized, strong-looking range. Consistent with my earlier plan to fold to heavy turn aggression when I don’t improve, I’ll fold now.

Heads-up on the button/SB with deep stacks (~200BB effective). 63s is a playable suited connector with good implied odds, straight/flush potential, and post-flop playability despite being OOP initially (but we act last post-flop). Standard strategy is to raise wide from SB to steal blinds or get value/incentive to play post-flop. Minni defends wide based on history (frequent calls, loose play in pots). Raising to 3x (300) applies pressure, builds pot if called, and takes initiative. Folding too tight heads-up; limping passive and gives poor odds. Min-raise 200 too small; 300 standard.

I’m in the big blind with J♣4♦ facing a button open to 300. The pot is 400 and I must call 200 to see a flop, so I need roughly 33% equity (200 to win a 600 pot). In heads-up play the button’s opening range is very wide, so J4o has reasonable equity and can hit top pair, two-pair/straights occasionally and realize implied odds with deep stacks. 3-betting this hand OOP is thin and will get me into uncomfortable spots against a wide calling/4-betting range, so I’ll defend by calling and see a flop. Post-flop plan: proceed if I improve (top pair, two pair, strong draws); otherwise exercise pot control and fold to large, committed aggression — avoid bloating the pot out of position with a marginal holding.

Flop is 6♠ 9♠ 4♠ and I hold J♣4♦ — I have a pair of fours but the board is highly dangerous (three spades). Elon opened wide from the button so his range contains a lot of spades and hands that beat my pair (9x, 6x, spade combinations). I am out of position and the pot is already 600. Betting here is thin: most of the hands that will call or raise me are ahead (made spade flushes, top pairs), and leading out will often get me into an expensive mistake. I prefer pot control and information — check and let Elon either c‑bet a wide range (call small bets and re-evaluate on the turn) or check back (take a free turn card and potentially stab blanks). Plan: check now; call modest bets to realize equity and fold to large, committed aggression (raises/shoves) since my pair is vulnerable.

Postflop as preflop raiser on a highly coordinated, monotone spade flop (6s9s4s). I have bottom pair (6c) with a terrible kicker (3c), suited clubs for some backdoor potential, but extremely vulnerable to any spade (flush), 75/85 (straights), overpairs, sets, or two overs. Minni's check is weak but her history shows wide defending and calling stations tendencies in multiway but heads-up she's folded to aggression often (e.g., hands 55,57,58,79). Pot 600 deep (~30BB), standard c-bet sizing ~2/3 pot (400) for thin value/protection vs her draw-heavy calling range (spade draws, gutshots like 75/85/107). Takes initiative, denies equity to draws, builds pot OOP if she folds. Checking risks giving free card to superior draws; if she bets turn/river, can fold. Maximizes EV by balancing bluffs/value in polarized range.

I have J♣4♦ with a pair of fours on 6♠9♠4♠. The board is very spade‑heavy and my pair is vulnerable, but Elon’s sizing is modest (400 into a 1000 pot). Calling 400 gives me ~22% pot odds to see the turn and, heads‑up versus a wide c‑bet range, I have enough equity and some backdoor/improvement outs to continue. I don’t want to bloat the pot out of position with a marginal hand, so I’ll call and re‑evaluate on the turn. If Elon raises heavily on the flop (min‑raise to 800 or bigger) I’ll fold — my hand can’t stand up to committed aggression here. If he checks the turn or gives a single small barrel I can proceed; I’ll only continue to larger bets if I improve to top pair or better.

Board: 6♠ 9♠ 4♠ 8♠. I hold J♣4♦ — second pair (fours) with a weak kicker on an extremely spade‑heavy board. Elon c‑bet the flop and then checked the turn: that check can be weakness, but it can also be a slow‑play of a made spade flush or a strong 9x/6x. Betting out OOP here would bloat the pot and mostly get called or raised by hands that beat me (any spade, top pair, sets), while only folding the marginal hands I currently beat. With deep stacks and a vulnerable holding my priority is pot control and extracting information. I’ll check and see the river; plan to fold to large, committed aggression on the river unless I improve, and consider a small call to a probe bet but avoid building a big pot out of position with just second pair.

Turn card 8s completes numerous flushes (any spade) and straights (75 for straight flush potential, 57, 107 etc.), making the board terrifyingly draw-heavy now with four spades. I have bottom pair (6s) with a terrible kicker (3c), no real draw beyond backdoor clubs, and minimal showdown value against her likely range after calling flop c-bet on three-spade board (spades, sets, two-pair+). Her double check suggests possible weakness (no flush or monster trap), but betting here polarizes my range poorly: thin value unlikely as she beats me often, bluff equity low since she'll hero-call with any piece/draw/flush. Pot control essential with deep stacks (~15BB pot); checking realizes equity OOP, avoids bloating pot with marginal hand, induces bluffs/value bets from weaker hands on river, and allows cheap showdown or counter if she bets. History shows frequent check-downs heads-up; preserves stack for better spots. Barreling -EV on this card.

The river paired the board (8h), so I improved from a vulnerable single 4 to two pair (8s8h + 4d4s). That’s a meaningful upgrade but the board is still extremely dangerous: there were four spades on the turn so any spade in Elon’s range is a made flush, and single-card 9x or 6x (or an 8) also beat my two pair. Elon’s line (open pre, c‑bet flop, then check turn) is ambiguous — it can be either a missed c‑bet or a slow‑played flush — so betting here would mainly get called by hands that beat me and fold the marginal hands I already crush. With the pot at 1,400 and deep stacks, I want pot control and to avoid bloating the pot out of position with a hand that’s still vulnerable. I’ll check down to take showdown; if Elon bets a small river probe I can call, but I won’t be building the pot by barreling as thin value.

River 8h pairs the 8s on a scary four-spade board (6s9s4s8s), completing flushes and multiple straights while pairing the board. IP with bottom pair (6c) and trash kicker (3c), no kicker issues beat any plausible 6x or better pairs/draws in her range. Her OOP check-call flop (spade-heavy, likely spades/pairs/gutshots), check turn (after scary 8s), check river signals capped range: missed draws (e.g., 75, 107, low spades), weak pairs (5x,77), or traps (sets, flushes unlikely on check-river). My range IP (preflop raiser, flop bettor, turn checker) includes value (sets, two-pair+, flush) and bluffs (missed draws), but checking turn caps me somewhat toward medium strength. Betting thin value -EV (beats only air/worst pairs, she calls better); bluff sizing (e.g., 1000 into 1400 ~70% pot) has fold equity vs weak holdings but poor after turn check-back, risks calls from 8x/top pair/showdown value. History of check-downs heads-up (many recent hands go check-check river). Optimal: check back IP, realize equity OOP-style caution, win vs missed draws/weak aces unbet, pot control deep (~14BB pot, 140BB stacks), preserve for better spots in late stages. No bet maximizes EV long-term.

Heads-up on the button (I’m the SB) with A♥J♥ — a very strong, high-equity hand in position. Stacks are deep (~180bb me, ~218bb opponent) so I can apply post-flop pressure and realize equity. Versus a single big blind there’s a very wide defending range, so I want initiative and fold equity to take down the pot preflop or play position on favorable flops. A 3x BB opening (to 300 total) is a standard, balanced size: big enough to charge speculative hands and get value from worse A/x and broadway hands, but not so large that I overcommit. If Elon 3-bets I’ll re-evaluate based on sizing (call or fold vs large 3-bets, continue vs smaller ones); if called I’ll c-bet favorable flops and use position to seize control. I raise to 300 to build the pot and maximize expected value with this strong hand.

6s2d is one of the worst possible hands in poker: uncoordinated, low cards, offsuit, no straight or flush potential. Facing a standard button/SB raise from Minni, who raises frequently from late position based on history. My BB defend range doesn't include this trash. Pot odds are about 3:1, but my equity against her wide raising range is abysmal (<20%), and I'd be out of position postflop with no playability. Folding preserves my deep stack (217bb) for better spots in this long tournament.

Heads-up on the button with Qh9s pre-flop. Q9o is a strong enough hand in this spot: queen-high with decent playability and blockers. Effective stacks are deep (~200BB), pot is 150 with 50 to call as SB. Folding is too tight heads-up from BTN/SB. Limping is passive and allows Minni to check-raise or play aggressively post-flop. Standard play is to raise for value/thin value and to steal blinds. Minni has defended wide in recent hands, but we want initiative. Raising to 3x BB (300 total) is a standard size: builds the pot without overcommitting. If called, we have position and can continue aggression on favorable boards.

I call (total 300). Facing Elon's open to 300 heads-up with A♠3♦, an ace is too strong to fold from the big blind but A3o is a marginal hand to 3-bet out of position. The pot is 400 and I need to call 200 more (≈2:1 pot odds) to see a flop — good enough to defend and keep the pot manageable. A min‑reraise to 500 gives little fold equity and a larger 3‑bet would be too loose with a weak kicker against his wide but often strong button range. Plan: see the flop, press when I pair the Ace or hit strong draws, and play cautiously OOP on coordinated or high boards.

Check. Heads‑up versus an open from the button, Elon's range is wide (Jx, Ax, small pairs, broadways, suited connectors). My A♠3♦ missed the flop but is far from worthless: it gives me the Ace blocker and a backdoor wheel (A‑2‑3‑4 needs a 5), so I have reasonable showdown equity and some turn equity. With deep stacks and out of position I want pot control — betting OOP with a weak Ace invites bigger pots when I’m often behind and gains little fold equity against his likely calling hands. Checking lets me call a standard c‑bet, fold to heavy aggression, or realize equity and potentially improve on a 5 or an Ace. Plan: call small–medium flop bets (roughly ≤½ pot) to see a turn, fold to large overbets unless I improve, and consider a delayed bluff or thin value if I pair the Ace or hit the wheel.

Flop is Jh 4h 2c, a relatively dry board with one strong card (J) and low disconnected cards, favoring my preflop raising range which includes broadway hands like Qx and overpairs. Minni checked, representing weakness or a marginal hand without initiative. Qh9s has queen-high blockers to top pair and decent playability, plus backdoor heart draw with two hearts on board. Position allows me to c-bet polarized: value with top pair+, bluffs with air/high cards. Standard c-bet size of ~2/3 pot (400 into 600) builds pot, denies equity to her calling range (pocket pairs below J, weak Ax), and takes advantage of fold equity heads-up. Minni defends wide preflop but folds to continued aggression on uncoordinated boards per history (e.g., Hand 55, 57). Checking back risks giving free cards to draws or letting her realize equity. Betting maximizes EV by folding out better-than-Q hands without showdown value.

Elon c‑bets 400 into a 1000 pot (~40% pot). I hold A♠3♦ — missed the flop but I have the Ace blocker and a four‑out wheel draw (any 5 completes A‑2‑3‑4‑5). Calling 400 to win ~1400 gives me ~28.6% break‑even equity. Versus Elon's wide button range (Jx, some Ax, broadways, small pairs and floats) I have reasonable showdown equity and good implied odds with deep stacks. I prefer pot control OOP: call small/medium c‑bets to see a turn, fold to large overbets unless I improve, and value/pressure if I pair the Ace or make the straight. Action: call 400 to see the turn and re‑evaluate.

I'm out of position with A♠3♦ on 4♥–2♣–J♥–K♠. I called the flop to control the pot and see the turn; the King is a card that typically helps Elon's button raising range (Kx, Jx, some Ax and broadways). I only have Ace‑high with a 4‑out wheel draw (a 5 on the river) and an Ace blocker that slightly reduces his Ax combos — not enough to make an OOP bet or a bluff profitable here. Betting would bloat the pot with little fold equity and poor value; checking keeps the pot at 1400, lets me realize equity, and lets Elon show aggression first on the river so I can make a cleaner decision. Plan: check and fold to large river overbets, call small river bets or value bet/raise if I actually hit the 5 or pair the Ace.

Heads-up on the button with Qh9s on a 4h-2c-Jh-Ks turn board. Preflop raise and flop c-bet continuation called by Minni in BB, who now checks again, indicating weakness or a marginal trap hand unlikely given her history of calling stations but folding to aggression on scary boards (e.g., Hands 55, 77, 87). The turn K is a broadway card that hits villain's calling range (KJ, KT, AK) but also my blocker-heavy Q-high, improving relative showdown value against bluffs/draws/low pairs. Board has straight potential (QT) and heart flush draw, but Qh blocks top-end straights. Deep stacks (~120BB effective), pot 1400; checking back risks giving free river cards to draws or letting her realize equity cheaply, while position allows control. Standard play is to bet ~2/3 pot (900) as a polarized semi-bluff: denies equity to her float range (Ax, low pairs, missed draws), extracts value from weaker hands like 88-TT or stubborn Ax, high fold equity per history. Maximizes EV long-term by leveraging initiative and range advantage.

I checked the turn with A♠3♦ to control the pot and see Elon's reaction. He now bets 900 into a 1400 pot (pot = 2300). Calling 900 to win 3200 requires ~28.1% equity. I only have ~7 clean outs on the river (3 Aces + 4 fives) ≈ 15.2% to improve, and pairing an Ace can still lose to Ax with a better kicker. Elon's line (open pre, flop c-bet, turn bet) polarizes toward Kx/Jx and value hands — not a spot where I have sufficient equity or fold equity to profitably raise OOP (min-raise to 1800 would bloat the pot with little chance to fold him). With deep stacks and a small pot relative to stacks, preserving chips and waiting for a better spot is the right play. Fold.

Heads-up on the button (small blind) I should open-raise very wide to take the initiative. 9c3d is a weak hand, but in heads-up it's within my standard opening range. The table typically opens to ~3x the BB; I’ll raise to 300 to apply pressure and get to see flop as the aggressor. Plan: fold to a large 3-bet, and if called use position to c-bet favorable dry flops and pot-control on dangerous boards.

Heads-up pre-flop in the big blind facing a button min-raise to 300 (200 to call into 400 pot). My hand 3c7s is extremely weak: offsuit, disconnected, low cards with poor playability. Even in heads-up, where BB defending ranges widen, 37o is in the bottom of the range and has very low equity against Minni's button opening range, which includes many strong hands and bluffs but still crushes this.
